review_body: This oil is the best for Asian cooking - pan fried noodles are downright heavenly cooked in this. It's also good for other types of food where the flavor of the oil is likely to be detectable and the kitchen smalls wonderful when I cook with it. Made from roasted peanuts, it is more akin to roasted sesame oil in flavor rather than the standard peanut oil you see in markets that is fairly flavorless. My local market used to carry this but stopped stocking it a couple of years ago and the closest product I'd found was one made by Hain that was not nearly as rich or flavorful. I was pleased to find it at such a good price, my market was charging close to $10 per bottle and the last time I ordered this from Amazon I had to buy a case of 6 bottles to get a good price and free shipping. Not a problem as I was able to share some with friends and use it all before it went rancid but it's nice to know that now I can just order a couple of bottles at a time if I wish.

Fry the onions by themselves to reduce the bite (white onion(s)).
Add the bacon (that has been set aside) half way through the cooking process, or it will be too salty (if added to soon).
Add the cheese half way through the cooking process also.
A little note..when adding the cheese..add a ton of it and just think.." I am making "cheese" "potato" soup.
I add a little 2% milk.. a half to a full cup, just to stretch it out (so I have left overs).
It will still be very "thick"
I make a double batch...BEST EVER !!

product_title: Amazing Grass Green Superfood
review_headline: Try it! You won't be disappointed!
review_body:
I'm very skeptical when it comes to health food supplements. I'm a full time college student, wife, and mother to a toddler. I never seem to have enough energy and caffeine doesn't effect me much. So I decided to try this, hoping it would give me lasting, clean energy.

I put a scoop into any kind of juice and mix it in my Magic Bullet. It does not blend well by hand. It tastes like grass and has a grainy texture. It isn't delicious but it is palatable and it grows on you.

I didn't really notice much of a difference after drinking this, but I kept drinking it anyway, 1 scoop every morning. When the container was gone, I decided not to order more. Within a day of not taking it, I started having intense cravings for vegetables. Nothing I ate seemed to satisfy me. One day I made a huge salad and it was the best thing I've ever eaten! My body was telling me something! I felt tired and mentally foggy. Overall, I simply didn't feel as healthy. There was a definite difference.

Now I'm on my 4th container and I fully intend on continuing to take this supplement. The effects may not be instantaneous for me, but it provides a sustained level of health and energy, which I love!

review_body: This is my first time trying matcha powder, but I did quite a bit of research before settling on this brand (didn't want a poor quality powder or have to worry about radiation contamination from Japanese powders).  The product arrived quickly and on time (2-day shipping with Prime), and was safely packaged. So far I've tried it in a latte (almond milk and some sugar), and also in a smoothie (bananas, almond milk, sugar, +/- ice).  The powder blends much better in the smoothie (but we did recently buy a small whisk for this purpose).  The color of the powder is bright (not muddy or dull) and it's very fine (not gritty).  When mixed/ blended the taste is pretty smooth (no real bitterness).  When I drink the smoothies in the morning, I do feel more energized.  I'm very pleased with the purchase and will definitely buy it again!

product_title: Brightea® China Famous Monkey Picked Vintage Oolong Tea (Wulong / Wu Long) - Chinese Fujian Anxi High Mountain Tie Guan Yin Iron Mercy Goddess (Iron Buddha / Tieguanyin / Ti Kuan Yin / Tikuanyin) Loose Leaf 4.1 Ounce Tin (Supreme Grade AAAAA)
review_headline: Lovely, top quality oolong.  Wonderful sipping experience!
review_body:
Oolong teas are my favorite teas. This Iron Buddha from Brightea is a top quality--unforgettable--oolong. I have some friends at work with friends that visit from Taiwan bearing gifts of fabulous teas. So I have been blessed with the fortune of superior-tasting oolong experiences. This offering from Brightea is right up there with the best.

If you are considering this tea, I suggest that you study the two columns of pictures that go along with this tea on this product page. The pictures say quite a lot. Notice the rolled leaves in the top, right. Now look down to the next and bottom right pictures and see the wet unrolled leaves. See how perfect they are, how large and not torn? Top, left: Notice the tin and then see the small individual vacuum-packed bags that are tightly tucked into the tin. There are 14 packets in the very sturdy, well-constructed tin. So your tea leaves will always be fresh when you go to use them. Each of the 14 packets makes many, many cups of tea.

There are many ways to appreciate oolong--and I try to make use of several techniques to get all I can from what is often a very expensive tea. With this tea I heated my fresh water to 195 degrees Fahrenheit, then poured it over the rolled tea leaves. (I know, this varies from the Brightea directions.) I left it to brew for only two minutes. It produces a lovely golden color, much deeper that what it appears to be in the pictures. The initial flavor--that you can only appreciate in the first few sips when brewed this way--is a slight nuance of apricots and their pits. It is a fleeting taste and one needs to be centered on the tea experience in order to appreciate it.

Subsequent sips of that first cup--and many subsequent brews--shows a bit of sweetness, and of course dried grassy green-ness. It is a lovely, full-flavored oolong.

I have taken up the practice of many of my oriental friends at work: I leave my oolong tea leaves in my cup a good part of the day and keep adding hot water to the leaves. With this wonderful tea, I can go all day on the same leaves. Be aware that if you taste bitterness in the brew, you have let the water set too long in the leaves. If you don't like the sensation of bitterness, drain that bitter brew and add fresh hot water and you are good to go again.

**I received a free sample product of this tea from Brightea.** I am so glad they brought my attention to this tea and their brand. I buy tea from several well-known tea companies, but I am always on the look out for superior, out-of-the-norm, and unusual teas--they are not easy to find. Brightea is a keeper!a good part of the day and keep adding hot water to the leaves. With this wonderful tea, I can go all day on the same leaves. Be aware that if you taste bitterness in the brew, you have let the water set too long in the leaves. If you don't like the sensation of bitterness, drain that bitter brew and add fresh hot water and you are good to go again.

I buy tea from several well-known tea companies, but I am always on the look out for superior, out-of-the-norm, and unusual teas--they are not easy to find. Brightea is a keeper!

product_title: Pace Ready Meal, 9 Ounce (Pack of 6)
review_headline: Easy & quick prep, pouch serves as a bowl, so no clean up- the taste is good for a precooked, shelf stable food product
review_body:
This review is based on one pouch of Pace Ready Meals, Fiesta Chicken and Rice

This is a convenient, pre-cooked food product that cooks is in the bag and that can be served in the bag. It doesn't compare to fresh home-cooked meals because of the class of food that it is in, so I am basing this review off a comparison of similar products.

I like it MUCH, MUCH better than Hormel Compleats. It is more filling and a bigger portion size than Campbell's or Chef Boyardee to go microwave ups. It's also tastier.

It is a bit sour; it has tomatillos and vinegar in it, and I can taste both, but it's really very tasty. It's like a stew, so yes, the rice is a bit mushy. I spot some corn, red peppers, and small tiny bell peppers in the mix. There's not a lot of meat in it, but I don't miss it really, but for the price, I'd expect a bit more. What little bit there is dark meat, but it is identifiable and doesn't look preformed or overly processed. It's about like what you'd see in Campbell's Chunky soups. The taste reminds me of salsa verde.

Preparation is easy- just put the whole bag in the microwave for 1 minute, on high, then let it sit for one minute. You do not have to tear the bag or anything. After a minute, I found the bag still a bit hot to tear, so I cut the top off at the line. It was tearing easily though, and straight. After the top is off, it makes a nice little bowl.
I do love this packaging. It is BPA free and VERY convenient- not only does it have a quick prep time, but you don't need a bowl, and since I used a plastic fork, there was nothing to clean up. (It is thick enough to eat with a fork, or you could use a spoon.)

I would buy this for my husband to take to work. I usually pack his lunch but there are times he forgets to take it. He works at a very remote location, so I like for him to have something there 'just in case.' This is perfect- it has a nice long shelf life- the pouch I received is good for 10 months, and it's filling enough to get him through the dayget him through the day

product_title: Sparkling ICE
review_headline: Does Not Tell How Much Sucralose Per Bottle - Tastes Great If You Get Past Fake Sugar Concerns
review_body:
I am conflicted on Sparkling ICE drinks. On the plus side, I am trying to find a good-tasting flavored drink choices, with no or very low carbs for a son who has Type 1 diabetes. Sparkling ICE fits this to a T. It tastes great and has 0 carbs and 0 calories. Everybody in the family loves how it tastes. At the end of the review, I'll give the full ingredient list for the two flavors we’ve tried. Each flavor has some slightly differing ingredients, but both have a small amount of juice concentrate.

On the minus side, Sparkling ICE uses the artificial sweetener sucralose (brand name Splenda). I have stayed away from aspartame and saccharine, and I thought sucralose was a step in the right direction, or should I say “not as bad”. This is a divisive topic, I know, and the trouble is, you can find too much information.

In short, sucralose is chlorinated sugar. Chlorinated organic compounds are mostly excreted by the body and partially accumulated in the body. The FDA says 11-27% of sucralose is absorbed. Apparently, the Japanese Food Sanitation Council says up to 40% is absorbed. Neither the bottle nor the Talking Rain website state how many mg of sucralose are in each bottle of Sparkling ICE. The FDA’s estimated daily intake (EDI) is 1.1 mg/kg/day, and the FDA says it is totally safe at that level. Studies sponsored by the Sugar Association state a lower number.

Regarding its plastic bottles, Talking Rain’s website FAQ notes that “We mutually appreciate concern for BPA and phthalate free products. You can rest assured that our 1 PETE plastic bottles are safe. PETE does not contain BPA or phthalates.”

Sparkling ICE would get 5 stars from me based on taste and antioxidants, and its lovely cooling sparkling. But I’ve reduced it to 2 stars because I’m not given enough information to determine how much sucralose I’m ingesting. And, I must admit, I am still not comfortable about the fake sugar and having to tell people, “It’s OK if you only drink one a day. Really!”

review_headline: The difference in Tahitian and Madagascar Vanilla flavors.
review_body: I am a novice in regards to the use of Vanilla beans.  Previously, I ordered the Madagasgar beans and wanted to see the difference in those and Tahitian.  I wanted to make my own Vanilla extract and have now made it with the Madagascar beans and the Tahitian beans.  The Tahitian  Vanilla extract is not as dark, and not as intense. It still has a wonderful vanilla scent, and is more floral and less smoky.  If you need  an extract with less brown color and less earthiness in  flavor, use the Tahitian. If you want the usual and more common vanilla extract flavor, order the Madagascar variety.  Both are good, but the Tahitian less flavorful in my opinion.

product_title: Bigelow Green Tea Chai 20-Count Boxes (Pack of 6)
review_body:
This is not your everyday tea. That said, in the winter months I brew a 2 cup pot every morning and drink it straight, no milk.

This tastes like nothing else. Ok, that's not true. If anything, it's reminiscent of Diet Dr. Pepper. I know how crazy that sounds, but to my tastebuds, it's true. And so, for any of you looking to kick the diet soda habit, this is a good step. It makes great iced tea.

I've tried all sorts of green chai blends. I like most of them, but this is my go-to for hot tea. When I make iced tea, I blend this with a couple other teas.

This tea has become very hard to find in stores. I hope Bigelow keeps making it because I'd be lost without this green tea chai.

product_title: Beech-Nut Just Organic Stage 2 Purees - Just Apple, Raspberry & Avocado - 4.25 Oz - 10 pk
review_body: I received a sample of Beech Nut Organics just apples, raspberry and avocado courtesy of Beech Nut Organics and bzzAgent. At first, I wasn't sure what my kiddos would think of this. With twins, I had been making my own baby food due to the cost of purchasing and because I don't like all the additives. Additionally, they had never had raspberries before. I was happy to sample this product, although hesitant. I love that Beech Nut Organics is simple and doesn't have added ingredients-JUST apples, raspberry and avocado! My twins loved it! They liked it so much I bought more and froze it in little drops so they can eat it as finger food now that they're old enough to feed themselves. In addition to being nutritious and tasty, it's so much easier to travel with premade baby food instead of the homemade kind. It's also a flavor combination that is never think to make at home, so they loved that it's something different. I definitely recommend this product!

review_body:
I drink this because I enjoy the taste, however, it hasn't done anything for my weight. The taste is a light blueberry flavor, and green tea naturally has a little caffeine, 35 mg. I drink tea a lot, and Yogi teas are consistently good. I buy mine usually at the grocery store or at the Swanson vitamins website for about $3 per (16 tea bag) box.

Supplement Facts
Serving Size 1 tea bag (Makes 8 fl oz)

Ginseng and Eleuthero Root Extracts
(Asian Ginseng, American Ginseng and Eleuthero)40 mg
Garcinia Cambogia Fruit Extract 20 mg
Proprietary Blend of Herbs: 1,680 mg
Organic Green Tea Leaf
Organic Bilberry Leaf
Organic Hibiscus Flower
Green Tea Leaf Extract
Stevia Leaf
Organic Amla Fruit (Amalaki)
Organic Belleric Myrobalan Fruit (Bibhitaki)
Organic Chebulic Myrobalan Fruit (Haritaki)

Other Ingredients: Natural Orange Flavor, Natural Blueberry Flavor, Natural Licorice Flavor.

Each tea bag contains approximately 35 mg of caffeine, as compared to approximately 90 mg in 8 oz of coffee.

Bring water just to boiling and steep 3 minutes. Drink throughout the day. For a stronger tea, use 2 tea bags.

product_title: Madhava Organic Agave Sweetened Coffee Syrup
review_headline: Great healthier syrup! Here's a tip to help bring out the caramel flavor with this....
review_body:
I'm a huge fan of salted caramel anything and LOVE it as a coffee flavor but the choices up until now were not that healthy. I was really excited so see this available and made with Agave instead of sugar! This is a much healthier choice and the flavor is good.
My only "complaint" is that the flavor is not as intense as the other, more expensive brands I've been buying but the trade off I guess is the Agave. I don't know if a more intense flavor can be achieved without the chemicals, but if it's possible and Madhava is listening to customer feedback, that's my input :)
One suggestion I have and something I do is add a touch of salt to my coffee when I use this syrup. Sounds weird, but it really works to "boost" the flavor and helps to bring out the caramel essence :) I do mean a TOUCH! I use sea salt which is more intense anyway so just a small pinch is all that's needed.
This is great too over ice cream or sorbet that I make in the VitaMix! You can also add it to bubble water made with the SodaStream machine.
Hope this was helpful info for all my fellow salted caramel lovers out there!
Enjoy!!!

product_title: Shiraz Fontana Wine Making Kit, 30 bottles (750ml/each)
review_body: OK Review update. This batch of Shiraz was started on 10/7/15. I went right by the instructions except for the addition of 1 tsp of L D Carlson yeast nutrient per gallon. Starting S G was 1.084. On 10/12/15 the S G was down to 0.996 so I racked it into the secondary fermenter. I follow an old fashion method from here. Every day after the first rack I rack it back to the primary and check the SG. Todays S G was 0.994 so it is still working. Then I clean out the carboy secondary and rack the wine back into it, This process has two benefits, first it removes a lot of the fines that need to be out and second it removes a lot of the CO2. This makes it easier to stabilize and clarify. I do this daily until I get 3 days without any change of the S G. At that point it is ready for the final steps before bottling. Oh what I use for a S G test goes into a glass for sipping. If it tastes like vinegar you can stop and bottle it for use as wine vinegar because it will never be fit for drinking.inted with to small of a font making it hard for old folks to read. Second they gave me some nice wine labels for my bottles but no corks or sleeves,But all in all for a budget priced kit is is pretty good and the Shiraz is a smooth wine that will go well with any meal.

Tequila Sunrise has the word sunrise in it, so I figured it was acceptable to mix into a breakfast cocktail. So, I added vodka to my orange juice with a squeeze of tequila sunrise ADD-itude and it was awesome! The Strawberry Hibiscus was excellent in Vodka.

ADD-itude is a really cool product line and I cannot wait to try other flavors! I think these will be great for parties because people can be their own bartenders and mix up whatever drink they like.

I also really like the fact that using ADD-itude replaces buying a bunch of different flavored vodka that I may not like anyway. That just leaves more room for my bourbon collection.

The only way to eat these is to rinse them off and add agave syrup or other sweetener to remove the bitter taste of the monk fruit.  I am stuck with an inedible product now. product_title: Milkmakers Oatmeal Chocolate Chip Lactation Cookies
review_body: As a first time mother, I was desperate to feed my baby who is fed just expressed breast milk. And I'm tired of pumping round the clock, especially since doing it for the last four months. So to increase my production, I bought a bag of these cookies from my lactation consultant. I ate a cookie in the morning and I pumped just two hours later and pumped double the amount that I normally pumped. I still eat two cookies a day and only pump three times a day. Plus, it satisfies my sugar cravings, what else could you ask for? Try it, you won't be disappointed.

product_title: Italian Grana Padano Cheese, 2.5 Pound
review_body:
This is incredibly good cheese. It's tiresome to grate so I grate it in my food processor. I often combine it with grated mozzarella or parmesan. Today I combined it with Panko bread crumbs, added olive oil to make a paste, covered tilapia fillets with that, and baked at 400º for about 12 minutes. I topped it off with a sauce of mayo, whisked with lemon juice then capers stirred in. Serve it with a veggie and salad for a delicious, easy meal. Enjoy!
DrJimM -

review_body:
This is a mix of half caramel corn, and half (what appears to be) caramel corn coated in cinnamon. So much cinnamon that those kernels may actually have a slightly more chewy texture. But it doesn't come off on your hand.

The caramel half is quality stuff, as Gary Poppins usually is -- not your typical "Cracker Jack". The cinnamon was occasionally a touch overpowering, but we still enjoyed this mix. The kernels aren't hard, and there were no unpopped ones in the bottom of the bag. And it's low in fat.

If you are a serious, hardcore fan of cinnamon, you may give this five stars!

Bulk Sampler of 40 Assorted Tea Bags (40 Count)
review_body:
Since I'm new to the world of tea, a friend of mine recommended that I get a sampler pack to try out the different kinds of teas out there. After looking around, I came across this pack.

I did get a few duplicates, but the majority are individual tea flavors. So individually, I got 19 black, 5 green, 5 ceylon, 4 herbal, 2 white, 1 earl grey, and 1 oolong. No idea why there's so many black teas. Would be nice to have a little more of the others. Since these are random though, I would imagine the amount of different types you get could vary.

Not going to lie, but the other reviews were sort of off putting when it states that they're giving unbiased and honest answers for free samples? Makes the reviews sound weird and not genuine : ( Decided to go ahead and purchase anyways because it was cheap and what I wanted.

review_body:
I was recently reading about natural healing and everywhere there seemed to be a mention of Chia seed. I decided to research it further and was surprised to find that there are so many health benefits of this nutritional powerhouse. In brief, they contain omega-3 fatty acids, carbohydrates, protein, fiber, antioxidants, and calcium.
One ounce (about 2 tablespoons) contains 139 calories, 4 grams of protein, 9 grams fat, 12 grams carbohydrates and 11 grams of fiber, plus vitamins and minerals.
The two pound bag is at a great price and the subscribe and save option helps you save even more money.

product_title: RetailSource Bone Suckin Habanero Sauce, 5 oz.
review_body:
I've been addicted to this hot sauce for the past 10 years...I put it on everything. To the point that my wife was really offended when we first got married...she thought I didn't like her food. But eventually she realized it's my love for this sauce and not my dislike of her food that drives me to put it on everything. I've been tempted to buy it by the case for shipping discounts.

It's a great balance of flavor and sweetness. And it's very hot. It's almost too hot for me, but I love the flavor so much, I put up with the heat. It may not be for everyone, but I know I'll cry the day they stop selling this sauce...hopefully that will never happen.

product_title: Brooklyn Bean Roastery Single-Cup Hot Cocoa for Keurig K-Cup Brewers
review_headline: Subtle but Fun Flavors For the Cocoa Lover
review_body:
I feel like a kid again. I don't often drink hot cocoa, my kids do but I was happy to try these out. Each cup has a subtle flavor underneath the rich cocoa base.

I'll start by saying that we have a child with a peanut allergy in the house and one of these packs is peanut butter cup. So to be safe, one of two children has refrained from drinking any of these. My daughter and I have been using them though and enjoying them.

The Campfire has a hint of marshmallow for a pretty sweet brew. This is one of my daughter's favorites (no surprise there since she has an incredible sweet tooth). Midnight has a slightly deeper, dark chocolate flavor, still sweet but richer. Milk Chocolate is just that, a traditional creamy milk chocolate cup of warmth.

Peanut Butter Cup is my second favorite. Besides the fact that I love peanut butter and chocolate together, drinking the flavor is wonderful. It is subtle though and not overpowering. Carnival is another favorite flavor combination of mine - caramel and salt. Again, it is not overpowering in flavor but in the background layered with the hot cocoa. Lastly is actually my favorite. Mexican is a slightly spicy hot cocoa - rich chocolate with a hint of Mexican spiciness.

I will reiterate that the flavors are subtle. You do not feel like you are drinking a mug of peanut butter cup but the flavor is there. My daughter did not like the Mexican or Midnight (no surprise since they aren't the sweetest ones!) but I have. They work well in our Keurig brewer and have be used in 8 oz and 10 oz with little difference in the flavor level. Sample box provided for review.

product_title: Explore Asia Organic Black Bean Spaghetti,
review_headline: Who Knew Gluten Free, Vegan & Organic Could Make Me So SIck
review_body: I was eager to try this black bean spaghetti because of the high protein, high fibre, high iron and low calorie nutrition information.  I have never, ever had a problem eating black beans before, I really liked black beans because they were a good source of protein as I am vegan.  So looking at the packaging I was excited to try this, especially since I saw that it was vegan and organic, among other things.  I spent a week eating this every second day and I loved the taste and texture but my stomach was so upset and bloated and I felt terribly nauseous.  I honestly didn't think it could be this spaghetti because it was vegan, gluten free, yeast free etc. so I kept eating it because I thought it must be something else.  When I talked to my mom about it she said she felt just as sick when she ate it.  So I tried it once more to see if it was actually the cause of my problems and sure enough I felt just as sick as I did all week.  I was extremely disappointed because I know I'm not allergic to black beans so what else could be causing this?  Is there an ingredient you're forgetting to mention here?  Maybe it's because it's manufactured in a facility that processes soybeans, I don't know.  All I can say is be cautious if you have any kind of troubles with food, it may look pleasing to gluten intolerant people and it tastes great but it may make you feel awful.

product_title: Roland Quail Eggs, 15 Ounce (Pack of 6)
review_headline: Best Quail Eggs In A Can
review_body:
Out of canned quail eggs I've tried, so far these are the most tender and delicious, and you'll pay for this naturally, as of now $4.63 per can at the going rate of $27.80 for 6 cans. If you eat half a can at a time you can cut this cost and feel less guilty about this as I do, but it's not easy to stop eating them! They are like any other steamed/hard boiled egg, so you decide if you want to add salt or dip them in a sauce to compensate. They are wonderful for salads when cut in half and added on top, so easy to make a mini cobb with.

So why you might wonder did I only give these 4 stars? Two reasons. Cost is a bit high (perhaps not unreasonably considering they are a delicacy and shelled), but they are only 3/4 of a can full! The rest is water which spills inevitably whenever you open the can, so be sure to open these where they won't make a mess or are easy to clean up. You'll get a 15 ounce can, but much of that weight is liquid which is heavy. If you look carefully, what you actually get is 7 ounces dry weight, says so at the bottom of the can, so they seem more expensive than I first thought. Still, if you are looking for a special treat, this is it, and Roland is a great brand. I've included a couple of photos so you can see exactly what is inside after being drained and rinsed since you already know what a can filled to the top with water looks like.

product_title: San-J Reduced Salt Soy Tamari Sauce, 10 oz
review_body:
I just called their 800-446-5500 number and asked about gluten-free soy sauce (I have celiac). The answer is this: before 2015, they did brew with a very small amount of wheat (even for the gluten-free soy sauce). So check your production date on your bottle.

Bottles that were manufactured this year in 2015, are 100% soybeans, salt and alcohol. HOWEVER ... there is cross contamination concerns.

Since they also produce the traditional soy/wheat soy sauce (shoyu), every time after they use the production line to make shoyu, the entire line is washed, sanitized, and tested for gluten - making sure it's only 2ppm of gluten (still gluten!) Then they switch the production line to making gluten-free soy sauce. Also if it doesn't say "organic soybeans" in the back, it's genetically modified soybeans. Only the "organic soybeans" one is 100% organic soybeans.

And no, they will NOT stop making the shoyu. So even though this has a great taste, it has 2 strikes against it: 1) cross contamination with gluten, and 2) not GMO soybeans. Still looking for the 100% gluten-free soy sauce.

I struggled with the waste of k-cups and the incredible convenience of them. With these, instead of adding more unnecessary plastic to landfills, they decided to put a light mesh there instead. They work, the grounds never seep through to our coffee, and it's delicious. So happy we found this brand!

product_title: Jason Winters Original Blend Herbal Tea - 20 Count
review_body:
This tea is AMAZING!! Many years ago I heard Jason Winters on a radio talk show talking about how he had been diagnosed with terminal cancer and told to get his affairs in order. He didn't want to just accept that so he went in search of alternative treatments. He visited some native Americans, some people from India and others and ended up developing this tea,
In the year before, my dad had had a skin cancer removed from his face and an identical spot was growing on the back of his hand. I bought the tea, he drank it every day and within 6 months the spot was GONE!!! Truly amazing!!!

product_title: KIND PLUS Gluten Free Bars
review_headline: They are great - I will be a repeat customer.
review_body: I read the reviews prior to purchasing the KIND PLUS Almond Walnut Macadamia + Protein bars. I had seen that some people wrote that the product they received was expired. I originally thought mine were expired as well. I ordered the product in August 2015 and the box seemed to say that it expired in August 2015 - a few days prior to ordering the product. I ate the bars and they didn't seem stale or anything. In fact, they were really good. About a week after I received the bars, I noticed that the packaging on the actual wrapper of each of the bars said they expire on August 2016. The printing on the box looked like a 5, but it's easily recognizable as a 6 on the wrapper of each of the bars, so they aren't really expired. I don't know if that's a mistake that fooled other people or not, but that's my experience on the matter.  Another review said that the labeling of this product was misleading in terms of how many bars come with an order. This is for a box of 12 bars and I paid $14.22 per box. I ordered 3 of these and received 3 boxes each with 12 individually wrapped bars inside, so 36 bars total.  I will be ordering these bars again. The carb-to-protein ratio is awesome and the bars taste great.

The only drawback was that there was only one net pot and with so many venus flytraps it was a tight fit but that truly wasn't a big deal. It's easy to get more pots. And the ready made bottles are too expensive to have them drinking a few sips and wasting it. I have been trying every product I could think of to find something other than store brand vitamins that their bodies won't likely absorb. They are such picky eaters and it is so important to me that they get all the proper nutrients they need in order to thrive. This product is a godsend. Finally something that they will drink other than the Nido Kinder by Nestle. I wasn't too sure about the nutritional quality of that product. With this one I know they are getting the essential nutrients they need and they actually finish their servings. It also hasn't caused any constipation like some of the other products we tried. I'll be sticking with this brand. I like that they like it and I love that it has the vitamins and nutrients I was looking for.

product_title: Flavored Coffee Variety Sampler Pack for Keurig K-Cup Brewers, 40 Count
review_body:
I only got 1 duplicate, unfortunately for one I am not fond of (mint, ewww), but everything else is different! Great selection, got a few I'm not too happy to try out, like the mint and coconut ones, but otherwise I am happy with 90% of the variety.

It seems that the Crazy Cups have edited their cups to include the Keurig 2.0 ring that's required for them to work. One didn't come with it, but since I did the yellow sharpie hack, I don't have to worry whether a cup is 2.0 compatible or not. Seriously, go look up ways to hack the 2.0 to read non keurig cups. I'm pretty amazed at all these reviewers who are throwing away a good number of perfectly usable cups simply because they can't tape a piece of paper onto the sensor or tape a usable k cup top to one of these. It makes me sad, because there's a yummy selection here.

My particular favourite so far has been a salted caramel one and all of the green mountain ones are great too. There's a bunch of different brands and flavours I didn't know could exist!

product_title: Pace Ready Meal, 9 Ounce (Pack of 6)
review_headline: Pace Ready Meals - Bad Execution / Poor Ingredient Mix
review_body:
I wasn't pleased with this product. Sometimes for the purposes of testing food products we are given multiple units. In this case, I am glad I only got one. This is a case of an interesting concept that is poorly executed.

The meal itself, if it can be called that, is a kind of soupy rice with bits of spices in it. The spice level is mild. However, there was almost no meat in the contents of this package. Hard to call it "chicken and rice" without meat; it should have been called "chicken flavored rice."

As many others have noted, the nutritional content of this kind of meal is bound to be lacking. I won't beat a dead horse. All I can say is that I did not think this tasted "salty" to me considering the higher than normal sodium content.

My plan was to add this on top of plate of rice to put all of that good sauce to use. But that's the biggest problem with this. Comparing the rice in this pouch to the rice I mixed with it, this rice is pure mush. I just don't think that things like rice and pasta can be pre-cooked effectively.

This is a product that will not be added to my shopping list. I recommend you avoid it.

product_title: V8 Veggie Blends
review_headline: I can drink it, but I can't get the family to go along
review_body:
We had a moment of pure comedy the other night when I shared my glass of V8 Veggie Blends Healthy Greens around the table - lots of faces made. No actual spitting, I'm proud to say, but I think unless you are offering this to someone who is already quite familiar and comfortable with *green* smoothies, you won't make the sale.

And the thing about the home-made green smoothies is that the veggies are fresh/raw/unprocessed. That makes a definite flavor difference to some, like my husband who will turn up his nose at cooked spinach but loves a spinach salad.

To get a really good smoothie you need a really good blender like a HealthTec or Vitamix. (No, the \\"Osters\\", \\"ninjas\\" and \\"bullets\\" won't cut it.) That's $$$, but so is buying prepared stuff over time. You could also use a juicer, but I'm not a huge fan of juicing as many nutrients and fiber is lost, and a pro blender will pulverize these into easy drinkability.

I think I can sneak what I've got left into fruit smoothies, which of course are more carb-laden and aren't as nutritious as \\"green\\". But going forward, I'm going to stick with V8 tomato when I want to bring home some \\"fast veggies\\".

product_title: Choice Organic Teas, 16-Count Tea Bags (Pack of 6)
review_headline: Pure Mint tea. Fair trade and organic. Great company.
review_body:
You decide on the flavor and types of teas you like. I cannot guide you with that. I prefer Black tea the most, followed by White, then Green. I find Green is just the most bitter, and Black tea goes down the smoothest. I also drink coffee every morning so the caffeine doesn't bother me. Black tea contains the most caffeine so I like that too.
I love this tea brand. I originally discovered it about 3 years ago also on Amazon.com. I buy fair trade and organic coffee, but I always just purchased store brand teas. And I said to my self, 'well why can't i find a fair trade and/or organic tea as well'?
So the search was started. It ended at Amazon.com (also where it began) and with this brand of tea. I had never heard of it before. After doing research this was the brand for me. The tea is manufactured with renewable energy and the teas are fair trade and organic which makes a better tea. Also, the tea bags do not contain any staples. I never noticed any metal flavor from any tea bag that had a staple, but their design is nice and more practical. They tie the string in a unique way to close off the tea bag which I am just fascinated by. I have never seen anything like it. The bags are not brown and unbleached, but they are not bleached bright white like most main stream manufacturers.
Pure peppermint tea, nothing else. You cannot get much better than this. If you love mint or just are not feeling well this tea is great for you. Since it is an herb tea, it does not contain caffeine. Again, it is also fair trade and organic which I don't know about you, but that is important to me and what I consume.

product_title: 100 Foil Seals to Reuse Your Starbucks Verismo Pods, CBTL and Caffitaly Capsules
review_headline: Not all their cracked up to be
review_body:
These are ok if you're looking to have a quick cover, otherwise I'd suggest just cutting your own aluminum foil to wrap the capsules. I've used these seals from My-Cap about 30 times so far and almost 1/2 of those caps failed. They popped off of the edges and didn't allow the machine to puncture the cover so the machine couldn't make the coffee. Other times, the capsules got jammed in the machine due to their bulkiest since they are not sized exactly to the Caffitaly/CBTL capsules and hang over/need to be folded over the edges.
So, as stated above...their good if you're in a hurry. Otherwise I'd just take the time once a week and cut aluminum foil to fit the top of the capsules while filling enough of them for the week. It's not only cheaper financially, but better for the machine.

review_headline: Great value in these larger containers
review_body: As anyone who makes a lot of spicy Mexican and Caribbean dishes knows, cumin is an important ingredient. As such a person myself, I found myself constantly running out of cumin when I bought it in the little containers available in the spice aisle at our local supermarket. In the case of some of my recipes (such as my black bean chili) I used two of these containers for one batch. That's why, when our supermarket started carrying Badia spices, I was happy to see this large 16-ounce container. I quickly did the math and realized that the cost per ounce was a small fraction of what I'd been paying. I bought a container, and when I used it I found that the cumin was just as strong as what I had been buying, perhaps even stronger. Ever since I've been using Badia cumin in my black bean chili and other recipes.

[[ASIN:B00ZB72VTA Candyland Variety Pack Candy Mixed Assortment Includes Air Heads, Sour Patch, Laffy Taffy, Jolly Ranchers, Lemon Heads, Swedish Fish, Mike and Ike, Trident Gum & Many More!! Includes Our Exclusive Custom Varietea Mints Bulk Sampler of 45 Packs]]

product_title: Dennison's Original Chili Con Carne with Beans, 24 Count
review_body:
This is seriously the best canned chili I've ever tasted. Actually, it's better than most of the home-made chili I've tried. The best part is that it's extremely thick; you could literally eat it with a fork.

I grew up eating Dennison's, and I was extremely disappointed when I moved to Nebraska and couldn't find it here (even though the manufacturer, ConAgra, is headquartered here). Nothing else I've tried has even come close. But now Amazon offers it by mail, at a price that's comparable to other brands at the local supermarket, and with free shipping if you're a Prime member!

This stuff makes great chili cheese burritos. Just heat the chili, then warm a flour tortilla in a non-stick frying pan. Put the tortilla on a plate, spread a spoon or two of chili in the middle, sprinkle some grated cheese, then wrap up the tortilla. Couldn't be easier. One can makes about 4-6 burritos, depending on how much chili you use.

product_title: Frontier Spearmint Leaf, Chopped & Sifted, Certified Organic, 16 Ounce Bag
review_headline: Great for Liver Heat ad Poor Circulation. Great Quality
review_body:
I was suffering from PTSD, and my Chinese doctor diagnosed Liver Heat and poor circulation. Both green tea and spearmint tea were recommended to me. Frontier's Spearmint Leaf is HUGE. And it is good quality, chopped and sifted leaf. It's all leaf, as far as I can tell. It makes a great little pot of tea.

Spearmint is the default mint referenced in Chinese herbology. It is warm, pungent & sweet, (Lu & Sp), diaphoretic, circulates Qi & Blood. Though it will make you perspire a little, it cools and calms the liver. Recent studies warn that serious overloading on Spearmint can adversely effect liver and kidney: all things in moderation. You'd have to really overload on it to reach the levels of toxicity. But a couple cups of it a day should do no harm, and I have found it helpful. If it tastes too much like weak Wrigleys gum to you, you can add the leaf to green tea to make a pleasant and fragrant tea, Spearmint tea with a touch of honey has a calming effect on me. And a bag of this size will last you for a long time. Seal it tightly, away from light, and in a dry cool place.

This is delicious and the colors can be soft or vibrant depending on flavor - not your typical same old cotton candy. My favorite flavors are coconut and sea salt caramel but you can't go wrong with any of them. I've served alone or on desserts and it's always a blast and the biggest surprise treat. It's a great, new spin on an old favorite treat! Can't go wrong with anything from Spun Paradise.

product_title: Prince of Peace Ginger Honey Crystals, 18gX30 Packets(540g)
review_headline: The sweetest gift (from my post office!)
review_body:
LOVE.

Spicy, sweet... the heat clears congestion and the flavor is wonderful.

I had to go to the post office one day when I was sick. I waited on the long line while sniffling and fevered. When I got to the window, a postal employee I've seen there for many years, took one look at me and asked if I was OK. I told her that I couldn't shake this cold. She said "hang on, I'll be right back". She went in the back of the post office, and then returned with three packets of this Prince of Peace Ginger Honey Crystals. She told me to drink one tonight, one tomorrow, and one the next day. She smiled and said I'd feel better soon.

The gesture was so touching. Who does stuff that like anymore? She doesn't even know my name, but she gave me these packets of her own personal drinks to help me feel better. I walked out of the post office still unsure if I'd actually try the stuff, but feeling so blessed to have been the recipient of such a genuine kindness. I must find out her name and remember it.

When I got home I looked at the ingredients, and figured it certainly couldn't hurt. So I made up a cup.

Yum, yum, yum! It's spicy for sure (heat wise). But good ginger heat with the sweet of honey in a nice warm cup to sip really soothes. I've since found out that many of my friends already knew about this stuff. You'd think they would have shared! No matter.. I'll share with you :o)

Yep. If these sound remotely appealing to you, you should try them. I've now placed them on my subscribe and save order on Amazon (even just writing this makes me feel like drinking a cup).

review_headline: This slimming tea helps lose belly fat pretty fast and definitely helps with controlling your hunger
review_body:
I have been hearing a lot lately about this Brazilian Slimming Tea from my friends and T.V. so I decided to give it a try. I am not a huge tea fan but being that it will help me shred a few pounds, I can learn to like it.

This package comes with a 15 day supply with a total of 60 bags. The instructions are very simple. Just drink your tea 15-30 minutes before meals or as needed to decrease your appetite and cravings. Steep each tea bag for about 5-7 minutes. You can drink it hot or add nice if you prefer it cold.

They do recommend you stay active during the day and if you do get hungry just drink a few extra cups of tea during the day. I wouldn't go overboard so limit the amount to 8 tea bags.
This slimming tea helps lose belly fat pretty fast and definitely helps with controlling your hunger. I really love that it is 100% natural and helps cleanse your body. And I love how it comes with different tea blends for different times of the day. They all taste great and are not overpowering in flavor. I have been using this for a little under a week now and have already lost 2 pounds. I plan on buying more and using these for a few months.

It also gives me a nice energy boost without feeling jittery. I haven't had any side effects from it at all and My body is starting to feel so much healthier.
Thanks Brazilian Slimming Tea for making such a great product!

This product was received at no cost for review and inspection purposes, No compensation for a positive review was provided. All of my reviews are based 100% off of my experience with the product and I never guarantee a positive review.

review_headline: ... you make your own pasta than this is the best! I'll never buy boxed dry pasta ever again
review_body:
If you make your own pasta than this is the best! I'll never buy boxed dry pasta ever again. Fresh pasta is so easy
to make and the texture and flavor is heaven compared to boxed. I also make pizza dough with it. Yum! The quality of
this flour is excellent and I always keep it in stock. I do advise to keep any and ALL flours you have stored in the freezer.
Most flours do contain insect eggs (which will hatch and mature into flour beetles) left at room temp unused for long periods
of time - warmer humid climates especially. Flour can easily be microwaved for 30-60 seconds at a time to warm it up
before using it. Freezing flour for 3 days after purchase will be enough to kill the eggs.

review_body: These are made in the traditional style of Cantonese style  rice flour fried dumplings.  The inside is traditionally made with salted radish and  pork but I think these are very well done.  Use extra oil and less water to prevent a break down in the skin.  I generally fry pan fry first and then add very little water to "steam" with the cover on.  Another alternative to make these more authentic is to deep fry and pool them out when it is lightly golden.  Do not over cook.  I am a retired chef and like to keep a box of these in my freezer.  Please continue to make these in vegetarian, pork, and chicken. An excellent product!  The only  negative is that these are a bit on the pricier side compared to Asian market prices.

review_body:
These gummies are an excellent way to get that late afternoon boost without a ton of sugar or empty calories like you would get from an energy drink or sugary coffee drinks. The individual bags make it very simple to put them in your bag and snack on them as needed when you begin to feel that slump. So far I have only tried the citrus flavored Gummi Bears but I find that they have a really good flavor and of course that undeniable gummy texture. I get a nice little boost without the added jitters that a caffeine drink tends to give me. It's also easy to control how much or how little you get - eat a few or eat a lot, whatever you feel you need. I know the gluten-free feature is something that will appeal to many - I don't have gluten allergies, but I know plenty who do, so this is something I can readily share with them.

product_title: Silk Original Pure Coconut Milk, 64 Ounce -- 6 per case.
review_headline: Silk Coconut Milk Great Product Amazon Worst Place to buy from, Over Priced, Shipping Expenses too High on Top of Price Gouging!
review_body:
Great product I use it daily for cereal, drinking, or making things like Cappuccino. It has to be extremely cold to get it too foam but my Commercial Cap. machine does a great job of getting it to foam without too much trouble. Be careful not to over steam the Coconut milk or it will separate into a watery mess, also a good time to add sugar or additional flavors like Cinnamon, Vanilla, Carmel Syrup to the Coconut milk before foaming to help hold it together. I keep my Coconut milk near freezing to use in my morning Cappuccinos, you can also add some of the dehydrated Coconut milk base to make it thinker or simply add powdered coffee creamer preferably flavored. Most if not all if it is made out of Coconut by the way if you bother to read the label on that package too, so no issues with it incorporating. Your basically getting with Silk Coconut milk is re-hydrated Coffee Creamer or the mix before it was dehydrated depends on how you want to look at things. Some goes for Ice cream most is also made with Coconut milk less and less with Cow milk due to allergies to antibiotics unstable Diary prices other "Market" problems. One thing that I am concerned about is the price with shipping here on Amazon? Almost $100. for a 6pack of Silk Coconut milk when you can get it at any Grocery or Walmart for $3-$2.00 less a carton then what its listed for on Amazon. You would have to be Nuts to buy it here, its not made out of Gold or some other valuable material its not even real Coconut Milk. See how its made, its closer to Coconut Oil and Coconut Water in an emulsion than "Milk" still with shipping its still not worthy of almost $100. from here. A great product yes, but not that great. Amazon is pricing themselves out of business before they have to pay the help what they work hard to earn. I used to know the term from Econ class short story get all you can while the getting is good. Before the help, Feds, everyone gets wise to whats really going on, By then sell the "Business" to someone else or have an designated "Idiot" to take the fall for all the Cooperate "Crimes." Since when did Amazon become Chaney/Halliburton or the GOTP? I don't buy here unless I have absolutely no choice can't get what I have to have from any other place in the world. Have looked at every site online am left with Ebay or Amazon to chose from the worst of the worst to buy from. I would give Silk all 6 stars if I could but Amazon minus 6 for its shady business dealings, the way it screws the help.

product_title: Russell Stover Sugar Free Peg Bag, Peanut Butter Crunch, 3-Ounce (Pack of 12)
review_headline: Tastes similar to a butter finger!
review_body:
These are fantastic! If you're on a low-carb diet and are looking to curb your sweet tooth these have a similar texture to butter finger, but with a bit more crunch. I personally like them even MORE than butter fingers!

But be aware, on the bag itself in tiny little font near the bottom says "Excessive consumption may cause a laxative effect". I personally can only eat 1 in any given day, or else I get extreme intestinal discomfort. If I eat two... this "laxative effect" comes into play, sorry to put it that way, but thought you should know before you chow down the whole bag! Otherwise, eaten in moderation like all candies should be anyway, these are a great sugar-free treat!

So, it really depends on your bodies tolerance to the sugar alcohols if you can eat more of these or not. For me, I can eat 1, for my wife she can eat about 3 pieces.

These and the peanut butter cups by Russell Stover's are my favorite. The strawberry creme ones are delicious to me, but my wife is not a big fan.

product_title: Liberte Mediterranee Lemon, 6 oz
review_headline: I get sad when it's done...
review_body:
After my mom's homemade yogurt, this is the BEST yogurt I have ever had and
It's the best flavored yogurt ever. The taste is smooth and creamy, rich but not too rich, sweet but not too sweet.
I've had the lemon and the vanilla and they're both amazing. The ingredient lists are tiny and they only use whole milk and cream which is probably why it tastes so smooth and creamy. Cane sugar is the only sweetener. In the lemon flavor you can actually see the zest in the yogurt. It's perfect for breakfast or dessert.
One serving is 260 calories, which may seem like a lot compared to other "100 calorie" yogurts, but the ingredients are real and the taste can't be beat. Plus with 8 grams of protein this alone, or with a boiled egg can keep you full for a long time.

product_title: 9GreenBox - Japanese Black Pine Bonsai Seed Kit- Gift - Complete Kit to Grow Japanese Black Pine Bonsai from Seed
review_headline: Cute and tiny! But beware of damping off.
review_body:
The box this comes in is only about a 3-inch cube as described. This contains:
1. an adorable tiny ceramic pot, with a hole at the bottom for drainage and a bit of screen to keep the soil from falling through the hole. The glazing isn't uniform all the way around, but it does look just like the one in the picture, a dark blue color.
2. a bag of rocks that are much too large (around 1/2 inch) to be the "fine gravel" the instructions tell me to use for drainage. Maybe I'll put them on top of some of my other plants as decoration?
3. a baggie of some unidentified potting medium, not sure what all is in it but it does seem to include both perlite and compost in tiny pieces. Very soft and fine to the touch, no large pieces. Just enough of it here to fill the pot.
4. a third baggie containing 14 seeds. There's a label on the baggie, the printing on it is cut off so I can't tell what the first line of text says, but I can guess from the second line that it tells me to soak the seeds in water for 2 days before planting so that they germinate faster.
5. a folded piece of thick paper with instructions on how to plant the seeds -- but what to do with the plants once you have them, that's up to you.

At the moment, I have five of the biggest and healthiest seeds soaking in water. I put down a layer of fine gravel that I had from another project instead of using the included pebbles. I'll try to remember to update this with my tree's progress.

UPDATE Day 14: I planted the five seeds on day 2, and on day 12 or so, one started to push through the soil in one corner. I've been having to spray more water on them several times a day because it's been so hot and dry lately (misting with water from my brita filter that has a teensy tiny amount of water-soluble fertilizer in it). Today I noticed that the top layer of soil across most of the planter had fused together into a little shelf that was raised up a bit, so I carefully lifted it off to reveal three other seeds that were starting to push upwards. I crumbled the shelf up into smaller soil particles again and gave it even more water. The fifth seed in the far corner has yet to show itself, but the first to pop up is starting to come free of its seed coat.

UPDATE Day 23: The fifth seed still has not sprouted, I think that one was a dud. I carefully moved three of the seedlings to another container, keeping the one in the center. The seed coat has fallen off, and the 8 cotyledons have spread out a bit, revealing the bud in the center that will lead to the first true leaves.

UPDATE Day 30: The sprout remaining in the pot drooped over, at first I assumed it was because of the heat wave, so I gave it a bit more water, but it didn't perk up even several days later. I pushed away some of the soil near the base and my suspicions were correct: it had succumbed to the terrible "damping off" fungus and was almost completely rotted under the soil. The three I transplanted to another container succumbed as well a few days later. Going to wait a while and figure out my best plan of attack to try again with some of my remaining seeds.starting to push upwards. The three I transplanted to another container succumbed as well a few days later. Going to wait a while and figure out my best plan of attack to try again with some of my remaining seeds.

The only flaw I see on the package is it does not note how much vanilla is in the mix. This can be a problem when I am trying to make chocolate cookies. I do not want to over power the recipe with vanilla. The package tells me to reduce my sugar by 1/2 cup per cup of cocoa powder but not how much to reduce my vanilla. I have had to play around with the recipes a bit to determine on my own. (It depends a bit on the recipe and the type of vanilla I am using.)

I love the chocolate frosting that this makes. It is great fluffy buttercream like frosting and it is perfect for frosting brownies or cupcakes!

review_headline: Same Outstanding Flavor, Quantity & Quality in this Plastic Bottle
review_body: This 4-pk product with free 2-day shipping, is comparative to buying at wholesale rates.  While I do prefer the glass bottles, plastic is a less expensive way to ship.  It's also safer & less fragile.  I'd definitely buy this again, especially because it is my favorite flavor (Coconut).  Keep looking, as I know Amazon also sells these 4-pks in a few other flavors too (Vanilla & Caramel pks that I'm certain of as well).  The shelf-life of the products is very good--about 2 years for plastic (3 years for glass).  Even, once opened, they still sit good for about 3 months (even though mine gets used up quicker).  These syrups can be used in many things besides coffee, tea, ice cream shakes; can be used to replaced alcohol in alcoholic beverages; Milk Steamers; and more (recipes can be found at Torani .com).

product_title: Prospect Tea Single Cup Tea for Keurig Brewers
review_headline: Smooth and mellow, with interesting nuances.  Vanilla Chai was a big hit with the coffee drinks!
review_body:
I drink a lot of tea and love trying new flavors, and this Prospect Tea Variety Pack didn't disappoint. I love the buttery, smoky flavor of the Golden Dragon, and citrus bite of the Duke of Earl Gray. Twinning's Blackcurrant Burst is my favorite tea, and Prospect's Graffiti Burst, with its hint of pomegranate, is similar in flavor. I also like the peachy undertones in the Himalayan Spring. The Brooklyn Breakfast is very smooth and the Bengal White has a very mild and pleasant taste. Both are great for an afternoon pick-me-up. I like my tea on the strong side, so I'm not a big fan of the Chamomile Chill and to me the decaf Prospect Park is a little lacking in flavor, but surprisingly the Green Power has a lot of punch (who knew a green tea could have that much flavor). But the biggest shock is that I really, really like the Vanilla Chai. I'm not a big fan of Chai and spiced teas in general but this stuff is good. Even the die-hard, tea-hating coffee drinkers in the house like it. One even said, \\"This is Tea?!!!\\"

So some of the blends I loved, some I liked, and others were a bit of a miss, but that the advantage of the variety pack: its lets you try new flavors without committing to a whole box of a single tea. And right now the coffee drinks in the house are fighting over the few remaining Vanilla Chai K-cups in the variety box. Guess I'll have to buy the coffee drinks their own box of tea.

** Free sample was provided for evaluation and review

product_title: Twinings
review_headline: Must try for classic tea lovers!
review_body: Earl Grey is a classic tea for all hours of the day. It is a black tea with pleasant citrusy bergamot undertones. I love the offering of Earl Grey by Twinings, along with a companion tea called Lady Grey which has lemon peel and orange peel oil in addition to bergamot undertones. Even though Twinings is usually pricier than other competing tea brands, it is well-worth the price. The taste of the tea with Twinings Earl Grey is just right. I also tried Earl Grey by Stash (another leading tea brand that also provides economy packs) and it was not particularly great. In fact, the Stash tea must have had some combination wrong with the citrus flavors and oils because I noticed that I started developing acne on my face every time I drank it. In my opinion, Earl Grey by Twinings is pricier but well worth every penny!

I would not order this from Amazon again. 5 of the bottles either were broken or had leaked despite the outside of the package being fine. It was a mess and and time consuming to wash each of the remaining bottles and throw away the cardboard containers. I just received the comparable order from JET and was astonished by the careful packing. It's important to compliment in addition to complaining and I will be sending them a sincere "thank you" momentarily.

review_body:
I love trying out seasonal variations on products, and since fall is my favorite season, I usually am guaranteed to reach for whatever gimmicky flavor is new for the year. However, for some reason the thought of "caramel apple" for an Oreo flavor didn't tempt me - possibly because the idea of fruit-flavored Oreo filling seemed a little weird. However, my husband really wanted to try them, so we bought a package to try.

The smell that hits you when you open the package is overwhelmingly caramel-y and cloying. The taste is not awful, but not great. The synthetic apple flavor was off-putting to me, and for some reason I kept tasting banana. I had two and told my husband he could have the rest. He wasn't too thrilled at the prospect either. review_headline: These baby foods often had extra ingredients I didn’t want to feed my baby like artificial flavors

These baby foods often had extra ingredients I didn’t want to feed my baby like artificial flavors, preservatives, added water, etc. It can be a real dilemma! Until now, thanks to Beechnut. I’m so excited about this because it means we’re likely to see more companies move this way – giving us more choice when it comes to providing our babies with good, REAL food without having to choose between price and quality. Beech-nut has released a new food for babies that has a short ingredient list which just says apples. How about that?! Just real, whole foods and vegetables inside those little jars. No more include artificial colors, flavors, or preservatives. Now that is REAL food!

product_title: Pok Pok Thaan Thai Style Charcoal Logs, 5 lb
review_headline: A Great Alternative to Traditional Charcoal
review_body:
I bought this looking for a longer lasting alternative to regular Kingsford charcoal briquettes. Barbecues at our house are usually an all-day affair with over 40 guests so I really wanted to reduce the time used to switch out or add charcoal to the grill. I've used this charcoal a few times so far and am loving it. It produces less ash than the regular Kingsford charcoals and lasts much longer. I use 4-8 pieces of this at a time and they last over 4 hours giving me a hot steady heat throughout. That means less work and time spent on charcoal upkeep and more time to chat with friends and getting food out! Also, I read somewhere that if you're done grilling but this charcoal isn't finished burning, you can run this charcoal under water to put it out and save it to use next time. I haven't tried this yet though, since I usually grill to the end.

I see some of the reviews mentioning the difficulty in lighting them. Binchotan-type charcoal like this is usually denser than regular charcoal and thus, harder to light. You need to apply heat a bit more consistently to get them to light. This is the way I do it: I light some regular Kingsford briquettes with a chimney starter (http://www.amazon.com/Weber-7416-Rapidfire-Chimney-Starter/dp/B000WEOQV8) and when they're ready, I pour them into the grill and spread them out. Then I lay the Thaan charcoal pieces out evenly on top of the lit charcoal (sometimes stacking them which seems to be more efficient) and leave them there to light. Alternately, you can use a charcoal basket lighter (something like this: http://www.amazon.com/Camp-Chef-Charcoal-Lighter-CLB-9/dp/B000M9NJ1G/) to light it on your gas stove.
Last tip: it's cheaper to buy this here from Amazon (with Prime) than direct from Pok Pok.... not to mention the faster shipping time.

I suspect Zevia made the change in order to stay ahead of the growing all-natural soda market, but it may actually have the opposite effect with loyal customers. (Can anyone remember the taste of New Coke?) I have been a daily drinker (and die-hard supporter) of Zevia cola since 2009--but not any longer. This week I began hoarding stock of the old formula from local grocery shelves and am now anxiously awaiting the reformulated Diet Pepsi. Good luck, Zevia!

product_title: Organic Raw Tigernuts
review_headline: Look at your food differently...and be happy!
review_body: Ok, do not go into this thinking they are nuts, and they're not, they're a tuber - which is a good thing!  Oh, and hello, chewing is also a good thing - it requires some amount of time and thinking, rather than mindless snacking. You're not going to blow through a bag of these in one sitting, like you could with a bag of chips. They are hearty and, yes, fibrous. I think people freak out about the texture because they think it's going to be like a almond or peanut, but it's definitely not. It's more like taking the first layer of an almond shell off, then eating the husky part and the almond inside. They're about the size of a large pea, and a palm full or less is going to satisfy you. That is what you want from a snack - fuel and sustenance. When you are chosing a cleaner and healthier lifestyle, things are going to be different. The things you eat will be more earthy and not over-processed to accommodate a delicate palate. The food will be what people found when they foraged for before humans got in their own way. Take a second and look at food differently - when you're chewing these and they're tough,  think about the great fiber that is going to use that roughness to clear out the goopy stuff that all over that chemically enhanced food-ish stuff has left behind. Your gut will be smiling, then you will be smiling!

review_headline: My children enjoyed the Berry Pink Lemonade flavored pouches and I tried ...
review_body: We usually save juice for special occasions (birthday parties, pool play dates, etc), so they are a special treat for my children (ages 2 and 3.5).  I have wanted to try the Oh So Organic kids' juice for awhile because it is organic, provide 100% of the daily value of vitamin C, do not use artificial flavors and colors, and they contain only half the sugar that other kids' boxed juices do.  My children enjoyed the Berry Pink Lemonade flavored pouches and I tried one as well.  The pouches are not overly sweet--they contain only 35 calories per serving.  (It is essentially filtered water and apple juice, with some other natural flavors added in).  I definitely feel better about treating my children to these packaged juices over other juice products.  (The reduced sugar and no artificial colors/flavors are big selling points for me).  Additionally, these juice pouches are tasty as a frozen treat--simply put the pouches in the freezer for a few hours, take them out and cut off the top, then crush up the juice ice with a spoon for a fun outside treat in the summer!  My children loved this idea! I was happy to complete an honest review of this product in exchange for a small discount; and I plan to buy these juice pouches in the future for birthday parties and other special events.

product_title: Cap'N Crunch Original Cereal - 14 oz by Cap'n Crunch
review_body:
Cap'N Crunch Original Cereal - 14 oz. (398g) by Cap'n Crunch, from Cap'n Crunch.
The taste of this cereal reminds me a bit of Kellogg's Corn Pops, except in a flat shape, rather than round. That's where the similarity ends. The strangely sweet taste of this cereal snuck up on me after I finished eating a bowl of it mixed with milk. The next thing I noticed was a slight feeling of nausea. I looked at the list of ingredients, and I am not sure what caused the nausea problem. The expiration date was 28 February 2016 (six months in the future), so that was not the problem. The remainder of the box promptly went into the birdfeeder, mixed in with the birdseed. I cannot give this cereal a rating of more than two stars, and even that is being generous.

product_title: Energy Tea - Blue Lady Black High Caffeine Tea - As Much Caffeine As Coffee Per Bag (20 Tea Sachets)
review_headline: Easy to drink and packs a punch.
review_body:
After previously trying the Blue Lady Zest Tea, I was pretty stoked to try more of their teas.

Earl Grey:
-South Indian Flower Orange Pekoe Black Tea, Bergamot Flavor, Natural Caffeine
-One of my all time favorite tea flavors so I was the most excited to try this one and the most disappointed by this one. I popped open the bag and gave it a deep sniff, and it didn't smell quite like the Earl Grey that I'm used to. There was the usual bergamot smell but there was something else there too. It's almost citrussy. I read the label and the ingredients list "Bergamot Flavor" while the paragraph next to the ingredients say it's bergamot essential oil. I'd be curious to find out why the bergamot is listed as a flavor instead of an oil. Fortunately, the Earl Grey tastes better than it smells, but it's still not quite the flavor I'm used to or had anticipated. I was disappointed.

Apple Cinnamon Black:
-South Indian Flower Orange Pekoe Black Tea, Cinnamon Chips, Apple Pieces, Cinnamon Flavor, Apple Flavor, Natural Caffeine
-This one was just plain yummy. It smells and tastes divine. It's very aromatic. You can see the little apple pieces when you look at the bag. I like it as a dessert tea - only if I plan on staying up late, the caffeine kick is very real with these teas.

Pomegranate Mojito Green:
-Young Hyson Chinese Green Tea, Peppermint, Pomegranate and Lime Flavor, Natural Caffeine
-This one took me by surprise. It smelled a little medicinal and I was worried about the pomegranate and mint combination. They're both strong flavours that I would think belong together. Luckily, it tastes better than it smells. When you drink it, you taste teh pomegranate and the lime and the peppermint comes in as after taste/palate cleanser. It's really interesting and it was refreshing. This tea has a little less caffiene than the others (140mg vs. 150mg) you probably wont even feel the difference. Remember that green tea is more delicate than black so you want to brew with water that isn't quite boiling and you want to steep it for less time or it can get quite bitter. Aim for a 3 minute steep.

Blue Lady Black:
-South Indian Flower Orange Pekoe Black Tea, Cornflower Petals, Hibiscus Flowers C/S, Orange Peel, Juicy Lemon, Orange, and Passion Fruit Flavor, Natural Caffeine
-This one ended up being my favorite. It's kind of flowery and citrussy. It's very refreshing. I tend to steep it longer than the recommended 5 minutes because I want to really pull the flavor out. But that can make the tea bitter so it's worth experimenting with until you find the right time and temperature for your preference.

All the teas pack a good caffeine boost. I'm pretty impressed by that. I'm am curious about the listing of "flavor" in all of the teas. What is the source of that? Are they essential oils? are they artificial? If I have the time I'll write the company and update my review with their response.

I received this product at a discount for the purpose of providing feedback in the form of an unbiased written review. My review is based on my honest opinion and experience with this product.

product_title: KIND Healthy Grains Granola Bar, 5 Count (Pack of 3)
review_headline: Healthy, tasty, and three flavors to choose from
review_body:
This variety pack is absolutely delicious. Of the three, the big surprise was that the pumpkin flavor was my favorite. Who would have guessed. I purchased the variety pack to sample the two that I had never tried before and ended up liking the one better than all the rest.

While these bars are a bit pricey for a small snack, I imagine it has to do with the great ingredients added. Everything seems top notch and of the best quality. While some KIND bars have recently had hemp oil extract added in (something that many companies warn against, specially if they conduct urinalysis tests), these little bars have no such ingredients.

Each bar is just big enough to satisfy the craving, but not so large as to fill you up. For only ~150 calories, they serve as a healthy alternative to regular candy bars (~200+ calories).

Overall, I'd say these bars are a healthy alternative, offer great ingredients, and a very nice selection of flavors. I'm a big fan of KIND bars and while they are a bit pricey, I am okay buying them from time to time.

product_title: Gluten Free Prairie Oat Groats
review_headline: I just emailed the company and found out they use ...
review_body:
I just emailed the company and found out they use a couple of very damaging herbicides, called MCPA and Bromoxynil. Look them up and you'll find they are very connected to GMO practices (especially the later one). So for the company to say they are "All natural non-GMO....." is a bit of a misnomer. I'd say these oats are very much to be suspected as unhealthy for the reason regarding this "syrup" they apply to the ground and the plants grow in and absorb. I know they use this, as I emailed the company for info regarding their growing practices and they provided that truth. Look elsewhere if you value your and family's health. These syrup ingredients are implicated in many health issues. For example re MCPA: http://www.beyondpesticides.org/lawn/factsheets/30health.pdf

We need to put pressure on farmers to get with air and start to protect our nations health!

She absolutely LOVES them - to the point where she was basically diving for the spoon and getting frustrated if we didn't get her the next spoonful as quickly as she wanted it. My only gripe is that it's very runny. I understand that a child could eat right from the pouch, but this is a stage 1 food. I highly doubt many 4-6 month olds can suck food from a pouch. Regardless, we will be buying more!

My daughter particularly liked these carrots, they didn't taste fake or gross as my husband is always one to taste test the babies food. The consistency is great, not too thick but also not too runny.  We love that they are organic and also that they come in a glass jar as opposed to the pouches or plastic containers some of the other foods come in. We've started keeping the glass jars and washing them out so we have small glasses for our daughter to learn to drink out of!

product_title: Iorgani USDA Organic Virgin Coconut Oil 32 oz
review_headline: Love it, and will order it again!
review_body: This is the very first time I have ever eaten coconut oil. For my entire life I've been allergic to nuts. I avoided any kind of nut, and included coconuts because why take the chance? Then I read up on the benefits of coconut oil, (antibacterial, heart health, antiviral, slows or reverses Alzheimer's, increases energy, improves type 1 and type 2 diabetes, kills Candida, promotes weight loss, and improves skin). Good lord! Also, studies found that the Sri Lanka natives that consume coconuts heavily had less than 1% heart disease! I researched further and found out that I should not be allergic to coconut oil, even if I am allergic to peanuts. Next I researched the types of coconut oil and found out that the best is 1. unrefined (virgin) 2. processed by centrifuge (low heat extraction), 3. raw and 4. organic. This one meets all the specs! Next step was to read the customer reviews, and 89% of them give this product 5 out of 5 stars, and 6% give it 4 stars! That's 95% giving it 4 stars or above! Hey, what else do I need? So I ordered the 1 lb size and tested it on my skin, then on my tongue, and found that I had no allergic reaction whatsoever. So I used it today in a smoothie and had no allergic reaction at all. It tastes good, goes into a smoothie well (melted first for 30 seconds in the microwave so I can add it as a liquid while the blender is spinning so there are no lumps in the smoothie). Perfect! I love it, and will definitely order it again.

product_title: Whole Raw Cashews, 3 Pound - Sincerely Nuts
review_headline: Okay I guess...but are they worth it?
review_body:
I love nuts. Tree nuts, ground nuts, crazy people-- I really enjoy them all.

I got on a cashew craze and started eating them like crazy. Two interesting things became obvious to me: 1) Cashews are expensive, 2) I moved from my "skinny" jeans, to comfort jeans--- but I digress.

I purchase these because they seemed like an awesome value and I disregarded those people that said they tasted stale. Unfortunately, I should have listened. Honestly, these aren't the worst nuts I've had (imagine a bar bowl or something), but they are also no where near the best nuts I've had (imagine a french bistro with cheese). These are basically average and have a very paper-ish texture.

In spite of buying these nuts several months ago, as I type this review, I have 2/3 of the bag sitting in my office drawer. I just can't convince myself to eat them, knowing they are fattening and just not that good. Is the value worth it to you? I suppose if you eat a lot of them, maybe...

So, its up to you my Amazon friends-- they are okay, but I think 3 stars is a very fair representation of these nuts.

product_title: Swanson Broth Cans
review_headline: A must have for the kitchen pantry.
review_body:
There are certain items that make me panic when my well stocked pantry runs low and Swanson Beef Broth is definitely one of those items. I have used Swanson for many many years and find it indispensable in cooking. It imparts a nice beefy flavor to dishes without that fake bouillon taste. I use it in vegetable soups, several kinds of stews, braised beef tips and tasty beef and noodles. Simmering the beef chunks all day in the broth makes them fork tender without loosing any of the flavor from drying out.

I usually buy the regular Beef broth and not the low sodium because very often it just the right amount of salt in it to season the whole dish without any additional salt. Now, if you are going to reduce the broth very much then I would suggest you go for the low sodium broth as it can get too salty.

I like to add two cans to my roasts, creating lots of liquid to make gravy and have left overs for hot roast beef sandwiches or to spoon over chicken fried steak and mashed potatoes.

This is such a versatile product that the uses for it are endless. This is why I like to have plenty on hand, especially with the cooler fall days ahead and then with winter approaching. There's nothing better than to have a pot of beefy soup cooking on the stove and a crusty loaf of bread baking.
