product_title: Rocky's Ginger Beer 12oz (24pk) 2 or More Cases
review_body:
Had a chance to taste this at Jackson Tavern, on Jackson Blvd in Chicago. Rocky's is a Chicagoland brand, so always good to try some local stuff locally ;-)

Not as strong a ginger beer like Fever Tree, but also less sweet. The ginger bite is not there, but after 5 or 10 seconds after taking a sip of a Moscow Mule, you do get that ginger taste in the back of your mouth. Color is clear, not so much yellowish like fever tree. I did not see any ginger deposits in the bottle like you see with Fever Tree.

Fewer calories than fever tree; Fever Tree 6.8floz has 88; Rocky's for 12 floz has 140

Have not had a chance to compare it to Goslings - although when drinking Goslings and Fever Tree, Fever Tree is my winner. Never developed a taste for Goslings, i guess.

I might switch over to this brand for the 'daily' cocktails, and use Fever Tree for the 'special occasions'.

product_title: Campbell's Slow Cooker Sauces, 13 Ounce (Pack of 6)
review_body: I'd been eyeing this slow cooker sauce for months and put off buying it because one of the reviews I read said it wasn't worth the purchase. Boy, were they WRONG. Ignore the negative reviews, dive in, and give it a try. Carl and I (Rita) just finished our first Sunday dinner made with this delicious sauce. It's not like any pot roast sauce I've ever made and the gravy comes out wonderful. What I did to make the gravy is after 7 hours on low, I removed the meat from the crock pot and put it in the oven covered with foil and set to 200 degrees just to keep it warm while making the gravy. I used the standard Wondra recipe to make a roux (2 Tbsp. Wondra mixed in 1/4 cup cold water). Turned the crock pot up to high and gradually added the roux with a whisk, then just for a little extra creamy texture, I stirred in 2 ounces of heavy whipping cream. The gravy was thick and delicious, and the taste heavenly. So for any of you sitting on the fence about this product, I'm hear to set the record straight. Campbell's has made a unique, tasty sauce here. The flavor is slightly to the sauerbrauten side and completely wonderful for that reason. My house smelled lovely too. Thanks Campbell's. I'll make this again. Enjoy everyone!

product_title: Marie Sharp's Belizean Heat Hot Sauce 10 oz.
review_headline: Marie Sharp's - the best, and original, Belizean Habanero sauce.
review_body:
While traveling and living in Belize, I fell in love with this sauce. Habaneros are in every farmer's market in Belize and in almost every vendor's stall, but no other chile peppers except for the occasional Jalapeno or banana pepper. No Anaheims, Serranos, or Poblanos, but I digress.

Marie Sharp's sauces are on almost every restaurant table in Belize and is most often the only condiment available. That's quite alright with me as this stuff is wonderful. I use it on "stew chicken, rice, and beans" (the defacto national dish), eggs, fish, and every dish that can be enhanced by this delicious essence.

Marie Sharp's makes a number of Habanero sauces, but I've only used the red (orange) ones, and haven't tried the green. They're all great, just with different levels of heat. The "Belizean Heat" is my favorite as it's quite hot, but the heat doesn't overpower the wonderful flavor of the chiles.

I highly recommend all of Marie Sharp's sauces.

product_title: Chef Boyardee Pizza Sauce with Cheese
review_headline: Just like the pizza sauce that came in the "CHEESE" box kits 40 years ago we remembered so fondly
review_body:
40 years ago, Chef Boyardee pizza kit in a box was the first pizza anyone in my family had ever had. Hubby and I were both latchkey kids, and one of the things we always loved was when our parents would buy us the Chef-Boyardee pizza kits in the boxes to make our lunches or dinners with. We were talking about how much we loved the sauce, and I thought it would be a neat surprise to be able to make pizza like that again for him. Now, I can make great pizza these days, and can certainly make my own sauce, usually just using crushed tomatoes, salt, pepper, and garlic, which is WAY more healthy than this, but it was that old taste I was after.
This is it. It's like the long thin cans that used to be in the kits. Did anyone else stick their finger in to get the left over sauce on the sides?

I used a wet pizza dough, stiffened up a bit with some flour kneaded in- see [[ASIN:B000XPNUPY Artisan Bread in Five Minutes a Day: The Discovery That Revolutionizes Home Baking]], stretched it out square on a cooking pan I'd sprayed, and topped it liberally with this sauce, pepperoni if you want it , lots of parmesan cheese (NO MOZZARELLA to get the OLD taste). I cooked it at 425 on the TOP rack (usually I'd cook pizza hotter and lower, but it was the OLD taste of being 13 and not knowing better I was after...) It came out a PERFECT reproduction- it was exactly like our preteen pizzas at a fraction of the cost, and I like to think using fresh dough and good quality cheese without preservatives bumps the health factor up a notch or two from the original boxed kits.

One can makes 2 big saucy pizzas.

review_body:

It melts nicely in the mouth and is absolutely delicious. I will never ever ever go back to the average run of the mill cotton candy and will definitely be repurchasing from Spun Paradise in the future!!

I received this product at no cost to me and have provided my honest and unbiased review in return.

review_body:
Kitchen Bouquet is a product that is both useful and versatile and one that is certainly worth having for a variety of purposes. We've used it in beef gravy as a flavor enhancer and have discovered that it adds a richer taste. We also have found that it works really well when brushed on steaks and roasts prior to grilling or roasting. Like with the gravy, it adds nicely to the flavor. Another selling point is that this product is sodium-free. Can't ask for much more.

This is a very useful product to have on hand and one that is extremely versatile. We have used it to enhance the flavor of beef gravy and have found that our gravy has a richer taste when we use it than when we don't. We've also brushed it on various cuts of beef (steaks and roasts in particular) prior to grilling or roasting and it adds to the flavor nicely. This product is sodium-free, which is an added benefit.

product_title: Lancaster Soft Creme Candy, 4 Ounce (Pack of 8)
review_headline: Nice flavor, soft and not sticky.
review_body:
The Lancaster Creme Candy looks like a caramel, which are one of my favorite candies. However, I usually avoid eating caramels to protect my dental work! So, I was very interested to try the Lancaster Soft Creme Candy to see if they would fulfill my caramel craving without that dangerous stickiness.

These candies are very soft and kind of squishy -- in an odd way, the texture is similar to a regular caramel but without the hard stickiness. The flavor is light and true, and is not over whelmed with sweetness like some candy.

The Lancaster Soft Creme Candy Vanilla and Caramel are a nice choice for fans of caramels and other non-chocolate candy. I liked these enough that I also bought the Raspberry and Vanilla flavored candies, which are very good.

product_title: Vosges Haut-Chocolat Matcha Green Tea & Spirulina Super Dark Chocolate Bar, 3 oz
review_headline: Very Good, But Not Sure If Spirulina Is A good Idea.
review_body:
Vosges Haut-Chocolat Super Dark Super Foods + Dark Chocolate Matcha Green Tea -- 3 oz. (85g), from Vosges Haut-Chocolat.
Vosges IP seems to have a penchant for off-beat chocolate combinations, and this one is no exception. Other combinations include bacon, coconut ash, coconut charcoal, pink salt, black salt, banana, smoke and stout. What next? This particular dark chocolate is aimed at the health food crowd, and is slightly bitter, getting close to unsweetened baking chocolate, and though it contains matcha tea powder, there is no green color, so the amount of matcha is minimal. It also contains spirulina powder. Spirulina is a blue-green algae form of protein, but it can easily become contaminated with microcystins that can cause intestinal problems and liver cancer. Spirulina may also contain lead, mercury and arsenic, so it might be a better idea to get your protein from other sources, like animal (meat) and plant (beans). See the Spirulina web entry on WikipediA: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spirulina_(dietary_supplement). Four Amazon stars.

The Nutrition Facts, and Ingredients, as listed on the package:

Nutrition Facts:
Serving Size 4 squares (43g), Servings per Container 2.
Amount Per Serving:
Calories 240, Calories from Fat 160.
Total Fat 18g (Saturated Fat 10g, Trans Fat 0g).
Cholesterol 0mg.
Sodium 5mg.
Total Carbohydrate 14g (Dietary Fiber 5g, Sugars 12g).
Protein 4g.

Ingredients:
Dark Chocolate (cocoa mass, sugar, cocoa butter, soy lecithin - an emulsifier, natural vanilla), cocoa nibs, matcha green tea, spirulina powder.

CONTAINS SOY.
MANUFACTURED IN A FACILITY THAT HANDLES TREE NUTS, PEANUTS, SESAME SEEDS, MILK, WHEAT AND SOY.
GLUTEN FREE. VEGAN.
CHOCOLATE BAR CONTAINS: 72% COCOA SOLIDS MINIMUM.

Sold by Caputo's Market and Deli.

And as for focus, well let's just say, it got me focus enough to write this review and finish some comedic scripts on time.

4 stars because the instructions should be a on a white font on the little package, and is there a way to drink it with out adding hot water to the cup, then disccarding it, then putting the matcha? (I am in California this summer, and want to do my part to help the drought).

Buy this stuff if you looking for tasty matcha, and a quality product.

With sucralose, this is not a health bar. It is on the same level as nutrasweet and causes severe reactions in some people including myself. There is nothing healthy about sucralose. Stevia is a very healthy and great tasting alternative and natural sweetener but extremely hard to find in current products and if the so-called health food companies would just use it, then it could be called healthy. Also, Saccharin is a very good sweetener that has been given a bad rap for years by the FDA for supposedly causing cancer but has never been proven. I have used "Sweet & Low" daily for over 35 years in my coffee, tea, cereal, etc. and have had virtually no reactions ever and am cancer free! Other countries don't want America's health foods because of the awful, unhealthy ingredients. The FDA should demand that products containing splenda and nutrasweet have bold lettering on the package warning consumers. Shame on atkins. Thanks

product_title: Energy Tea - Blue Lady Black High Caffeine Tea - As Much Caffeine As Coffee Per Bag (20 Tea Sachets)
review_headline: Tastes good but the price is steep.
review_body:
I've been recently trying to cut back on the amount of coffee I have been drinking....because (if I'm honest) I've been drinking way too much. Sometimes I drink 6 cups a day, sometimes more and I realized that the coffee is partially why I'm anemic (the coffee prevents iron absorption) so I have been slowly scaling back until I'm at no coffee. This morning is the first morning that I've gone without coffee and instead I brewed a nice mug of this tea and sweetened it with some honey.

I definitely like this tea. It tastes like apples and cinnamon (but moreso the cinnamon) and is definitely a nice fall season tea or cool weather tea. I definitely feel more alert after drinking it and I like the packaging -- it's more compact than a box and thus takes up less space in my tea pantry. My only concern is that this tea is $15 for the entire packet. I'm no stranger to a higher priced tea (I purchase Harney & Sons on the regular) but I'm not sure that I would shell out the full $15 + shipping for this tea. It tastes good and it definitely gives you a jolt but I'm on the fence about whether or not I'd repurchase.

Their product is clean, delicious, super quick to arrive and never clumped together.  We like both the coarse chunky kind for cooking and the super-fine for our table. product_title: Bulksupplements Pure Stevia Powder
review_headline: Very sweet, I don't detect bitterness.
review_body:
I also received this as a free sample for purchasing another product.
My package label reads "Stevia Extract".
It arrived well sealed, I carefully tapped the product down into the bottom of the foil pouch and sealed the zip closure before cutting the top open. no poof of powder. I dipped my finger along the inner surface to get a good film of powder on it, which I tasted directly. Excellent nearly overpowering sweetness, and zero bitter flavor, either it isn't present or I am not sensitive to it.

I made up a liquid solution for use in a dropper bottle. I started with 15g powder and 60g water (2oz) in a 4oz bottle, same as for sucralose. It dissolved quickly but as expected was about half or less as sweet when measured/diluted in a glass of water and sampled. I added another 15g powder, still a bit weak and much slower to dissolve, requiring a bit of bottle shaking and waiting. Still not enough to equal the sucralose. I then added another 5g of powder, I think this is approaching the limit as it took about an hour to get it to dissolve. Final result is about as close as I can tell to the sucralose solution of 2 drops = 1 packet packaged sweetener = 2tsp of sugar. This was accomplished at 35g Stevia extract added to 60g of water.

product_title: N'Joy Zero Calorie Sweetener
review_headline: Empty/Partial Packets are an issue -- are these Seconds?
review_body:
This review is for the 2,000 packet N'Joy sucralose. Bottom line: a nuisance as I have to weigh each packet to weed out the partially filled ones as nearly 3% are partially filled or completely empty.

I purchased a 2000-packet box in January 2015, and I just finished the box in December 2015. Out of these 2,000 packets, 48 were empty or partially filled. I have bought my replacement from another Amazon vendor (miracle sweet). The problem is that either the packets are cut incorrectly (misalignment of the cutters?) during the manufacturing process and the sweetener comes out, or the packets aren't even filled with anything.

My advice would be to seek another source as there are options at about the same price that might not have this problem.

If you do buy these, be careful. The packets arrived loose in a box. If you see a picture showing them in a bag, well, that's not how they arrived at my place. So be careful when you open the box so you won't cut the packets and make your situation even worse.

Note: each packet (when full) weighs 1.2 - 1.3 grams (1.0 grams powder). I know because I bought a scale just for this purpose given the number of partially-filled packets I have been finding.

product_title: Essentia 9.5 pH Drinking Water
review_body: I bought this originally at Wegman ( grocery store in Rochester, NY) so I already knew what it taste like. I know that when you sweat a lot from any kind of working or from heat/humidity you loose a lot of minerals from your body which you need and most people usually drink plain water but, they should be drinking something with electrolytes in it to hydrate their body. This product has sort of a sweet and fresh taste to me. I do not only drink this as it is a 9.5 pH drinking water (mainly when I sweat or am sick)  I also drink filter water as well. If you body is to acidy then this is an excellent water to bring it back into balance. I would strongly recommend that you get pH Test Strips - Litmus Paper for pH Testing 4.5-10 Range. Note: these test strips are for the pH in your body not in the water!  Amazon sells them or you can go to most any Health Food Store. (NOTE: when I did this write up I did not know there was a recall on some Essentia 9.5 pH drinking water nor did I know that the company gave some customers a hard time or I WOULD NOT have recommend this product. So use care when you take some ones opinion as they may not have all the facts. Would I buy this product again? No I will find something else.)

product_title: Happy Baby Organic Baby Cereals with DHA + Pre & Probiotics, 7 Ounce Canisters (Pack of 6)
review_headline: A good alternative to traditional baby cereal
review_body: I avoided purchasing baby cereals for my child for a few reasons; primarily, I was concerned about the level of processing involved in production, but I also didn't see the benefit (especially at the cost) when there are more nutritious foods for baby to consume. So for a long time, I made due using my food processor to blend oatmeal that I cooked for her. On the recommendation of a friend, I decided to try this oatmeal. I definitely prefer the nutritional benefits of oatmeal to rice cereal, and the added supplements are great for helping your growing little one to get enough iron. And honestly.... this is just so easy (even compared to the relatively simple process of oatmeal making!) Just mix with water and it's ready. This purchase has lasted quite a while, and my LO enjoys it (especially when I add a little cinnamon- it is pretty bland on its own.) I would buy again!

review_headline: The best thing about the Gift Package was the shipping container..

The sirloin steaks were thin and tasteless. The burgers were grey and barely edible (only because we covered them with cheese.). I grilled the "filets" and the taste was OK but they are so thin that it is just crazy.

These guys sell the pictures and descriptions of their stuff but it DOES NOT look like that when it arrives at your doorstep.

Oh yeah, EVERY ONE of the "vacuum sealed" packages leaked all over the refrigerator during the thawing process. It would be funny if they did not get so much money for this garbage.

Shame on you Omaha Steak...

review_headline: Moist, Flavorful and SOOO YUMMY TO MY TUMMY!

I make "diet" milkshakes with coconut milk (some liquid and some I freeze into ice cubes) some vanilla powder, a few drops of vanilla extract and stevia. It satisfies my need for ice cream. You can substitute any kind of nut milk or fresh organic if you like but this suits me fine. I also top the shake with a sprinkle of coco nibs. So delicious.
Terasoul is a company I highly recommend. I have purchased other items from this company (see golden berries and sunflower seeds) and I have to say they definitely provide a high quality FRESH products. This vanilla powder is almost like having a vanilla bean in your cup. I haven't done this but it could also be added to coffee grains when brewing. I just thought of that! If you like vanilla you will enjoy buying the Vanilla Bean Powder.

product_title: Monster Energy Drink, Absolutely Zero, 16 Ounce (Pack of 24)
review_headline: Tastes pretty close to the familiar Monster original green can formula.
review_body: I'm trying to cut back on sugar so I usually go for diet or better, zero calorie soda. Most of the drinks out there with 'zero calorie' has a chemical ingredient to counteract the taste of natural sweeteners. So, this tastes really sweet, like the regular without empty calories. Why 3 stars? I was subscribing to this for about $32 per month starting October 2014 last year. This was all fine because it works out to $1.34 per can, while 711 and Sheetz would mark the price up usually by $1.25. Then starting in July, the price went up to $45 per case, at almost $2 per can. I dont understand this price gouging. The company is able to produce the volume of product, has been marketing bigger cans than the leading competitor Red Bull at a lower price, and was keeping the mainstream price consistent until this point. It's bad enough it costs 3 times as much as soda, but c'mon. As protest I have unsubscribed for now and will just have to see if the price drops. I guess I'll stick with coffee in the morning unless the price of that goes up exponentially.

product_title: Black Turtle Beans
review_body: I have purchased dry black beans (aka turtle beans) for years. I prefer higher quality black beans because they tend to have truer coloration. I frequently purchase Bob's Red Mill for other products due to their high quality. But I will not be be purchasing these beans again. During the soaking phase, many of the beans split apart. By the time I cooked them, they all had split. I followed Bob's Red Mill instructions and tried with both stove top and pressure cooking. The end result was the same. I do not think the issue is the cooking time (as I check them frequently) but the quality of the beans. When they are already splitting apart during the soaking, you must expect a difficulty with the actual cooking process. These would be okay for most soups or refried beans or dips. They are not acceptable when you want the beans to be firmer for an entree or taco salad.

The taste is good, but for me the method of meal delivery is odd. It feels strange squeezing and sucking a smoothie out of a bag. I'm used to drinking my smoothies out of a blender cup. However, when I don't have time to make my own, this is a good alternative.

It isn't the healthiest of options though. While it doesn't have high fructose corn syrup, it does have added sugar and totals 24 grams of sugar. Some of that sugar is from the fruit added of course and some from the yogurt, so does it really needed sugar added on top of that? No. If they omitted the sugar, I would feel much better about buying and consuming this.

review_headline: Safer than chemicals to us for cleaning
review_body: We live where we deal with hard water and its ISSUES! I have found this to be the safest & most effect way to deal with hard water deposits and the scum it leaves around the facets and in the toilets, and not to forget the dishwasher. Just put about a teaspoon full with your normal dish washing detergent and be amazed at how this citric acid ( found in lemons & limes,+smells better than vinegar) takes all that build up off, it took a few times to take it all away, but that's OK with me, because it looks like new inside now! I also got a sturdy spray bottle and put in about 1/4 cup of citric acid and then filled the top with water to be able to spray the facets with the solution and wipe off. I just had to remember my science, alkaline counter acts acid and visa versa:-) BUT I HAVE TO SAY IT IS A STRONG ACIDIC SO BE CAREFUL ESPECIALLY WITH CHILDREN. IT'S LIKE SOUR PATCH KIDS TIMES 10:-)

product_title: Kraft Macaroni & Cheese Dinner, Original, 7.25 oz
review_headline: Old faithful friend after a long, hard day.
review_body: I admit it. I love Kraft orange Mac and cheese. All the trendy food stuff with gluten free, no carbs, organic whatnot etc etc I simply do not care. When I have a cold, the flu or just a rough day I want comfort food that doesn't require fancy ingredients or any actual effort to prepare. That is what Kraft Mac & cheese is - straight up, creamy, tasty, easy to make and inexpensive comfort food. You can dress it up if you want with tomato sauce or meat or extra real cheese or even cheese and tuna for a lazy bachelor's type of tuna noodle casserole if you're so inclined,but plain straight from the pan into my bowl is just fine too.

product_title: Pure Organic Fruit Sandwich, 20 Count
review_headline: Packed with Fruit Flavor and Goodness
review_body:
These little bars are packed full of delicious fruit flavor and goodness. Each bar is only 60 calories, but they pack a fruity punch that makes them taste better than candy. I use them when I'm craving something sweet, and I always feel satisfied when I have one. Because they are big flavor bombs, they feel indulgent even though they are a relatively healthful snack. The bars are small enough to tuck into a purse or pocket, and are perfect for travel.

The flavors of both pineapple and passionfruit come through, and the bars are both sweet and tangy. The texture is a bit odd, sort of like hard gelatin, but I kind of like it. The bars are a bit sticky, so if you're traveling, I recommend you peel the wrapper down to expose the bar and eat the bar while it's still in the wrapper.

product_title: Jordan's Skinny Gourmet Sugar Free Syrup
review_headline: Not the pumpkin spice syrup I was looking for.
review_body:
I was very much looking forward to fulfilling my lifelong dream of enjoying a delicious, homemade pumpkin spice latte without the sugar I don't need. (And by "lifelong" I mean since the early aughts when I discovered pumpkin spice flavored treats was a thing.)

Sadly, this syrup failed to make this dream a reality.

It didn't taste like pumpkin spice, at all. It didn't taste like any spice I've ever encountered. It did sorta seem like a combination of the bitterness of Stevia, the sickly artificial sweetness of saccharine, the texture of motor oil and the aftertaste of children's aspirin.

That being said, I'm willing to concede that it could be my fault for not using whole milk, not doing the whole frothing and warming thing, and trying to make a lazy-man's latte by just adding a tiny bit of syrup to my coffee and stirring.
There are SO MANY great reviews that I must have done something wrong.

I hate to bash a product that might be made with the best of intentions for people who really need an alternative to the sugar that is in everything and
I'll gladly change my rating if there is a way to make this syrup taste even acceptable!

I might try again when the memory of the taste of the plain syrup in coffee fades enough to allow me to recall it without gagging.

Bright side: this is a great item to have in my poison control kit!

product_title: Penguin Caffeinated Peppermints, One 16 oz Bag and One 1.75 oz Tin
review_body:
I recently quit drinking soda and can't drink coffee, so these are now my primary source for caffeine. They're perfect for me: two for a little burst of energy, then another one now and then to keep it up. There's not too much caffeine in each mint, so if you're used to larger amounts, you might not notice, but I do. They also have a bit of a chalky taste, but they are still quite enjoyable as a mint, even if you don't consider the caffeine added into the equation.

I really like that they sell these in a bulk bag with one tin. I buy another brand of mints, and can only get them in tins so I have a bunch of empties that I repurpose and give away. This way of selling the mints is ideal for me.

Great for long trips where restrooms are few and far between. (Or when you're stuck in a middle-of-the-night mountain pass closure because a semi hit a bear - true story!)

product_title: Near East Long Grain & Wild Rice Mix
review_body:
For $2.14 per box, delivered, this is a great purchase.

Tabouli is one of my favorite foods. The base ingredients are always nicely packaged without any issues. It has a very long shelf life. This is much better than purchasing plain bulgar wheat at the grocer because it contains the spices that help make the traditional tabouli dish.

Everyone's recipe is a bit different but I prefer strong, fresh lemon and lots of cucumbers, tomatoes, parsley and onions. It needs a while in the fridge for all the flavors to meld. Shake or stir very few hours.

I take this on trips when I carry my cooler. I prefer to avoid fast-food places and meat products. Always take something to rinse your mouth and brush your teeth because tabouli likes to hide in crevices.

I vacuum seal it in jars and bags but always add the lemon and onion just prior to eating. A cheap meal that can go a long way.

Not sure why this is not showing as a verified purchase.

product_title: Kellogg's Special K Special K Cereal - Gluten Free - 11 oz
review_headline: One of the best GF cereals
review_body:
In my opinion one of the best gluten free cereals on the market now. The cereal pieces are thick, crispy and sweet and hold their crispiness well in milk. Most gluten free cereals I've had lack sustenance, have a less than desired taste, or are just off a bit. This is one that I think you would be hard pressed to know it was gluten free. I've been sensitive to other gluten free products, but have eaten this cereal for a few months and haven't had any adverse symptoms and recommend trying a box.

Before eating gluten free, I had eaten Special K and while the flakes resemble somewhat the original Special K, the taste is not the same. For me I really like the gluten free taste and wasn't disappointed that the product wasn't made to exactly match the original.

product_title: Coffee Variety Sampler Pack Single Serve Cups for Keurig K-Cup Brewers, 40 Count
review_body: I'm reordering this collection. Although there were a few selections I didn't care for (mostly the bold) I was able to work around them. I agree with one of the reviewers. I wished there were some dupes of ones I liked, but then one can always order single flavors, although it looked from my research that the price is higher for single flavors. The price is right for this collection. I did have a issue with a couple of units that did dump grounds into the mechanism the cup fits into. I had to take it apart and clean it. I think it happened twice with this collection. I can't remember the brand but I think the flavor was peanut butter and jelly, and a pumpkin something. I'm expecting company over Labor Day weekend. This will be a nice treat for an evening coffee, letting the guests choose a flavor. With any luck, they will pick the bold ones. If not, I weaken them down by running through the process again, adding water and microwaving, or adding some cream and sugar.

product_title: Good Earth Organic Tea
review_body:
From the moment the first sip touches the tip of your tongue, to the third time you refresh the paste on your toothbrush, this tea will not let you be. It seems to have a strange, sickening, aggressive, slightly burning, cleaner-esque sweetness that burrows into the tip of the tongue. The abhorrent flavor then divides and careens down the sides of the tongue until there escape seems impossible. And release will not come easily. Once the house is empty of peanut butter, and the toothpaste tube is just a curled husk, one has to resign themselves to the strange sensation and oddly sweet taste loitering on the tongue.

One sip; it only lasts several hours. The taste was gone when I woke up the next day.

2) The oil is coconut oil, which is the best oil for popcorn and makes for the best flavor
3) The kernels are a good and of great quality. You don't want too large of kernels because in my opinion that means they are tough

Cons:
1) I wish the bag had a perforated opening so you wouldn't have to have scissors all of the time.

Adding in a bit of honey or blue agave helps with taste.  OK, so why only 2 stars...yes, you guessed it.  Like other reviewers here, the flatulence is ridiculous.  I've had gas from time to time in my life but nothing like this.  Between the farting and constant constipation, it's not worth it.  Plus, although it does not tout itself as a weight loss product, I figured I'd lose a few pounds with not eating 1-2 meals a day.  Not only did I not lose weight, I GAINED weight!!  I cancelled my subscription.

product_title: Traditional Medicinals  Tea
review_headline: which I HATE by the way
review_body:
Effectiveness: I've been drinking the tea for about a week now and have seen a small gradual increase in milk supply. Definitely something I will continue trying long term.

Taste: The tea has a strong black licorice taste; which I HATE by the way. So, I started putting some honey and 100% real lemon juice in it and BOOM, my taste buds are in looove! The lemon is key to masking that strong licorice taste. Now I drink it even when I probably don't need to. Lol!

Convenience: The box states to "enjoy 3-5 bags per day" and let each bag sit in hot for at least 10 mins. Depending on your schedules, this may be an issue. I'm currently on maternity leave so I haven't really found this to be an issue. Plus, I just love the taste of the tea so much (thanks to my added ingredients, of course) that drinking 3 - 5 cups a day really doesn't bother me at all!!

Side effects: I have not seen any side effects in myself or my happy baby boy.

review_headline: Milk Packaging Games with price and quantity

review_headline: Good taste, good xanthan gum level, no grittiness, no off-flavors
review_body: I've tried this flour-substitute, and found that it has good taste, about the right amount of xanthan gum, and I did not experience any grittiness nor off-flavors.  It does have some brown rice flour in it, which some people might pick up as having a "grassy" flavor. But I didn't notice it -- and I usually don't like flour mixes that have brown rice in them.  I much prefer this to the heavily touted Cup4Cup which I find has WAY too much xanthan gum in for my taste -- when I use Cup4Cup my cookies come out looking life golf balls!  I also make my own flour mixes when I have the time, I really like the formulas found in the intro to Carol Fenster's 1,000 Gluten-Free Recipes (ISBN 978-0-470-06780-2)

product_title: Chef-mate Chili with Beans, 17-Ounce
review_headline: Wonderful Bulk Supply of a Classic Diner Meal!!
review_body:
I have eaten a number of Creamed Chipped Beef (which is the same thing as Creamed Sliced Beef)

With that said, I found this brand to be very good. I gave it 5 Stars because the quality was great. It was as good in my view, as Schwan Foods Creamed Chipped Beef, and you get a huge amount for a reasonable price.

The quantity may be off putting, but let me just say if you have any tupperware containers it easy stores in a couple larger sized ones, or a rubberband and plastic wrap over the can in the refrigerator once opened seems a good idea too. That is what I'm currently doing with my can.

The Tupperware would yield to longer term storage.

Also another Reviewer here, suggested spooning it into ziplock freezer bags and freezing it, then microwaving the whole thing.
I have not tried that, but I just want to offer the suggestion, as 6lbs 10oz is a lot, and will take some time to eat.

I like it over biscuits or buttered or unbuttered toast is also good. Try potato bread if you have not tried that particular bread with Creamed Chipped Beef. It does a good job.

One reviewer here said he tasted becon in the beef(?) I dont' know where that assesment came from. Chipped beef is salty (perhaps like bacon can be) but I really dont' taste any bacon. I taste a processed (salty) type beef underneath the creamy gravy. That's what I wanted.

review_body:
I learned about zevia a several months ago. I absolutely love it. My only complaint was literally the added color in the soda. And then they went to dye-free sodas!!! I couldn't have been happier.

I have been able to successful shed almost 25lbs in the last few months and a huge part of that is having something that fills me up and conquers my sweet tooth. I drink one everyday with lunch. I have told so many people about it. Even bought some for my mom to try. She texted me days later and said she had the munchies but because of the black cherry zevia she was able to not blow her diet but still have something sweet.

I do NOT taste a differences in the colored vs colorless sodas. It is sad how much marketing makes us assume that colors = flavor. It does't have to be colorful to be good. I for one am happy that we are actually drinking a real drink without added ingredients that is just there to make it look good. I am also thrilled about it being GMO free verified. One of my concerns was the Erythritol since that is something that can come from corn. But after it went GMO-free Zevia rose 100 points.

Overall, zevia is allowing me to diet and be successful. I can't tell enough people about zevia. My favorite flavor is probably the ginger ale.

product_title: Dancing Moon Colombia Nariño Whole Bean Organic Fair Trade Coffee, 2 lbs.
review_body:
This coffee is just pure heaven. It is well rounded, smooth, and versatile. Even though this isn't necessarily a coffee or roast for espresso, I was evil and make it with a stove top espresso maker. The coffee is pleasure, so nice to drink.

This coffee works well in a stove top espresso maker, a pour over, and French press. Getting the grind right is an important side of things. For a French press, be sure to grind the beans coarse. The coffee is rich and dark. There is no bitterness. A slight bit of interesting acidity that gives a nice edge. This is some of the better coffee I have prepared. The roast is exactly right, slightly dark. The beans are slightly glossy without oil on them. They grind beautifully.

After getting a manual burr grinder, I've turned into a coffee grinding snob. It is a horrible disease. The only way to appease it was to buy a big automatic electric burr grinder. If you are going to grind your own beans - spend a little money and try a manual grinder. Be sure to brew your pot of coffee with an hour of grinding. And throw away any ground coffee you haven't used. It is amazing what a difference freshly ground coffee makes. Keep your beans in a sealed container - the Dancing Moon bag is a good place. If you destroyed that opening the bag, a sealed glass container is the best thing to use. You want to avoid a lot of air and you want to keep all the flavor in that bean.

By grinding the coffee just before you brew it - you avoid oxidizing the flavors. Grinding aerates and creates a lot of surface area. Air can degrade that ground coffee pretty quickly.

Now if you don't have a grinder - well you'll have to go to Costco or Trader Joe and use their grinder. Try to store the ground coffee in the tightest container possible. It isn't the best thing to do, but it will work. The one thing you really don't want to use is the little electric blade grinder. That creates a lot of variation in particle size - you just can't brew a consistent cup of coffee with one of those.

Dancing Moon coffee is really outstanding. I've tasted coffee fresh out of the roaster. Somehow Dancing Moon has done a good job at making this coffee taste almost that good and fresh.

A free sample was provided for review.t can't brew a consistent cup of coffee with one of those.

product_title: Gourmet 3lb Bag of Salt Water Taffy by eTaffy
review_body:
When I was chosen for this review, I immediately got excited. From a little girl who had it every summer, I hadn't had it in so long that I forgot what it tasted like. Thanks to eTaffy that all changed.

I received a 3 lb. bag for my review. I was so overwhelmed by all of the flavors that were offered. I believe I went with a pink one at first and man was it yummy. My husband claimed the orange and white ones. My kids didn't really care which ones they chose, just as long as they were able to have some.

Each taffy comes individually wrapped. This makes it great because you don't have to worry about flavors mixing together and turning into something yucky. Speaking of flavors, you get 10 in this bag. They are as follows: Blue Raspberry, Peach, Huckleberry, Cinnamon, Chocolate, Neapolitan, Orange/Vanilla, Peppermint, Pear and Banana.

Having some of this taffy brought me back to my childhood. No, it didn't make me feel as if I were at the beach while eating one but it did remind me of how good taffy really is. I missed having it so much. Who knew something such as this would make me feel little again. Haha. I also remember watching the people make the taffy in their shop. I thought it was one of the coolest things in the world. Oh, the memories.

Beings this is such a huge bag, it'd be great for parties and other types of get togethers. For the price, you can't go wrong either. I remember the taffy at the beach being rather costly. Now that I know there's an option to order online, I will have to do so in the future once this bag is all gone.

eTaffy allowed me to try flavors of taffy that I have never tried before. Some flavors I loved, others not so much. For $15.99, I totally recommend this product. What are you waiting for? Order some now. I guarantee you will love it and you won't regret it.

*I received this product for review purposes. All opinions expressed here are my own.

product_title: Emerald Cove Silver Grade Kombu (Dried Seaweed), 35-Ounce Bag
review_headline: You will need a scissors to cut these large kelp sheets into narrow strips of kelp (kombu)
review_body: There is nothing wrong with the quality of this kombu, which is a product of South Korea.  Meanwhile, Emerald Cove's wakame is a product of China (because it is farmed near the Shandong Peninsula of China).  Emerald Cove also sells an expensive seaweed medley product from Japan, which must be ordered directly from their website.  Yes, wakame and nori are more popular than kombu because they are easier to use and somewhat more deliicious than kombu.  However, the seaweed soup from both kombu and wakame are extremely delicious.  Simply add boiling hot water to strips of dried kombu in a 16-ounce cup.  Next, discard the seaweed soup, which is too salty.  Next, add boiling hot water again to the rehydrated kombu.  Finally, enjoy the delicious seaweed soup.  Unfortunately, sea salt is not good for our health because it causes high blood pressure, stroke, kidney disease, stomach cancer, esophageal cancer, and osteoporosis.  However, the biological nutrients in the kombu are extremely good for our health.

It tastes sooooooooooooooo good. If you love sour cherry flavor, this candy will make you happy!
I received this item at a substantial discount in exchange for doing an honest review, and this is it!
I will buy this again, at full price even!

product_title: Choice Organic Teas, 16-Count Tea Bags (Pack of 6)
review_headline: Pure white tea at it's best, fair trade and organic from a responsible company.
review_body:
You decide on the flavor and types of teas you like. I cannot guide you with that. I prefer Black tea the most, followed by White, then Green. I find Green is just the most bitter, and Black tea goes down the smoothest. I also drink coffee every morning so the caffeine doesn't bother me. Black tea contains the most caffeine so I like that too.
I love this tea brand. I originally discovered it about 3 years ago also on Amazon.com. I buy fair trade and organic coffee, but I always just purchased store brand teas. And I said to my self, 'well why can't i find a fair trade and/or organic tea as well'?
So the search was started. It ended at Amazon.com (also where it began) and with this brand of tea. I had never heard of it before. After doing research this was the brand for me. The tea is manufactured with renewable energy and the teas are fair trade and organic which makes a better tea. Also, the tea bags do not contain any staples. I never noticed any metal flavor from any tea bag that had a staple, but their design is nice and more practical. They tie the string in a unique way to close off the tea bag which I am just fascinated by. I have never seen anything like it. The bags are not brown and unbleached, but they are not bleached bright white like most main stream manufacturers.
I like this White Peony tea because it is just a simple pure white tea. Which is exactly what I wanted. It didn't contain other leafs, spices, or flavors; it was just a pure tea. If you want a pure white leaf tea i would highly recommend this product.

review_headline: Great way to have coffee anytime, anywhere
review_body:
I’ll be honest with you, I didn’t know what to expect when I received these. Coffee sticks, I mean really, can they give you a good cup of coffee, really?

Well, guess what, I was wrong. I like these. I like the way each stick is individually wrapped, that’s a plus to me, as I can see tossing them into a bag when I go travelling since they are wrapped that way.

I’m really impressed by the way the coffee makes up as well, just place the stick in your cup, add six ozs of water, as the water was being added I could easily see the fresh coffee within my mug. Stir it to make sure you got everything and then add whatever you need, that’s all.

I was really pleased with the fact that once the coffee is made, there is no left overs in the stick, just toss the empty stick afterwards and you’re done, wonderful.

product_title: Popcorners Kettle Popcorn Chips, 3 Ounce -- 16 per case.
review_headline: Sweet & Salty Popcorn in a Chip
review_body:
Sometimes you want sweet, sometimes you want salty. Sometimes you want a chip and sometimes popcorn sounds tasty. This Popcorners Kettle Popped Corn Chips combine all four wants into one light chip with great texture. The texture is kind of a bubbly, quilted texture which enhances the chips' appeal.

The kettle flavor is just the right amount of sweetness to me so it's not overpowering, yet you can taste a bit of saltiness, too. And the chips are fairly healthy, as they are lowfat, low sugar and low salt.

Ingredients listed on bag: enriched Yellow Corn {Yellow Corn, Niacin, Iron, Thiamine Mononitrate, Riboflavin, Folic Acid}, Sunflower Oil, Sugar, Sea Salt. The oil used is a healthy oil and like the use of sea salt instead of regular salt.

These are air popped with real corn. One bag has 140 calories and is low sodium (110 mg or 5% DV) and low sugar (3g), so they are relatively guilt-free. There are no artificial additives or preservatives and they are gluten free. This kettle is one of my favorite flavors and the only sweet flavor they make. I like the sea salt, too which tastes like salted popcorn.

Great to serve at a party for something a little different.

After reading and hearing about the health benefits of eating dark chocolates over processed, high-sugar milk chocolates that I've been stuffing my face for many years, I came across this at my food store and knew I had to see what the fuss is about. I have had dark chocolate from a different brand, mind you, but this one... I don't know what it is, but it felt like I was treating myself with something rich and luxurious; it's deep, dark notes enticing my taste buds, and I knew I was doing my body good. And you know what? Something AMAZING happened after I indulged a few ounces tonight... I didn't get the usual headaches or sugar crashes that I used to get with your run-of-the-mill processed chocolates. I felt balanced and satisfied, not at all craving for more sugary or salty snacks like back then. This is definitely a girl's best friend when she's about to get her monthly visitor and needs to comfort herself with some chocolate, but a chocolate that actually adds to her health and not rob it away!

product_title: Otto's Naturals 100% Natural Cassava Flour Made from Yuca Root Bag, 2 Pound
review_body: I'm a huge fan - hoping to see Otto's sell this in greater quantities and at more reasonable prices, but I refuse to dock a star for the price. They're bringing something to market that nobody else is selling.  This flour is as close to a one-to-one substitute for wheat flour as you'll find.  For those who are serious about going "Paleo" or gluten-free, the concern tends to center around a good balance of nutrition and starch.  Hard-core Paleo practitioners may not approve of this Cassava flour as its nutritional values aren't as rich as almond meal.  However, if your purpose is to eat as healthy as you can while removing the bulk of empty calories as you can, using this flour in conjunction with almond meal and golden flax seed strikes a good balance.  See DJfoodie's recipe for the infamous One Minute Muffin (http://www.djfoodie.com/One-Minute-Muffin) and use two tablespoons of this Cassava flour, and only 1 tablespoon each of the flax and almond meal and you will get the fluffiest, most flavorful and nutritious breakfast that fits into a busy morning routine nicely.

product_title: Amoretti Syrup
review_headline: Fantastic taste. Easy to dispense. Multiple uses.
review_body:
Rich, indulgent, delicious in teas or on top of desserts! The Amoretti Crema Di Carmel Premium Syrup is - simply put - everything I had hoped for taste wise. Made with natural flavors and real sugar it still is only 35 calories per serving which is great if you watch your calories for any reason. My bottle came well packed, arrived quickly and in addition to the bottle of syrup you see I received the pump that is shown in my photo. It does reach clear to the bottom of the bottle so no syrup is wasted.

This product does combine well with iced drinks and does not settle to the bottom once combined. I liked that. The customer service is great and are familiar with the products. This item does not require refrigeration. The generous sized bottle would work well for parties or other events where a large number of guests are expected. The back of the bottle recommends 1 1/2 to 1 3/4 pumps per 8 ounce beverage. I found that one pump per 16 ounce iced sweet tea was plenty for me. Anymore than that and the tea was too sugary tasting for me.

Disclaimer: I did receive this item for free in exchange for my honest, unbiased review. All opinions are my own.

product_title: MatchaDNA Black Matcha, 10 Ounce
review_headline: A nice, quick grab of tea, add hot water, stir.
review_body: I've tried this from their 3oz multipacket bag for a $5 difference in price, but I love the flavor and ease of getting a quick glass of tea. It's a mix of green and black teas, powdered, and drank in total.  I work a swing shift, different turns each week. I like coffee, but got tired of the taste. I've tried their easy-to-use packets and enjoyed the flavor mixed with honey. So, seeing the savings $20-$25 for 3oz-10oz difference, I've tried and found it just as good. I probably use more as I don't have a measured scoop, but I feel it's a great value as long as you like it. I mix mine as a big glass of tea instead of the traditional slurry. So I have a bit of mud left in the glass, can drink it, but I dump it. Your preference, either way its a great substitute to soda, enjoy it.

product_title: ECOTEAS Organic Yerba Mate Unsmoked Tea 24 Bags
review_headline: If you are trying Yerba tea for the first time, read this first!
review_body: Purchased this Yerba tea at Abe's Market - great site and service, but I got really bumped with this tea. This was the first time I have ever tried any Yerba tea, so I read about it online beforehand. Some descriptions were saying that Yerba tea could even have a cocoa taste. So I expected something pleasant. But... in no way it is pleasant. It taste like bark and twigs! It is bitter and very strong, no matter how week I make it. And I am the one who have switched from black to green tea about a year ago. And often I steep two green tea bags in my cap, because of how week some green teas taste to me. But this Yerba tea I can't tolerate. It makes me nauseated and dizzy every time I attempt drinking it. And the taste is just awful. Sorry, but this was the first and the last time for me trying Yerba tea. I won't be experimenting with it again.

product_title: Crown Prince Skinless & Boneless Sardines in Olive Oil, 3.75-Ounce Cans (Pack of 12)
review_headline: Salty and of the 24 tins the quality was all over the map.
review_body:
I enjoy sardines. I've purchased many of this same product at the grocery store and recall usually getting about 4-5 small fish in a light olive oil without ever being noticeably salty.

I deplore wasting food but many of the tins were so salty I spit the partially chewed mess out and reluctantly tossed the remainder of the contents into the garbage. Too much salt makes food inedible for me.
Some of the other tins were edible but contained 3 large dry fish in an oil that ranged in color from golden to orange-ish.

Maybe I'm spoiled but it seems like what is in the tin at the grocery store of the "same" product is of a different quality,

Is anyone else having similar experiences with these subscribe and save sardines?

------------------------
9.17.2015
Update: I ordered 12 of the "[[ASIN:B000EEWZF0 Crown Prince Natural Skinless & Boneless Sardines in Pure Olive Oil, 3.75-Ounce Cans (Pack of 12)]]"
So far out of 3 of the 12 tins that I've enjoyed, all have contained 4-5 tender lightly salted fish in what I consider to be a fine grade of olive oil. Delicious! In my Opinion these "white boxes in pure olive oil" are the best sardines that Crown Prince offers.

product_title: Sea Salt Shack Classic Variety 4 Pack | Himalayan Pink Crystal Salt, French Grey Sea Salt, Red Alaea Hawaiian Sea Salt, Black Lava Hawaiian Sea Salt
review_headline: Variety is the Spice of Life
review_body:
I replaced table salt for sea salt years ago because of its health benefits. I was afforded the opportunity to review this Sea Salt Shack Classic Variety Pack and this is how I used each salt since they have arrived:

1. Hawaiian Black Sea Salt - I used this salt to make a body scrub that consisted of extra virgin olive oil and a couple of drops of Argan oil. The consistency of this sea salt exfoliated and polished my skin, leaving it silky smooth and refined. I also added it to my bath with a few drops of Lavender oil to take a soothing and relaxing bath. This caused the impurities in my skin to be drawn out and left me feeling relaxed and my skin luxurious.

2. Hawaiian Red Sea Salt - I made a Captain's boil of shrimp, potatoes, corn, crab legs, onions, and garlic with a splash of white wine and other herbs. I added a dash of the sea salt once the water came to a boil and I also used it to sprinkle over the food once I placed it on the serving dish. The coloring and the taste went perfect with this dish and did not take away from the natural flavors of the seafood...it actually enhanced them in a very tasty way.

3. Himalayan Pink Crystal Salt - This salt is widely know for its purification properties, so I used this to cleanse my crystals and meditation stones, and even the air in the room (I have some sitting a small glass bowl in my room). I also dissolved it in some water and consumed it for internal detoxification. This is a salt that I can use in a hundred different ways.

4. French Grey Sea Salt - I used this salt to finish freshly baked salmon. You don't need much, but it made the the salmon (along with the rosemary) taste simply divine. When I removed the salmon from the oven, spritzed the fish with fresh lemon and dashed a small amount of sea salt over the top...delicious!

There are numerous ways you can use this variety pack, but the above are how I have used them thus far. I am very satisfied with the quality, appearance, and taste. Great for those who want a healthy alternative to table salt or just want to jazz up their spice rack.

***I received this product in exchange for my honest and unbiased review.

product_title: Kashi GOLEAN Crunch! Cereal, 21.3-Ounce Boxes (Pack of 3)
review_headline: Better than granola, really -- both in health and in taste/texture.  You'll never switch back after trying this.
review_body:
The best tasting crunchy cereal I can find on the market that also gives you a LOT of protein, and also high fiber. I'm trying to keep down absorption of sugars when I eat carbohydrates, and the way to do that is with proteins, fats, and fiber. Of the three, obviously the best way to do it is with fiber, and second best is protein. The fat content should be low, and it's very low in this cereal, and you can get it from 1 or 2 percent milk.

The only alternative is an all-bran type of product, and with those, you get the crunch, but you also get the cardboard-effect in flavor. You don't have that problem with the GoLean Crunch by Kashi. It tastes to me as good as any granola out there. You can add the raisins and nuts if you want, but to me there is no need. This cereal stands up to milk or yogurt and remains crunchy.

I use it as a dessert with a bit of REAL Maple Syrup drizzled over it -- that's much better for me than the high-sugar and high-fat alternatives.

Another way to use it is to spoon it over a nice low-sugar and low-fat yogurt. It takes the place of the granola you might otherwise use and it's so much better given its fiber and protein content (instead of sugar).

If you are used to eating regular granola, and you want to switch to this, give yourself about 1 box to get used to the very slight differences. Frankly, I now find regular granola way too sweet, and GoLean Crunch seems just right in taste. You end up feeling sated after eating it because all the protein in it makes you feel full. That's a better effect than regular (sugary) granola, which can cause you to be hungry again quickly.

product_title: One pound Ground Dill seed
review_headline: Love the Product; the Package Needs Re-Thinking
review_body:
Four stars for an excellent product: dry, fine, uniform in color, texture and particle size. The particles are nice and small, which maximizes their surface area and maximizes their taste impact. Packed and shipped safely, and arrived in fine condition. Adds a nice subtle taste to breads and salads. Very happy with the contents, thank you.

Supplier lost a star for several reasons arising from the plastic zipper container the product arrives in.

The bag carries no opening instructions or markings, and only the most limited of description of contents.

The bag's zipper-style open-and-close feature seems poorly designed. There is no obvious way for a Mechanically Capable Buyer-User to determine how to access the zipper feature for the first time. So our MCBU assessed the package and decided that the package called for scissoring across the future opening left-to-right, between what looked like a production seal and the resealable zipper. Our MCBU guessed wrong! MCBU cut off the active resealable area.

Currently, the floppy polybag containing great product slumps on a shelf, secured by a wrap-around rubber band (supplied by the MCBU), looking like a forlorn rag doll next to other manufacturers' baking supplies stored more neatly in their own upright and clearly labeled jars, boxes, and shakers.

A generous bag of ground dill seed lasts a long time, and re-entry to the package is important.

product_title: Volcano Dust Bhut Jolokia (Ghost) Powder - Very Hot
review_body: When I ordered this I had high expectations. The Ghost Pepper had only been spoken of in hushed tones like a thing of mythology.  I approached it with fear and reverence.  I put the faintest sprinkle of it on a little bit of food expecting that I would regret having done so. I tentatively nibbled at it. I detected warmth but nothing to support its legendary status.  In small increments I tried more and more.  Finally, I decided that caution was failing me. I poured it on to a tablespoon and just shoved a whole pile of it into my mouth. It was hot I guess but I feel like a little splash of Tabasco sauce would've put up more of a fight then this powdered pepper. It has a nice peppery flavor and does add some warmth when I put it in food but if you're looking for the hottest pepper in the world you won't find it in this jug.

review_body: I usually have a hard time drinking water because I prefer either Sweet Tea, Cokes, or even Lemonade (with sugar!).I have also tried all the Crystal Delight water additives, etc...  I have even tried numerous so called "Lime" water and they are always "carbonated water" which to me tastes like a fountain drink where the syrup has run out. So, I happened to run across this product and figured I would risk $10 to try it. First, I am using 1 packet to a 16 ounce bottle of water (Nestle Pure Life aka the Cheap Stuff). While the Lime is not very strong using 1 packet, there is enough taste that I actually enjoy the water. I have gone from drinking almost no water a day to 4-5 bottles a day. Yes, I still indulge in soft drinks and sweet tea occasionally, but I figure every time I use my water instead is a small victory and better for my health. It's only been about a week, but so far I am really loving this product.

product_title: Chia Seed Parent
review_headline: I like them a lot and I plan to continue using ...
review_body:
I started adding these to my protein shakes that I use as a breakfast replacement and it makes me feel fuller. The next time I eat, I feel full much faster, therefor I eat less. I like them a lot and I plan to continue using them. They are also very good for you!! Chia seeds are among the healthiest foods on the planet. They are loaded with nutrients that can have important benefits for your body and brain. Another area where chia seeds shine is in their high amount of antioxidants. Almost all the carbs in them are fiber. Because of all the fiber, chia seeds can absorb up to 10-12 times their weight in water, becoming gel-like and expanding in your stomach. Theoretically, this should increase fullness, slow absorption of your food and help you automatically eat fewer calories. Fiber also feeds the friendly bacteria in the intestine, which is important because keeping your gut bugs well fed is absolutely crucial for health. Chia seeds are 40% fiber, by weight. This makes them one of the best sources of fiber in the world. Many health experts believe that chia seeds can help with weight loss. The fiber absorbs large amounts of water and expands in the stomach, which should increase fullness and slow the absorption of food. Here's a little more info:

Fiber: 11 grams.
Protein: 4 grams.
Fat: 9 grams (5 of which are Omega-3s).
Calcium: 18% of the RDA.
Manganese: 30% of the RDA.
Magnesium: 30% of the RDA.
Phosphorus: 27% of the RDA.
They also contain a decent amount of Zinc, Vitamin B3 (Niacin), Potassium, Vitamin B1 (Thiamine) and Vitamin B2.

product_title: Prince Of Peace Tea Premium Pu-erh Tea, 100 teabags
review_body: I read about this tea and given the things I read, I wasn't sure I'd like the taste, but I'd also read it might be a good tea to use to make homemade Kombucha and it reportedly has other good benefits. We make Kombucha at home with other types of tea so I decided to buy it to see what it is like as a tea to drink. I don't know if I'll ever use it to make Pu-Erh Kombucha, because I'm not certain I want to run more than one batch of Kombucha at a time, but I have really enjoyed drinking Pu-Erh tea about a half hour after my evening meal. It's sort of an earthy taste and strong and mine turns out about the color of coffee. It's probably "strong" because I have a tendency to steep my tea longer than the average person does. I let the water come to just under a full boil. I don't put sugar in my tea or anything else, I like it plain so I can taste the flavor of the tea and I find this Pu-Erh enjoyable. I will purchase it again. I think tea is a very individual drink and depends on the person drinking it as to what types they like, how long they like it steeped and whether to have anything else in it. Some are bothered by smell or taste, how strong it is, etc. So, we buy and try and hopefully what we don't like gets passed along to someone who might enjoy it if we don't. For me, this is a keeper! Another point to mention is you get 100 tea bags in this box, which is a goodly amount and they are individually wrapped. I like it when companies go with the additional touch of wrapping tea bags so they stay fresher longer. This is a plus for this company and I went with them for this tea because I do use another one of their other teas as well and like it.

product_title: Davidson's Tea Bulk
review_headline: A sweet, hardy herb that makes a bold
review_body:
A sweet, hardy herb that makes a bold, brothy and frothy brew. The traditional way to make rooibos is to infuse on the stovetop in a pot (or coffeepot), for a long period. You can steep it all day and it only gets sweeter.

I only let it simmer for an hour or two, after bringing to a boil, because I don’t want to wait for that wonderful taste. If you haven’t heated rooibos for at least an hour, you’re missing the flavor of rooibos.

Extra can be left steeping, and reheated in the pot later—just as delicious! Only make sure not to overboil it, because I notice it becomes a little astringent that way.

product_title: Pamela's Products Artisan Flour Blend
review_headline: I love her bread mix - both for bread and pizza ...
review_body:
I don't believe I've ever given such a low review for any product before, and I hope I never do again - especially for a Pamela's product. Her baking mix turns out delicious waffles and very passable dumplings. I love her bread mix - both for bread and pizza dough. But this stuff...

I made biscuits for supper tonight using the same recipe from the Art of Gluten-Free Baking website I've been using for more than a year. My non-gluten-free family members like them as much as I do. Since I don't want to mess with buying a bunch of flours, gums, and whatever else you need to make your own, I've been using a competitor's cup-for-cup blend and they turn out great. Every time. Not realizing I was quite as low as I was, I ran out and kept forgetting to order more. So I figured Pamela's products are good, I'll try this.

Even baking the biscuits quite a bit longer than the recipe called for, they were like a glob of dough. The only parts that got done were the very outside and the edges. What a disappointment. In the future, I'll be sticking with the competitor's flour for biscuits in the future. Like another reviewer, I'm sorry I wasted the butter and other ingredients on this blend.

review_headline: and these beans are the best we have tried
review_body: We make our own vanilla flavoring, and these beans are the best we have tried.  Slit them down the middle and immerse in a fifth of vodka, put into a brown paper bag and place in a dark cupboard and let sit for 6-8 months, and you've got the best vanilla flavoring available.  Shake the bottle once or twice a week to stir up the vanilla seeds, but otherwise, you just let it sit and steep.  Strain when it is ready.

review_headline: Aspartame really isn't good for you, it turns out...
review_body: Okay, I started drinking this while pregnant when I had to kick my Dr. Pepper/caffeine addiction. This was a good replacement for the sweetness of dr. pepper and seemed much healthier. As a result, I started drinking lots more water, which I thought was good. I probably drank 4-5 water bottles of this a day and made big pitchers at home. Recently, I realized that the aspartame in this has documented ill effects and this is pretty much as bad for you as diet soda. Shoulda done my research the first time around. So...if you don't mind the aspartame thing, it's a good tasty product. But it's not good for you!

review_headline: Coca-Cola Life taste just about like regular Coke
review_body: In my opinion, Coca-Cola Life taste just about like regular Coke. The sweetness is somewhere between regular Coke and Coke Zero. But Coca-Cola Life is a healthier option esp. for those that don't like the taste of diet cola. The label has a nice appearance also. Coca-Cola Life is sweetened with cane sugar and stevia (Stevia is a natural product made from a leaf) . The Nutritional information: one 8 oz. bottle has 16 gm of sugar; 60 calories; sodium 25 gm; caffeine 19 mg. and 16 grams of total carbs. It has 35% fewer calories than regular coke and has a great taste. I hope other cola makers start making a healthier option like Coca-Coca Life is.

product_title: Nitro2Go Instant Energy
review_body:
My husband said this is the best tasting energy drink he has tried yet! (And he has tried MANY!!) It did give him a great boost of energy for about 4 hrs, but after that he did have a crash, which he was hoping to avoid. Also, while the ingredients seem fine, he had a GI reaction that he experiences when he has gluten or dairy, so we wondered if there was possible cross contamination in the manufacturing process? Just to be safe we passed them along to a military friend and he loves them!! He is getting 5-6 hrs of energy boost and does not feel a crash at all! He also agrees on the flavor being the best ever!!!

We received this product at a discount in exchange for our honest and unbiased review.

product_title: Amazing Grass Organic Wheat Grass and Amazing Trio
review_body:
First off, I have Irritable Bowel Syndrome and I am plagued with chronic constipation. After reading the impeccable reviews that applaud this product for its digestive benefits, I used it religiously for a few weeks. I mixed mine in smoothies, and could only taste the product slightly. Taste was not a problem.

Although I'm sure this product is packed with many health benefits, I did not experience a difference at all in my digestive problems. Persons who life the "regular life" are probably prone to increased bowel movements.

I will continue to use until this is gone, but I will not purchase again if I don't experience improved digestion.

Also, this product is very messy. Every time I open the canister my kitchen is filled with clouds of green powder everywhere! Green powder in the air, on the floor, on the counters, etc. However, the green did NOT stain my blender, cups, sink, etc. like some people claim. I always cleaned up right away, but it was very easy to clean up.

product_title: Sherpa Pink Himalayan Salt
review_headline: O.K. I Have Some Points To Make About This Salt
review_body:
Sellers like to use hyperbull (sp) to sell their products. "The Best", "Better Than", and other such terms.
Other than the Sherpa Pink Gourmet Himalayan Salt label attesting to this products worth, the rest is subjective..
For instance we like the pink look.
Also, it's to our taste, having used Morton Salt (TM), Real Salt (TM), Kirkland Sea Salt (TM) and a few other brands
that I don't recall their names, we have definitely settled on this one brand.
The Salt Company's Sherpa Pink, well It, uh, tastes good!
It also dissolves well, and doesn't leave a pile-o-sand in the bottom of our after meal salt mouth-wash bottle.
(as other brands have).
Our point of view is, that this particular salt is a long way from pollution. A good thing.
If the salt's source has had open access to man-made pollutants by sea (mercury-oil spills), land (runoff) or air (chemtrails)
in the last 120 years, it's not for us -- our choice, maybe yours too.
Our first order was 5lb, very pleased, this one is 20lb, and were good to go.
You will not be disappointed with the look, flavor or cleanness of this salt. A good buy!

product_title: Milliard Organic Cacao Nibs
review_body:
I'd had cacao nibs a few times before getting these. If you've never tried them, they taste like very very dark chocolate (which makes sense since these nibs are what chocolate is made of!) but more bitter. I think these are like all-natural chocolate chips without any of the sugar and additives you don't need. They are very crunchy- some are a little sweet and some are bitter-sweet, I think it depends on how roasted each nib has been.

I like to sprinkle them into baked goods or over ice cream. I've even tried them on top of salads (just like how you'd put nuts on top of salad). They're also tasty straight out of the bag as a snack. Since they're full of antioxidants there is no guilt when I eat them! I saw a recipe that said that you can heat them up and candy them and that would probably be even tastier than chocolate chips! I received this complimentary product in exchange for writing an honest, unbiased review.

review_headline: Jif Bars are the perfect on the go snack!
review_body: I’m a member of the Crowdtap Jif™ program, and Jif™ sent me a sample of its Jif™ Bars to try and asked me to review. I was really excited to try these bars because I had seen an ad for them but hadn't found them in stores yet. My box arrived at precisely the right moment because I was preparing for a garage sale and was starving but hadn't had the chance to stop and have lunch. I was so happy to see those bars! I immediately went for the Chocolate Peanut Butter bar. It was a fairly sized granola bar with chocolate on one side and peanut butter on the other. It wasn't crunchy, but more of a creamy feeling mixed with granola. It tasted like a protein bar. I found it to be yummy and very filling! My husband had the crunchy peanut butter. His first response was " you need to find these in the store". My daughter had the creamy peanut butter and she said it was pretty good. She did ask for if i would buy some for her to take for school snack, so that means she liked it! All in all my family really enjoys this wonderful Jif Bars sample and I am keeping my eye out for when they hit shelves at my local grocery store.

product_title: Little Me Tea Single-Serve Tea, 30 Count
review_body:
This product is named "tea" and this is the product description:

** Organic white, rooibos, hibiscus and chamomile teas
** Sweetened with organic apple and red grape juice and two vegetable juices

Compare to the actual ingredients list (in case you don't know this - the order of ingredients indicates the quantity, so that the ingredients listed first are what constitutes the bulk of the product while the ingredients listed last are only present in minute amounts):

** Water (filtered)
** Grape juice from concentrate
** Apple juice from concentrate
** Carrot juice from concentrate
** Sweet potato puree from concentrate
** White tea
** Rooibois tea
** Hibiscus tea
** Chamomile tea
** Lemon juice from concentrate

So basically, this is the same water with chemicals that gets sold elsewhere for much less, with just a little bit of tea added (for marketing to gullible parents like myself, I guess). It tastes exactly like that too - the grape flavor that I got is just like every other cheap fake grape "juice drink" or cough syrup or whatever.

Yes it's less sweet than MacDonalds apple juice but that is its only saving grace.

product_title: Cipriani Food Pappardelle Extra Thin Egg Pasta - 8.82 oz
review_headline: Wonderful, Delicate and Evoking Great Memories
review_body: Not since his childhood delight of relishing his great grandmother's icomparable homemade pasta has my husband flipped head over heels in love with a boxed pasta that could compare to hers.  She was born and raised in Novi Ligure, in northern Italy. Clementina came over to Oakland, California in her early twenties after her marriage to young Fredrico Brunetto from Rialto, slightly northeast of Genoa.  Finally, we have found this pasta that is incredibly light and delicate and tasty, truly superior to any pasta we have purchased, ever.  It is so delicious that my husband sometimes snacks a little bite,  even when it is dry, right out of the box.  It is wonderful and equal in taste and texture to his own homemade pasta. (Not as perfect as Clementina's, of course!) Still, we adore the Cipriani pasta because it brings back great memories for my husband, of his Italian great-grandparents and the big family dinners in their home.  It is expensive, but we think it is always worth it. It has proven to be worth more than money.

product_title: RxBar Real Food Coconut Cacao Protein Bar, 1.83 Ounce (Pack of 12)
review_headline: Excellent product AND excellent customer service
review_body:
I order these often, they have become a staple for a healthy on-the-go snack in my house. My favorite is the Coconut Cacao, which tastes like a yummy fudge brownie except it's entirely made of real food ingredients that fits the way I prefer to eat. I don't know how they add the protein without sacrificing taste, but they do. It's Whole30 approved, but if you follow that plan I'd add the caveat that this could easily become Food Without Brakes... in a good way, it's just that delicious. For example, when my colleagues are serving yet another birthday cake or cookies for some office celebration, I'll eat one of these instead and feel perfectly happy.

So the product itself is great, but I have to give extra points for outstanding customer service. I had a problem with one bar coming in an unsealed wrapper so I reached out to the company via the web form on their web site. Within a few hours I had a personal phone call AND email following up. They apologized for the inconvenience and wanted to be sure they understood what the problem was, explained what could make that happen, and promised to send me a replacement. So I went from loving the bar to loving the company - they definitely earned a loyal customer!

product_title: Black Truffle Salt
review_headline: Not enough of the Truffle mushroom added to this product
review_body: If you have ever really tasted  Truffle salt you would know that this is a very faint attempt.  The makers of the production bank on the fact that the consumer wants more bang for their buck.  As to where I agree there is a very small amount of shaved Truffle in the salt, I feel there is not enough to capture the full flavor of what the truffle can bring you dishes.  You need to add more salt to you dishes to get the flavor.  You can take this product, buy a true small piece of the Truffle mushroom, shave some in this salt and you will have the flavor you are looking for.  When adding true Truffle salt to anything, you can taste a very good flavor of the Truffle and only use the salt for taste (lightly).  I guess I should never have had true Truffle salt, butter, oil, honey etc.  After all you get what you pay for.  For the taste and the mount of Truffle in this product this price is toooooo much.

Good for you. Good for the environment. Good for your cupcakes. What more can you ask?

Sure, they cost a bit more and aren't decorated with flowers or stripes or holiday images, but they work very very well and don't leach bad stuff into your cupcakes. I've used these for a while now and they not only come out of the cupcake tin perfectly, but are easily peeled off the baked cupcake. These "large" cupcake papers are actually normal sized, just to say. They aren't for those monster sized cupcakes you can also bake. These paper cups would make a nice gift, along with a cupcake pan, and some recipes (or for the less experienced with some nice mixes) and maybe some sprinkles, etc to decorate the cupcakes with. product_title: Kellogg's Origins Ancient Grains Granola Cranberry Almond with Pumpkin Seeds, 12.5 Ounce
review_body:
I purchase this locally so am not going to cover variables like price and shipping.

Surprisingly, this is a decent granola given that the brand also makes cereals that are heavier on sugar and other additives. This one is a step in the right direction. It's also slightly sweeter than the [[ASIN:B00WBGINFA Kellogg's Origins Fruit and Seed Muesli]], which has slightly better nutritional values and more roughage.

Unsweetened this granola with skim or soy milk is a nice, quick breakfast. I prefer the muesli, but enjoy this once or twice a week. A half cup serving comes in at 200 calories, of which 30 are from the 3.5 grams of fat. Carbs come in at 38 grams, 8 of which are from sugars, and the protein is 6 grams. Cholesterol is zero, but sodium is 110mg (the muesli has zero sodium), and the serving with a half cup of skim milk comes in at 250 calories.

This is about 12 percent of a 2000 calorie per day diet, so is a good source of morning energy without eating into your allocated daily calorie budget.

If you like granola this is a safer choice than some on the market. It is not as sweet as most, but once you become accustomed to that you will be able to enjoy a nice mix of flavors that stand on their own merit.
