review_headline: If you like chocolate, stay away
review_body: Almond Breeze milk is great! I love the vanilla flavor and the chocolate flavor - but this unsweetened choclate flavor tastes horrible. It's nothing like chocolate. Nothing. The only thing that resembles chocolate is the brown color. The taste is bitter. It's like drinking brown water that leaves a powdery bitter aftertaste. At first I thought I made a mistake by not shaking it up. I admit that I neglected to do this. But after shaking it up immediately after, viciously, it tasted no better. I even let it sit in the fridge for two days more. I tried it each day, shaking it like a madman, but it never tasted any better. I recommend other Almond Breeze products, but stay away from this one.

product_title: Boost Nutritional Drinks Compact Complete Nutrition Drink, 4 Count (Pack of 4)
review_body:
I'm pleasantly surprised by the taste of this Boost Compact Complete Nutritional Drink. I have tried many nutritional drinks and many of them have a taste that I don't care for, or outright nasty. This Boost drink has a very pleasant taste. It is like a double chocolate flavored milk, only thicker and creamier.

I like the compact serving size. It's the perfect size to snatch on the go. Each bottle packs 240 calories with 6g fat, 37g carbohydrate and 10g protein derived from milk and soy. It also contains 10g sugar and a plethora of minerals and vitamins. It packs a lot of nutritional value in a 4 fl oz bottle that is easy to transport and tasty to drink on the go.

The only caveat is that it doesn't fill you up at all. I don't feel satisfied after consuming a bottle of it. I often found myself snacking that adds more calories to the 240 calories from this drink.

All in all, this is a tasty chocolate flavored nutrition drink. It packs a lot of nutritional values in a compact bottle. It isn't very satisfying but I still like it a lot.

product_title: Lancaster Soft Creme Candy, 4 Ounce (Pack of 8)
review_body:
Lancaster vanilla and caramel soft crèmes have rich flavor, and a seductive, dulcet texture which avoids the stickiness of many caramel candies. They get that texture with a blend of milk, butter, palm oil, mineral oil, and glycerin (which seems like cheating, but it works). There are 25.7 calories in each piece. They are sweetened with corn syrups and sugar, but somehow avoid excessive sweetness.

Lancaster was the first company founded by Milton Hershey before the company that bears his name (but not his quality standards). These caramels are now made in Canada, probably for short supply lines for good dairy products, but still distributed by Hershey.

I'm giving them four stars instead of five for over-use of non-food ingredients plus the palm oil. Still a distinctive and elegant candy.

product_title: Nutiva Hi Fiber Hemp Protein Powder - 16 OZ
review_headline: Get over the taste; this stuff is great!
review_body:
Hemp Protein is a Complete Protein
Foods can be classified as complete, partial, or incomplete sources of proteins. In order for a food to be classified as complete, it must contain all eight essential amino acids. Hemp is considered a complete protein because it contains all eight essential amino acids in sufficient quantities to meet the body’s needs. This also makes hemp a good alternative source of protein for vegetarians and vegans.

Easy to Digest
Hemp protein is easy to digest. It contains two types of proteins, edestin and albumin, which are known as globular proteins. Globular proteins dissolve easily so they are readily accessible for the body to use. This is in contrast to soy protein, for example, which can be more difficult for the body to digest. Soy contains inhibitors that block the action of trypsin and other enzymes that are needed to break down protein. This can often result in unwanted bloating and gas.

Source of Good Fats
Dairy products and red meat are good sources of protein. However, they can be high in saturated fat. Hemp protein is low in saturated fat. Furthermore, it contains the essential fatty acids, omega-3 and omega-6, in an optimal three- to-one ratio. According to The Office of Dietary Supplements of the National Institutes of Health, eating these essential fatty acids in an optimal ratio reduces risks for heart attack. cardiovascular problems, atherosclerosis and certain types of cancer. Consuming unsaturated fats is also associated with a reduced risk for dementia. The “Journal of Alzheimer’s Disease” revealed in a 2010 article that elderly people with diets including greater amounts of unsaturated fats had a reduced risk for memory impairment and the later development of dementia.

Earth Friendly
The production of hemp protein on a large scale is earth friendly. It grows tall and fast and its dense growth kills weeds. Therefore, the use of pesticides or herbicides is unnecessary when growing hemp. Additionally, Manitoba Harvest, a hemp producer, reports that hemp is a sustainable "green" product even when produced on a large scale. Compared to other crops, growing hemp is more cost effective and less damaging to the environment.

Summary
Healthy for you and a reliable source of protein and fiber. Sure it tastes like hay, but what do you expect? It's hemp, and knowing this product can only benefit you -- and in so many ways -- makes it a smart purchase.

review_headline: THE REAL DEAL... GENUINE & DELICIOUS describes this aceto balsamico (balsamic vinegar)
review_body: I lived for several years in Italy and traveled the country extensively so you can't fool me with a low quality balsamic vinegar.  My Italian friends brought me a bottle of 12 year D.O.C. vinegar when they came to CA for a visit.  If you ever receive a gift like that it's because they think YOU'RE REALLY SPECIAL and they have great taste and know that you do too.  Running out of good balsamic is a great excuse to have to travel to Italy.... because you just have to buy more!  Instead, this time, I searched Amazon and found this 25 year balsamic from the correct area of Modena from a legitimate producer.  (Yes you always want to ask the seller about the individual registration number on the bottle to make sure it's a registered producer allowed to use this unique shaped bottle.  DO NOT settle for an Italian vinegar that is NOT in the cute bottle! This is the only bottle that the Consortium of Traditional Balsamic Vinegar Producers in Modena use.)  The 12 year vinegar that I went to purchase is really special.... but instead I went for this 25 year old bottle.  This is simply the most refined vinegar you can find. A few drops will transform any dish into a new culinary experience.  Try this liquid gold on just about any type of food (with the exception of spicy) -- Don't forget dessert:  a few drops on fresh berries, cheesecake, or gelato is just what one needs for truly blissful experience.

product_title: Melitta Café de Europa Vienna Roast Ground Gourmet Coffee
review_body: Having recently reduced the sugars in my life, I can honestly say that this coffee fills a niche that was missing... not only is the smell wonderful, but the taste is very delicious too. Don't get me wrong, dear reader, it is not "sweet" in the way one of those caramel creamers is. But it gives you the essence of the flavor without the sugar. I put regular coffee mate in my coffee as well as half and half and I find it is a very wonderful blend. It is extra fine ground so I  use less in my drip coffeemaker than I do regular coffee. Recently to save money I have been mixing it in a large can with my other favorite Don Francisco butterscotch toffee---the two meld very well together. I have no acid problems with either, nor do I find them harsh. But on special occasions I will brew this all by itself and enjoy it anytime of the day. product_title: Alligga Flaxseed Organic Cooking Oil
review_body:
I've never tasted flaxseed oil before, and this was more bitter than I was expected. That said, once I got used to the flavor, I've decided I really do like it. I like that the filtering process during manufacture makes it possible to cook with it. Since I started eating healthier, I figured that fried chicken was something I'd have to give up. Oh, I could cook it on low heat with olive oil, but I really dislike the flavor of olive oil. Alligga allows me to have my favorite dish (with a few alterations). Instead of dredging the chicken in a flour mixture, I coat it with raw shredded coconut, then saute it in Alligga flaxseed oil. It has a delicious nutty, coconut-y flavor, and it's as crunchy as the fried chicken I used to eat.

I like that this oil comes in a dark bottle (which prevents oxidation). And as long as I keep it in the fridge between uses, it stays as healthy as the day I got it.

product_title: Air Plant Xerographica W/ 8 oz. Fertilizer
review_body: I bought this in June and waited all this time to review to see if it really was easy to care for. I also wanted to see if it would acclimate itself to an air conditioned home and living in Pittsburgh, PA.(constant 75.) First of all the packaging was very sturdy. I couldn't believe the size of this plant when I opened the box and it was beautiful! It had been very well taken care of. Now after 2 months it is STILL growing and thriving and this was my first air plant! Once a week I pour a gallon or so of water into an extra large pot and I put this plant in it upside down for a couple of hours. I use water from the tap that's gone through a PUR Filter into a gallon jug. I always let my bottle stay open on the floor for a week before using only because I have other plants sensitive to chlorine and chemicals. That's my way and it's been working. Once a month after watering I give it one quick spray with the fertilizer that came with it. Possibly, when I have to turn the furnace on and it gets dry in the house, I may give it a misting mid week. I think that Xerographica from Hinterland Trading  was nicer than as described and easy to care for!

product_title: Cajun Foreplay Dry Spice Rub- Case of 12 5.5oz. Net weight bottles
review_headline: great stuff without all the salt
review_body: Had this at dinosaur bbq in Rochester and loved it.  I have tried a LOT of Cajun seasonings and this is the one for me. product_title: Viva Naturals #1 Organic Maca Powder, Gelatinized for Enhanced Bioavailability, Non-GMO
review_body:
I take various superfood claims with a grain of salt. I want to feel an effect or see a difference health wise before rushing to judgement. I had heard about the supposed benefits of Maca powder (this is the gelatinized-more digestible-form) and was curious to try it. I'm glad I did.

After incorporating Maca into my morning smoothie -- which could include yogurt, fruit, chia, honey and various supplement powders and proteins, etc. -- I was sleeping more deeply, feeling better, and my general mood had improved overall. I tend to overthink things and sometimes can be easily annoyed, but the longer I have been ingesting Maca, the more mellow and go with the flow I feel. That isn't to say I walk around in a blissed out stupor. Not even close. I feel more alert and aware than ever. The little things just don't bother me as much. Health wise, I feel good physically and have lost a few pounds in the weeks I've been using Maca.

I won't say it was love at first taste though. In fact, I cringed the first time I had some. After reducing the portion slightly in my smoothie and over time, I've come to tolerate and even like the flavor. It is something like a nutty, over-ripe potato. Very hard to describe. Anyway, I don't mind it now, especially when mixed with ingredients that compliment or mask it.

Along with other Viva Labs products, this is certified organic and GMO free. Viva has yet to let me down with their nutritional supplements. I did receive a sample from them for objective review purposes. Don't let the sparse nutritional list fool you, Maca *IS* a superfood.

~ Kort

product_title: Victoria Marinara Sauce, 24 Ounce
review_headline: No sugar, No Tomato Paste and it tastes freshly made.
review_body:
Though I generally review entertainment products on Amazon I do occasionally review food products and after returned from the Fancy Food Show in New York , this product was sent to me by the manufacturer for an objective review. I had a chance to try it there but I wanted to try it at home.

The first thing I look for on a jarred pasta sauce (no, I don’t make my own “gravy”), is the list of ingredients to see where both sugar and tomato paste rank on the list of ingredients. Victoria puts the list on the front of the jar and in print size you can read easily.

Here are the ingredients for the Marinara Sauce: 100% Italian tomatoes, Imported Olive Oil, Fresh Garlic, Basil and Onions, Sea Salt & Spices. What? No tomato paste? No sugar? No chemical names? Nope! I still have issues with companies that list the word “spices” without identifying them (I’m guessing there is some oregano here and maybe some pepper but it is not what we call “spicy”) but this sauce tastes freshly made with no chemical or tomato paste taste.

It’s pricier than Barilla and other major brands but in line with premium ones like Rao’s. But I like this one better. I plan to try their vodka sauce too.

The 24 oz jar is about 50% larger than most jarred sauces so it’ll last longer and I used it one night over pasta and the next night over pan fried breaded pork chops. Both times it tasty and I can certainly recommend it.

It is listed a number of times on Amazon and the price ranges greatly. So look for feedback on seller when you compare the price.
I hope you found this review both informative and helpful.

Steve Ramm
“Anything Phonographic”

Boy was I wrong, my daughter can't get enough of just apple, raspberry, & avacado and I feel confident giving it to my babies. Beech-Nut Organics ingredients are exactly what they say they are, and even better they don't add excess water to their food. They use a gentle cooking process to protect the nutrients inside.
This is a huge help and saves me from stressing or worrying when we are getting ready to travel to visit family, and takes one thing off my long list of things to do, I know my babies are getting the best and they absolutely love it.

product_title: Zoe Organic Extra Virgin Olive Oil, 2.5 Liters
review_body:
The oil lacks flavor.

That could be because my can arrived unsealed as flavor can weaken with oxidation. When I contacted the seller to find out if this was normal, I was told that they didn't have such info. I was then given the option of a free return and the contact info of the manufacturer/importer/wholesaler/whatever, so I could get an answer to my original, simple Q.

Personally, I think it makes sense to expect the seller to both be able to answer such Qs and to contact their sources when more basic info is asked for. (Ever try to contact one of these concerns? It's usually neither easy nor pleasant.)

As for the oil:

Two local Costcos carry an organic from Crete for about the same $/volume. And that product comes in 750ml glass bottles and has tons of grassy, earthy flavor.

I love to know if others received an unsealed can before I pass final judgement on this oil.

product_title: Carolina Gold Nuggets Microwave Pork Puffies Bake-N-Puffs, Original, 16 Ounce
review_headline: Totally Frustrating Product - Does Not Pop Well!
review_body:
I was so disappointed in these. I eat Paleo, so crispy snacks are hard to come by, lol. I thought these would be such a great idea - easy storage and you only have to make as many as you want, and probably all of the reasons you are thinking of right now...

I have tried popping these in all kinds of microwaves, and they don't pop up well. And a lot of the ones that do pop don't pop all the way, so the edges are really hard. And when I say hard, I don't mean extra crunchy like an overcooked French fry or chips do edible, I mean extra hard like a popcorn kernel - you literally cannot bite through those parts and it hurts your teeth when you accidentally chomp down on a hard bit.

If you follow the directions - put 1/4 c. on a paper plate - and put it in the micro - barely any pop. I've tried all kinds of times and all kinds of microwaves. When you put a 1/4 cup on a paper plate, they naturally spread out on the plate. So, I tried making sure they were all spread out - barely any popped. By trial and error, I have finally started to make a pile in the middle of the plate so that they are all touching - more of them actually pop this way - but there are still very few perfectly edible ones without the too hard edges. Which is very disappointing - the popped ones taste great and are warm like popcorn.

I WISH the Lawry's microwaveable didn't have bad stuff in them - I'm curious how they pop up for comparison. But if you eat Paleo, my suggestion would be to stick with bags or barrels of already cooked pork rinds. This product will drive you mad with frustration.

I had issues where I would buy yogurt the store only for them to expire, I had to look for more money saving way to eat yogurt. My friend introduced me to these freeze-dried cultures and I was hooked! Follow the directions to the T and it is easy to make yogurt on your own. product_title: Live Organic Milk Kefir Grains by Lifetime Kefir
review_headline: not a TBS but probably more like a teaspoon amount
review_body: The batch I recieved was very small, not a TBS but probably more like a teaspoon amount.  They took a while to get to my front door and then was delievered to my hot metal mail box.  It was a few hours until I was able to retrieve them and put them in some milk to feed them. I worried about them being so small and sorta dry gummy like.  They did not come in a plastic bag that most have said, mine came in this small tub with a lid and that was in a cheese cloth sack that I did use for the first day to vent the top of the bottle, but later I decided on using a coffee filter instead.  The insturction sheet that you get with this order is great and even after 3 days I had nothing and worried about them being dead. on day three milk change I really thought they were dead, I coulnd even see them.  I would suggest probbly not doing a full cup of milk. But on day 4 I was rewarded with curds and way. so exciting!  I am very happy with this product and just stick with it and they will produce your kefir.

product_title: Coke Life Reduced Calorie Coca Cola with Stevia 12 Oz Cans - Case of 24
review_body:
It doesn't contain artificial sweeteners (linked to cancer), nor is it so sweetened with high fructose corn syrup that it can cause long term damage to your liver or kidneys like regular Coke could. It's reduced calorie, and sweetened partially with Stevia (a potentially "safer" sugar substitute). However unlike Diet Coke, it tastes just as good as regular coke.

The health risks of regular Coca Cola and Diet Coke are believed to be rare unless consumed in obscene amounts, but among the varieties of Coke, this product is seen as the "safe" cola.

review_headline: Like to chew your cud when you graze? Like it tough like leather.. Look elsewhere! Perfect balance of size, moisture, texture..
review_body:
MY FAVORITE BEEF JERKY... I HAVE FINALLY FOUND YOU AGAIN... LOL I like this Old Fashioned version, and the Peppered version in the black bag. It's a little more on the "moist and chewy" versions of beef jerky without being too thick for my taste like the thicker versions of "Jack Links" for example. Eating "Jack's" gives me a headache.. Just a little to chewy. This tastes like "beef" and not some unknown substance (unlike some other brands) but keep the bag sealed when not in use... Otherwise, you have dried out leather that's been left out in the sun and rain. If that's the kind you prefer, you can have your "dried out tough" version in a day or two.. Just leave it in the car with the bag wide open. I am still amazed at other brands of Jerky that taste like dried leather straight out of the bag that are still in business.. This one is different! Maybe a little closer to the old Pemmican style but has larger cuts of meat. It's on the average size of "thinness," not to tuff, not to chewy, so you won't feel like a cow chewing their cud while you enjoy this wonderful treat.. ;)
If you live with a crew that won't seal the bag.. You might want to divide this awesome treat up into smaller portions so less of this "treasure" is at risk for going to waste.. This is a picture of what it looks like so you have a better idea of what you are getting. This is not the entire bag contents.. Hope this picture helps you decide if this is the type you like or not..

It was still very good but it was almost like a dense German bread or an Ezekiel bread. If you like that kind of texture then this is definitely the flour for you but I like my bread soft and pliable and hence from now onwards I will definitely mix this flour with another flour to make my bread. I prefer the molino grassi organic flour if you're looking for an Italian flour that makes delicious bread that doesn't give you a gluten/wheat reaction. review_headline: Good crunch and flavor. Pretty mild on the heat.
review_body:
They're really some of the better dried peas I've had. I don't like wasabi and a lot of the Japanese peas have some wasabi flavoring.

I was expecting hotter, but the mild heat is really about right. Just a tiny bit of heat that doesn't hit till the end and barely lingers. I do suggest they change the fire as the main symbol and the "ablaze with taste and goodness". I guess it might say that because maybe they use fire to dry them out? Because it made me think they'd be really hot/spicy. I was disappointed with my first bite but when I went back to them later with my expectations in check I was really happy.

While not spicy/hot they do taste spicy--as in spices. Paprika and chilli are listed on the label. Without knowing what I'm talking about I think they have a slight Indian flavor. A lot of dried peas have a very Asian flavor, where these slide toward the Indian.

The quality of the peas is very nice. When I've bought dried peas in the Asian section, there are often peas that are too crunchy. These are all the right amount of crunch. I liked them a lot and will buy again!

Now onto the sugar. I bought the vanilla rock sugar last time, and I loved it but wanted to try something new.

I have chocolate tea so grabbed this.

I def like it better than the rock sugar. It melts easier. It's sweeter for sure. If you're going for subtle go with the rock sugar. You CAN taste the chocolate cream but it's not overwhelming at all. It's just enough.

product_title: Frontier Psyllium Husk Powder, 16 Ounce Bag
review_headline: Not what is shown in photo.  This product is some kind of overseas-origin, inedible garbage! STAY AWAY!!!
review_body: The granular product shown in the photo is false.  This is a fine powdered material of unknown origin.  No country of origin on label (an FDA/FTC requirement to be legally sold in the USA if of non-USA origin).  Although all psyllium I have ever come across comes from India.  I suspect it may even be something packaged in China, based upon the package.  I have many years experience importing products from China and I am familiar with the typical Chinese packaging.  This is like no  no psyllium husk powder I have every used. It gels up very quickly in water as if it were guar gum or some other hydrocolloid into a thick gelatinous messs that is, in my opinion, completely inedible.  I have been in the natural products industry for more than 30 years, purchasing and distributing natural ingredients.  This product smells like chemicals when you open the bag.  I suspect this is some kind of chemical treatment used to sterilize it because it may have originally had high microbiological plate counts.  The product is a fine powder, unlike the photo in the advertisement appears to represent on Amazon.  It says it is not returnable.  I made a strong appeal to Amazon, since it said it is not returnable and Amazon refunded my purchase price and told me I could just discard this material, which I did.  Kudos to Amazon!  I understand they cannot  know what every product they sell is, so this is what customer service is all about.  Thumbs up Amazon, thumbs down Frontier.

product_title: Stokes Green Chile Sauce with Pork, 15 Ounce
review_headline: NEW MEXICO is the green chile capital!
review_body:
All the reviews on here talk about Colorado green chile. Uhm... HELLO! NEW MEXICO! Hatch, NM is the green chile capital of the world. We put green chile on EVERYTHING! Burgers, pizza, grilled cheese, macaroni and cheese. (Colorado? Pfff). Come to the Hatch Valley anytime in August for the harvest and see and smell the wonderful aroma of green roasting green chile. Buy fresh chile in 30lb bags or get your chile roasted in a tumble roaster where you buy it.

This is pretty decent chile sauce as chile sauces go. It's not the greatest, but with the addition of pork pieces, it has that extra bonus of being more than just chile, garlic, and flour. This company is based in Colorado - which probably explains why it is readily available in the Colorado market and little elsewhere. I've seen it in New Mexico stores a few times, but stores are pretty well stocked in NM with a few dozen brands from NM. The best chile sauce is "505" brand. Also available for a ridiculous price on Amazon. You may also want to check out Cannon's Sweet Hots, which isn't a sauce so much as a condiment - good for dips and chile con queso. Both products are picked in nearby Hatch, roasted and packaged in Las Cruces, NM which is in the Hatch Valley. The best place to get these is straight from the source... Google The Truck Farm in Las Cruces, NM. But yes, the Stokes is decent stuff and good for smothering burritos, etc. Or as the base for a dip. Add a little meat (the pieces in the can are minimal) for some chile stew.

FYI, for chile enthusiasts and others out of the "know", "Hatch" chile is not a type of chile. It is a brand name of chile that is either grown in New Mexico or Georgia. It is also the location where the best and most abundant chile is grown, Hatch, NM. Most people refer to the chile grown in Hatch as "Hatch Chile" because it is the preferred source location due to the climate and optimum growth factors both for flavor and "heat". "Chile" and "chili" are two different things. "Chile" is the fruit (and yes, it is a fruit) itself, and "chili" is actually a Texan concoction made with tomato sauce, red chile powder, cumin, beans, and meat. In the Southwest, this is often referred to as brown chili, to distinguish it from a red chile stew with meat (aka chile con carne) or green chile stew with meat (aka chile verde).

http://www.amazon.com/505-Southwestern-Variety-Roasted-Chipotle/dp/B00KRV4PY4/ref=sr_1_4?ie=UTF8&qid=1439968212&sr=8-4&keywords=505+chile

Note: The brand name "505" derives from the area code that used to encompass all of New Mexico. When the company first started, this area code included Las Cruces and Hatch. Since then, the area code has changed for the entire state, with the exception of Albuquerque and Santa Fe, which maintains the 505 number (Breaking Bad fans will be familiar with "the 505").34;. I ate anywhere from 5-10 a day and for a week, was so sick, and on the toilet the entire time with diarrhea and stomach pains I didn't know what the issue was until I put two and two together and did some on line research, and once I stopped eating them in large quantities, (I'm actually afraid to even have one now) all the stomach pain, severe bloating and major diarrhea all stopped!  Enjoy one or two, but stop there!  They are yummy and additive so I ate more then a couple each day. Yikes! The back of the package will actually says "EXCESS CONSUMPTION MAY HAVE A LAXATIVE EFFECT".  I didn't see this until after my research of being sick for an entire week!

product_title: Vosges Matcha Green Tea Dark Chocolate Bar 3oz (6-pack)
review_body:
Vosges Matcha Green Tea Dark Chocolate Bar 3oz (85g) (6-pack), from Vosges.
Note: This is a product review only, and not an endorsement of the vendor.
Vosges IP seems to have a penchant for off-beat chocolate combinations, and this one is no exception. Other combinations include bacon, coconut ash, coconut charcoal, pink salt, black salt, banana, smoke and stout. What next? This particular dark chocolate is aimed at the health food crowd, and is slightly bitter, getting close to unsweetened baking chocolate, and though it contains matcha tea powder, there is no green color, so the amount of matcha is minimal. It also contains spirulina powder. Spirulina is a blue-green algae form of protein, but it can easily become contaminated with microcystins that can cause intestinal problems and liver cancer. Spirulina may also contain lead, mercury and arsenic, so it might be a better idea to get your protein from other sources, like animal (meat) and plant (beans). See the Spirulina web entry on WikipediA: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spirulina_(dietary_supplement). Four Amazon stars for this chocolate bar.

review_body:
Tastes great, organic, low sodium, and gluten-free.

Here's a recipe for Turkey And Zucchini Quinoa Pasta Casserole With Mozzarella:

Turkey And Zucchini Quinoa Pasta Casserole With Mozzarella
Ingredients for 5 servings
~32 oz lean ground turkey (93%)
8 oz (1 box) quinoa pasta (shells)
5/8 cup shredded mozzarella (1/8 cup on each)
1/2 red onion (chopped)
2 cups zucchini (chopped)
1 bell pepper (chopped)
2 tomatoes
1 cup chicken broth
Two 6 oz can tomato paste

Directions
1. Set oven to 375 degrees F.
2. Boil quinoa pasta, rinse with warm water, and set aside.
3. Set a skillet on medium-high heat and lightly spray with coconut oil. Toss in red onion
and granulated or freshly minced garlic. Cook in the skillet until the onions are nearly
translucent.
4. Add in the ground turkey meat. Stir and cook with a wooden spoon or spatula. Chop
the turkey and separate it as it cooks.
5. Once the turkey is nearly finished cooking, add zucchini, tomato paste, tomatoes, bell
peppers, and one cup chicken broth.
6. Stir slowly and reduce heat slightly. Cover and cook until the mixture begins to
simmer.
*Tip: If your skillet is large enough, add in cooked pasta. If not, continue on to
step 7.
7. Put the pasta in a pyrex baking dish. Pour the mixture on top of the pasta.
8. Top with mozarella
9. Bake in the oven for 20 minutes at 375F.
10. Eat and enjoy!!!

Nutrition Facts
Serving size: 1 serving
Recipe yields: 5 servings
Calories 475
Total Fat: 14 g
Total Carbs: 49 g
Protein: 42 g

product_title: Zevia Natural Zero Calorie Black Cherry 6 cans X 12 Fl. Oz
review_headline: Black Cherry Zevia is a great no-caffeine, zero calorie soda!
review_body:
I really like the Zevia brand sodas and this is my favorite flavor. I wanted a soda made with natural ingredients, and WITHOUT aspartame, so I went looking in natural food stores and found Zevia! Zevia sodas are made with Stevia (a plant) instead of artificial sweeteners. I found them a few years ago and have tried many of the different flavors. The Stevia flavored drinks have a different kind of sweet flavor than sugar or artificial sweeteners, so you might need to have a few to get used to the flavor, but I liked it right away and was thrilled to have an alternative to the mainstream sodas for a zero calorie drink. It costs more than your typical aspartame sweetened soda, but it's far cheaper than coffee at the local coffee shop!

The black cherry flavor does not have caffeine. Some of the other flavors do have caffeine, like the cola and the Dr. Zevia flavors.

I can't seem to live without this soda :) It's my drink of choice!

product_title: Quaker Chewy Granola Bar
review_headline: Current Nutrition Facts And Ingredients........... See Photos~
review_body:
Since my husband and I love our Old Fashioned Quaker Oats and are constantly trying new snack bars, I was stoked when I found these bars with a coupon running... I went ahead and added them to our Subscribe and Save order for the additional savings and thought these were a steal! A total of 48 bars for under $7, not bad at all...

I didn't pay too much attention to the reviews or nutrition facts and ingredients, since they were such a steal. I'm uploading some photos to include all of the current info, along with an idea of just how tiny these bars are. Essentially they are two to three bites each.

While they are just enough to satify a sweet tooth (and between the chocolate and peanut butter, of course taste wonderful!), they really pack a lot of combined sugars and carbs.

Although they are free of High Fructose Corn Syrup, if you were to eat two of them, making them more the size of most protein type bars, you'd have 44 effective carbs. And still you would be eating under 1.7 oz, which really isn't a whole lot.

The ingredient list being so lengthy, I don't think these are non GMO. They do not contain some of the ingredients other prior reviews mentioned so it seems the facts have been updated. They are quite delicious little snacks but I still would not choose them for being the healthiest of choices.

You can see in the photos that the box is nicely designed, with perforation for easy access to bars from the pantry. Even considering the value to be more like 24 bars, in my opinion, these are still a great value with the current pricing.

I rated these three stars, primarily because of their size and nutrition facts. Based soley on flavor, I'd give them five stars and would say they are worth trying if you're not super carb conscious or gluten sensitive. Smile. Hope this is helpful!

product_title: Sterling 21016 1/2 Pint Aqua Glaze Water Base Glazing Compound
review_body:
The product is very good but the picture is misleading!
As of the time of this review, the photo on Amazon is misleading. I will first deal with the product itself:
Aqua Glaze is a very difficult product to find. It is fairly unique in a market dominated by DAP glazing compound which is an oil base product. Aqua Glaze is water based as the name implies. There are advantages on both sides of the fence, but Aqua Glaze is a much easier product to work with. It goes on a lot more smoothly, and can be smoothed out with a wet putty knife. One obvious advantage is that it can be painted about an hour after application. Dap suggests 3 weeks or more., When you are painting, most painters don't have that long to wait. Dap stays flexible while Aqua Glaze hardens up. Agua Glaze is better suited to wood sash windows than metal. They recommend priming metal before you apply it to a metal sash. I used it on wood.. I was happy with the results (so far). I painted the frame about an hour later. Cleanup of the putty knife is a breeze since its water based.
I found this product because I was looking for a product similar to Elmers Glaze Tuff which I had used many years ago. I REALLY liked it. This is quite similar. I will need to see how it holds up over time. The "Elmers" held up pretty well.
The real problem with Glaze Tuff is THE COST!. This leads up to the deceptive photo.
The cost for a HALF PINT of Aqua Glaze is $7.49. The cost for the same amount of Dap is under $4.00 in a regular hardware store. When you add the cost of shipping, Glaze Tuff is over $13.00. This is FAR too expensive to make it practical. I glazed two 24x24 windows (one double hung) and did a touch up on a few others. There is not enough product left to do any more than a few more touch ups. So a half pint doesn't go very far.
Now for the photo:
FIRST I am embarrassed to say that I really didn't read as well as I should have. There are only a few places to buy this product. It seems that the company called "Life and Home" is the distributor on a few sites. They use the same photo as well. The photo is a picture of a METAL can of Aqua Glaze and is labeled ONE QT. Of course I have already established that this is NOT One Quart, its a HALF PINT. BIG DIFFERENCE! The photo doesn't match the description. They don't sell a quart size HERE on Amazon. A quart size elsewhere, costs about $13.00 + shipping. You can order any available size from the Life & Home web site and the shipping may be less depending on location.
In any event, the metal can no longer exists anyway, regardless of the half pint or quart size. The picture is obsolete. The product you will get comes in a plastic tub with a tight fitting snap on lid. It will be marked STERLING Aqua Glaze. Sterling is a division of Savogran.
I actually verified all of this by calling the toll free number on the tub. Most of us are pretty much limited to buying this product from Amazon or Life and Home or another popular auction site.
Again, it is too expensive to be practical, but if you really want it, you are better off buying a QUART directly from Life and Home. Most of us can't use a gallon of the product, which is also available. The stuff would probably harden before we would ever use that much.
Professional painters, however, may have another option. Savogran said that the product can be ordered through Sherwin Williams stores. HOWEVER, you MUST order it by the CASE. Again only professional painters and contractors would need that much product.
Its really too bad that hardware chains like Ace or True Value dont offer this as an alternative to Dap.

P.S.I will try to modify my review is and when the picture problem is fixed. Until then, be aware of what you are ordering and what you will get for your money.

product_title: Nutiva Hi Fiber Hemp Protein Powder - 16 OZ
review_headline: A review of taste and usability.
review_body:
So I legit just got this product yesterday, and I was very excited to try it out. After trying it, I was compelled enough to write a review for it.

The taste on it's own is vegetal and sandy, neither are to be unexpected. It's subtle, though, sort of earthy. I was expecting it to taste far more "green," something like lawn clippings or gnawing on some hedge leaves, but it's not, really.

But there's never going to be a point where I just slam this stuff in a glass of water and drink it, so even if you're not keen on "subtle earthy" flavors, I wouldn't worry about it if you're doing what most of us are doing: making smoothies and shakes.

Now, for me, I tend to buy fruit in bulk, clean, chop, and freeze it into individual "smoothie packs." I prefer this method because I tend to drink a smoothie in the morning before my eyes have fully opened, because I'm not enough of a morning person to cook myself breakfast. So, this morning, into my bullet blender went one tablespoon (yes, I know a serving is 3 tablespoons, but for a trial run, who would ever use THAT much?) of Hemp fiber/protein powder, a smoothie pack of banana, mango, strawberry, and a heaping handful of spinach. Dropped in 1.5 tablespoons of plain, unsweetened, boring-old yogurt (for more protein and some additional "creamy" texture), some filtered water, and a bit of stevia because these last few smoothie packs didn't seem so sweet and Blended for about 10 seconds, opened it up and was greeted by...

...something that looked like decaying organic matter. You ever leave a bag of spinach in your crisper drawer and forget about it? You know that green/brown slime it turns into? That's what this looks like.

I smelled it, however, and it smelled tropical. Like my smoothie pack normally does.

I sipped it. It was velvety. It was good. It didn't really blend all that well, but I didn't expect it to. It hung in suspension with the liquid, and went down easily. Kind of had a velvety mouth-feel that wasn't what I'd call "normal" but totally unoffensive.

So all in all, it works well in smoothies, and I imagine if you're not opposed to it turning whatever you're blending it with some sort of deep greenish-brownish color, you could probably mix it into quite a bit of stuff. I don't think that it'd be good in anything that wouldn't be thicker - smoothies and shakes are probably this stuffs wheelhouse.

All in all, it's not bad. I like it well enough and will probably buy it again.ith the liquid, and went down easily. Kind of had a velvety mouth-feel that wasn't what I'd call "normal" but totally unoffensive.

So all in all, it works well in smoothies, and I imagine if you're not opposed to it turning whatever you're blending it with some sort of deep greenish-brownish color, you could probably mix it into quite a bit of stuff. I don't think that it'd be good in anything that wouldn't be thicker - smoothies and shakes are probably this stuffs wheelhouse.

All in all, it's not bad. I like it well enough and will probably buy it again.

product_title: 5LBS Colombia Excelso Decaf Unroasted Green Coffee Beans
review_headline: Nothing Like a Great Cup of Coffee!
review_body: There's no better way to get a really good cup of coffee than to roast it yourself.  This Colombia bean by Bodhi Leaf is among the best I have found.  Colombian is great by itself and the perfect anchor for blends. I initially used a small roasting machine.  When it broke, I could not find the necessary part to fix it.  Having become a coffee snob, I purchased a second 'improved' roaster; it also broke and still could not find parts from any source to fix that one.  I next used a popcorn popping pot, the kind with the handle to turn to keep the beans in motion.  Because it was difficult to keep the popper from sliding around my ceramic cooktop, I decided that the right roasting vessel is a Lodge cast iron dutch oven.  The weight of the pan keeps it in place while I keep the beans in motion using a whisk. The sides of the dutch oven are deep enough to keep all but the most vigorous bean from escaping during the cracks.

product_title: Peppermint Puffs - 4.0 Oz
review_body:
Red Bird Soft Peppermint Puffs. Individually wrapped. I love peppermint; I love the flavor and the softness of this particular peppermint candy. They just melt in your mouth; they taste so good. They are good at any time, but especially after a big meal (or after any meal), they are a nice finishing touch.

And I love this particular type of soft peppermints, because these do not have any corn syrup.

Peppermints are awesome as after-dinner candy, or anytime candy. But they also go beyond just plain ole candy: The Christmas when my oldest daughter was a senior in high school, she made a peppermint chocolate cake, with crushed peppermints. That was the most delicious cake I'd ever tasted in all my life. It had the chocolatey and pepperminty tastes, and it was just delectable. It was delicious and perfect. I know she is the best baker ever! She went away to college after that, and then got busy with her own life, so I haven't had another one of her peppermint chocolate cakes. I think I will make one this coming Christmas, even though mine won't be as good as hers. I love peppermint!

product_title: Darjeeling Oolong Singbulli Black Tea
review_headline: like flowers on the mountain air
review_body:
Fluffy, light, and soft, like flowers on the mountain air. There is a loud floral overtone, even fruity. It tastes of aromatic perfume, with an undertone of dark wood, reaching down into the soil for a hint of spices that lingers on after the fruit-flowery finish. The most lovely amber hue, it shines brightly in glass. Similarly, the leaves are mostly unbroken and have a pleasing, autumnal shade to them in glass.

True to form, this Wu long tea must be brewed at Wu long temperature. Heat will immediately bring out an overwhelming astringent mouthfeel and flowery taste. So, this delicate tea requires a delicate heat. Not boiled.

Unusually, I brew this tea in a teapot, rather than a gaiwan, and for about 3 minutes. Twice, or three times. Longer steeping seems to favor the spicy tones. The normal amount of leaf is sufficient. Altogether a worthwhile and elegant tea.

product_title: Uddo's Kitchen Red Lentil Penne Pasta, 36 Ounce
review_body:
Uddo's Red Lentil Pasta (penne) is probably intended for the gluten-free consumer. We do enjoy wheat pasta, though, so I will compare to my usual product, Garofalo whole wheat pasta. The first thing I noticed was a lovely deep-orange color, which makes a very attractive salad. I used it instead of bread in a panzanella recipe. The contrast against the basil and tomatoes was beautiful. Next, I checked the nutrient labels on both products. The whole wheat pasta has twice as much dietary fiber, 4 grams/serving, vs. 2 grams/serving for the lentil pasta. The protein content of the lentil pasta is about twice as high as that of the wheat pasta - 14 grams vs. 8 grams/serving. The comparisons of the rest of the nutrients required to be listed, and the calorie count, are nearly identical per serving.

In addition to the salad, I cooked one of the 12 ounce bags in the box using a "cook-in-sauce" method. I made a sauce using extra water, then cooked the pasta right in the sauce in a covered pan. At sea level, the lentil pasta takes a couple of minutes more than whole wheat pasta, whether cooked in sauce or in a pot of water. The texture of the two pastas was quite similar, with the lentil product seeming a little more chewy and "rougher" than whole wheat pasta.

Uddo's Red Lentil Pasta is a fine alternative to wheat pasta, much better than rice pasta, which I have found to be inedible.

product_title: X-Mode Energy Shots on Tap
review_body:
I am loving these refillable power shots! I've been using these for about a month now.

As someone who has been taking expensive 5-hour energies throughout college and my full time job (software dev) for 6 years, I can say this stuff really does give you just as effective of a boost as they did. I usually take 2 bottles a day, one in the early morning and one after lunch. Haven't had much for crashes, just like 5-hour energy. They recommend no more than 3oz a day (a bottle and a half), but being so used to energy shots, I felt it was necessary to take 2 full bottles a day.

I was skeptical of the box and faucet at first, but it is very decent quality and holds up just fine in my fridge. There are no leaks (as far as I can tell). I don't feel other peripherals are necessary for this product. The box and 2 bottles you get with it are all you need. They did include a coupon for a free xmode shot glass with a purchase of another box. I guess I'll probably get that, but it's not really needed.

All in all, I am satisfied with the product. It gets me up in the morning and through the slowdown at lunchtime. Great purchase and I highly recommend it!

product_title: Shirakiku Miso Aka (red) Soy Bean Paste, 35.2-Ounce Bags (Pack of 2)
review_headline: Tasty and plentiful, if a little high on the sodium content.
review_body:
The local grocery stores only seem to carry either the dehydrated miso packs, or little 8 ounce bottles of paste for about $6 a piece. So Amazon has been my go to for miso purchases. I've tried three different kinds, and this brand is so far the only one I've been completely happy with.

The packaging is convenient, and does not seem like it wants to break or explode because I looked at it funny. There's two bags of it, so that's about 4 and a half pounds of miso for $16. That's enough miso for months. And that represents the cheapest price per pound that I've found by quite a lot.

So for the actual taste of the miso, it's got a rather strong flavor. Which is fine with me, because I do like the slightly stronger flavor over white miso. Plus you can always dilute the paste to your liking. It's been a great side dish for fish and pork dishes. Only issue I can think of is it's a bit saltier than the other brands of miso that I've tried. I don't mind, but it could be an issue for other people. It's probably not the brand of paste for people with lighter palettes. Also if you are too used to white miso, maybe try some red miso before committing to purchasing this.

This miso is quite cheap, but it's also quite good. Of the brands I've tried that are for sale on Amazon, it's been the best overall. And as such it's my highest recommendation.

product_title: Italo's Gourmet Fresh Basil Pasta Sauce 6 Pack. 100% Natural, One Of The Best Tasting Traditional Southern Italian Sauces Available For The Discriminating Pallet.
review_headline: AMAZING! real Italian sauce, FRESH and So GOOD.
review_body:
As an Italian American who LOVES good pasta and is SERIOUS about food, (worked in some of the best NY restaurants) I can truly say, that I have finally found the sauce for me!
I'm so excited I just sent an email to all my friends and family. They have to taste this sauce.
I was born into a family that made sauce, pasta and pastries :) from scratch (both in Italy and when we came to the US) I know Good Pasta Sauce! Many moons ago when Nona made her sauce and pasta fresh, it had a wonderful aroma and texture that let you know it was fresh and natural. When you tasted her sauce you knew that it was made with pride and love:) I still remember the taste, the texture, the soft but OHH so good aroma of her Sauce. Nona is no longer with us. And her sauce is a long and distant memory.
Today I tasted my much awaited "Italo's Gourmet Fresh Basil Pasta Sauce 6 Pack"
At first sight when you looked at the tomato Sauce it looked good to me. It did not look bought. It looked like Nona made it :) I'm serious! It had that fresh just made look and feel, way before I even opened the jar. I could not wait to make pasta tonight. When I opened the Sauce and got the first hint of the scent, I knew that this was special! OH MY GOD. I could not believe it. It truly looked, smelled and felt fresh. It took me back to the days in Italy when I was a little guy watching my Nona make the Sauce. Every bite was special to me today. Hence my excitement.
Italo's sauce is AMAZING! real Italian sauce, FRESH, and So GOOD. The tomato was fresh, the ingredients had real flavor. REAL FLAVOR. I mean it. Not like today's Jars with goop and rushed garbage that tastes all the same. This is great Sauce. Just like the label." Ingredients, Tomatoes, fresh Onions, pure olive oil, fresh basil, fresh garlic, sea salt, pepper, secret spices."
I have no idea why I did not know about this sauce before. Thank you Amazon, this is the best!
The one complaint I have is that it was not available on Prime.

product_title: Koffee Kult Coffee Beans Medium Roasted - Highest Quality Delicious Coffee - Whole Bean and Ground Coffee - Fresh Roasted Gourmet Aromatic Artisan Blend É
review_body:
Some might actually hate me, and I apologize to Koffee Kult before hand if I've ruined their fine blend. Without a doubt, this is a fine, fine coffee, I have drunk it on its own, black, no sugar, no milk and it's very good. However I've recently discovered, to my horror.... I'm a bean mixer. Don't hate me. Ok so regardless if it's just marketing, or that I'm a Hawaii resident but I loves me some Kona coffee, except drinking 100% Kona coffee everyday makes for some expensive coffee drinking. So just like everyone else I've been resorting to preground 10% mixes, which aren't usually that good tasting (that's why they resort to adding mac nut flavors, hazelnut flavors, vanilla flavors). Thus began my quest to roll my own Kona mix, and the first thing was to find a base coffee that I like. I'm not really a coffee aficionado, so I don't know how to describe why I prefer one coffee over another, (if you want a body, flavor, oilyiness, tastoid review others have made better reviews) but as that old saying goes: "I know it when I see (taste) it" with Koffee Kult I definitely taste it.

Here is how I prepare my daily brews, I put in 4 to 5 grams of Kona beans and fill the rest with Koffee Kult beans to get 22 grams, I then grind the whole thing and in an aeropress aerobie using the upside down method; put water into the level 4 mark; let it soak for at least a minute before pressing (though more often than not it soaks for however long it takes me to complete my morning simpsons tapped out ritual). After that badaboom good cup of morning coffee. Koffee Kult on its own is very good, very good, and I do occasionally drink it on its own, however adding that little bit of Kona makes it... well it makes me feel like I'm not drinking (the excreting) gold every morning while still enjoying a very, very good cup of coffee.

Koffee Kult's customer service is great, before I had even received the beans, they were already emailing me, I asked for aeropress tips they sent me a PDF with a whole bunch of different brewing methods. Note though, they send (and I'm paraphrasing/summarizing) a "thanks for the order email," a "hope you received it" email, a "if you've had a chance to try it please review it" email etc. Though unlike other vendors, who ask you to give a 5 star rating all they asked for was a review, and I'm quite happy to do that (see length of this one as proof), however if you don't like that amount of touchy feelyness, it could seem spammy. Now I'll go back to enjoying my morning coffee.DF with a whole bunch of different brewing methods. Note though, they send (and I'm paraphrasing/summarizing) a "thanks for the order email," a "hope you received it" email, a "if you've had a chance to try it please review it" email etc. Though unlike other vendors, who ask you to give a 5 star rating all they asked for was a review, and I'm quite happy to do that (see length of this one as proof), however if you don't like that amount of touchy feelyness, it could seem spammy. Now I'll go back to enjoying my morning coffee.

product_title: Native Forest Coconut Milk Powder, 5.25 Ounce (Pack of 6)
review_body: It's not as "healthy" as proclaimed, it has maltodextrin,which is used as a thickener or filler because it’s relatively cheap. Foods labeled “natural” can be deceiving, and maltodextrin is no different. It’s derived from plants, but it’s highly processed. Generally, maltodextrin comes from corn, rice, or potato starch. It also can be made from wheat. The starches are cooked and then acids or enzymes are added to break it down further, before resulting in a white powder.  Healthy would be eliminating the highly processed maltodextrin and adding more coconut, or stevia.

review_body: In reality I'd like to give this a one star rating.  The reason I'm not doing that is that I am aware that I will not be able to give this product an unbiased review.  The reason I'm writing a review at all is to warn other people who might share my dislike of gooey, mucous-like textures in their consumables.  This might be a great product - I'll never know because I simply can't swallow it without gagging.  I find that I avoid using it even stirred into juice because of the memory of that gooey-niss.  I've been on a product search for a liquid vitamin/mineral supplement.  So far, Intramax has been at the top of my list while this product is at the bottom.  This is a reasonable expensive product so if you're like me and squeamish about textures, do your pocketbook a favor and look elsewhere.  I was going to add a photo but the "add a photo" button is not working for me.  THIS IS AN ADDENDUM TO MY EARLIER REVIEW.  WHILE MY EXPERIENCE OF THESE PRODUCTS HASN'T CHANGED, MY EXPERIENCE WITH THIS SELLER HAS BEEN EXCEPTIONAL.  I WAS IMMEDIATELY CONTACTED BY THE SELLER AS A RESULT OF MY REVIEW AND OFFERED A REFUND.  FURTHER, I WAS INTERVIEWED AS TO MY EXPERIENCE WITH THE PRODUCTS IN A GENUINE EFFORT TO "TWEAK" THE PRODUCTS TO MAKE THEM MORE PALATABLE TO MORE PEOPLE.  I DON'T KNOW ABOUT YOU, BUT I HAVEN'T SEEN CUSTOMER SERVICE LIKE THIS IN A LONG, LONG TIME.  THIS IS CLEARLY A COMPANY THAT CARES ABOUT IT'S CUSTOMERS AND IT'S REPUTATION.  BASED ON THIS, I'M SURE THAT THE PRODUCT PROVIDED IS ALSO AN EXCEPTIONAL ONE AND I'M TRULY SORRY THAT IT WASN'T FOR ME.

product_title: Ricochet Mints with Xylitol, Cocoa Mint, 100-Count Mints (Pack of 6)
review_headline: A SWEET TREAT THAT'S GOOD FOR YOUR TEETH...IMPOSSIBLE????
review_body:
These teeny mints have a hint of chocolate and a hint of mint. Not overpowering of either but when you put them in your mouth they immediately explode and dissolve with just a hint of the two flavors combined.

I have only tried one other - the Licorice which I love and unlike the licorice the flavor on these is not overwhelming (which is a good thing about the licorice) but the flavor is nice and you get a kind of cooling effect (maybe it's the Xylitol);all in all they're a nice little something to put
in your mouth when your sweet-tooth is a-hollering!

I do have a problem with all the Ricochet products prices. They are extremely overpriced and if I could find them elsewhere I certainly would BUT for now I'm willing to go further in debt (further and further and further) for this is a treat that I'm truly becoming addicted to.

By the way, check out Xylitol online; as in my other review this substance is used in all kinds of products (edible products) for it has many benefits beside being GOOD FOR YOUR TEETH..can you believe it.......if they weren't so damned expensive I'd buy every single kind.....my dentist will be oh so happy!!!!!

Let me know how you like them!

product_title: Fontana Sauce, 63 fl oz
review_headline: Love the sauce, hate the pump!
review_body:
This sauce is amazing! I've tried the Ghiradelli, Torani, and do-it-yourself syrups/powders and nothing comes close to this. I use about 2 pumps per cup of coffee, and drink about a cup a day. On the days that the pump won't be used, I recommend covering the pump opening with plastic food wrap to avoid the sauce from drying out and clogging the opening. This has happened several times, but it's no biggie -- I just use a sharp knife to scrape out the dried sauce.

My only problem with this is the pump. It doesn't reach the bottom of the container. For the last couple inches of syrup left, I have to pour it out. Sometimes I pour too much and white mocha overload, sometimes I pour too little and don't get my white mocha fix, and sometimes I don't pour at all because I'm in a rush to get to work. :\

product_title: Evamor Natural Alkaline Artesian Water
review_headline: Good water, cheap, delivered to your door
review_body:
I'm not a water scientist. However, I've read that it's best to have PH neutral, or slightly alkaline water. So I ordered a few 4 packs of the 64oz Evamor water bottles to see if I liked them. I've finished four bottles so far. Let's see:

The four pack of bottles arrive in a standard amazon box that's easy to open. No need to waste a bunch of packaging, these things are pretty rugged. The four bottles are tightly wrapped in relatively think plastic that makes them easy to manage. Additionally, for older or physically disabled customers, the weight of the four bottles probably won't be too much for them to manage. (contrast with a "flat" of smaller bottles that would be likely pretty heavy and hard to load into your refrigerator. The bottles themselves claim to be BPA free, which is good. The contour of the bottle makes it easy to grab and hold, even if there is condensation on the bottle itself. The wide mouth design both makes drinking quickly easy to do, as well as allows for easy refilling of the bottle if you want to reuse it. The water source is claimed as a well in Louisiana, so it's nice that this water isn't just municipal water from any old city, nor is it coming from a drought stricken environment. The water itself tastes crisp and clean. It's hard to describe the taste of a water, but if I had to, I'd say it's close to Evian tasting, in my opinion. I drink about 3L of water a day, and will be using these bottles as my post workout water. I've noticed that I don't get as much of a "bloated" feeling when I drink the Evamor after a workout. I think that has to do with the more natural PH of this water. I've noticed that the more acidic the water, the more that I feel bloated when I drink it.

In summary, I like this product a lot. I'll keep buying it regularly.

product_title: Hellmans Olive Oil Mayonnaise Style Dressing 430ml
review_headline: Less Calories Than Regular Mayo ~ Tastes Good, Too!
review_body:
As olive oil is supposed to be healthy for you, we tried this olive oil mayonnaise. Love to roast chicken and make a chicken salad with this mayonnaise and a little onion, celery and spices to eat on a sandwich or on top of a green salad. Ingredients in this mayonnaise include: water, oils (soybean oil, extra virgin olive oil), vinegar, whole eggs and egg yolks, modified corn starch, sugar, salt, lemon juice, (sorbic acid, calcium disodium edta) used to protect quality, xanthan gum, citric acid, natural flavors, oleoresin paprika, beta-carotene (color). It is gluten-free. They say they use cage-free eggs.

One tablespoon is 60 calories, 9% of your total fat, 2% cholesterol, 5% of your sodium and no sugar. If you can keep to a tablespoon or two on a sandwich or in your chicken salad or egg salad serving, it's very tasty and won't ruin a diet. I like the faint taste of the olive oil and now buy this olive oil mayonnaise exclusively. If you like the taste of olive oil, you should enjoy this mayonnaise.

As well, as a skeptic of differed brands of caffeine pleasure, I kept that very packet sitting on my kitchen counter for two weeks before giving in to actually trying it, due to the fact that I woke up late for work and didn't have time to brew me a cup......and WOW was I surprised by the rich and creamy flavor, like something you would actually order at a corner cafe. Since then, I knew that I had to add that experience to my daily morning ritual. And with the help of Amazon.com that pleasure has become a reality in my life.

product_title: Typhoo (240 Tea Bags)
review_headline: One of the Great U.K. teas!
review_body: This an excellent "olde" tea from England--it has the robust and full flavor character--and is not on the lighter side of the continuum.  My temperament with tea varies over the weeks--so maybe next time it may be PG Tips or even Yorkshire Gold (3 leaves).  Any of these three are really superior to the other teas on the American market.  If you are a tea lover, unfortunately none of the U.S. options are worth the effort.  Yes, paying a little more for British teas, (or even Irish teas), is necessary but far--far--worth the price.

product_title: Janet Craig Dragon Tree - Dracaena fragrans - 4
review_body: I received this plant about a month ago and it's doing very well.  It arrived very healthy and was well packaged for shipping.  It's happy in the pot it came in so I am letting it be for now.  No need to re-pot it.  These are terrific plants as they tolerate almost any light except bright, direct sunlight, in my experience.  It's very easy to care for and doesn't need much attention.  When the soil gets dry I fill the clay saucer I placed the pot in with water and let the roots pull up the water.  I don't water many of my plants from the top. The most important thing in caring for this plant is to not over water it.  Don't let it sit in water as root rot will set in and it will die.  Make sure the soil allows for good drainage too.

product_title: McCormicks Grill Mates Seasoning
review_headline: Jazz up your meals with a little McCormick seasoning!
review_body:
Our new grill does a great job of roasting meats with infrared heat. We like to baste the meat with different types of marinade and we add in this McCormick Brown Sugar Bourbon with olive oil and water and dissolve the seasoning and we baste it onto the meat. Sometimes we even use a little balsamic vinegar to the mix to help tenderize the meat. Afterwards we also sprinkle a little onto the meat as we grill it.
We also mix this into Panko bread crumbs and we flour chicken fingers, dip them into egg and then into the Panko bread crumbs and seasoning mix to spice them up a little. Everyone seems to like them a lot. You can even substitute crushed Cheese-It crackers as a crust to make a new flavor.

This is a good product and we like to experiment with different seasonings. This is one of our favorites.

product_title: Sun Maid Mixed Jumbo Raisins, 12-Ounce Pouches (Pack of 5)
review_body:
DECEPTIVE DESCRIPTION - Not "jumbo". I won't buy this Sun Maid product again.

PRO - They are raisins. They taste like raisins. They are "mixed" sizes and colors. Most of these raisins are bigger than the usual Sun Maid raisins.
CON - Package says "Mixed Jumbo Raisins". The raisins don't look like the jumbo raisin graphic on the label. Jumbo? There are very few "jumbo" size raisins. These are about a 50 / 50 mix of medium and small raisins, and about a 50 /50 mix of black and white raisins.

ALTERNATIVE - I usually buy the "Trader Joe Jumbo Raisin Medley" product. The Trader Joe product is excellent, and really "jumbo", significantly larger than this Sun Maid Product.

Supplement Facts

Ginseng and Eleuthero Root Extracts

Proprietary Blend of Herbs: 1,680 mg
Organic Green Tea Leaf
Organic Bilberry Leaf
Organic Hibiscus Flower
Green Tea Leaf Extract
Stevia Leaf
Organic Amla Fruit (Amalaki)

product_title: Great Lakes Gelatin, Certified Paleo Friendly, Keto Certified, Collagen Hydrolysate, Peptides, Pasture-Raised Grass-Fed, Non GMO, 16 oz, FFP
review_body:
I took this for a month and my skin started looking nice and glow-y.

However, IF you are susceptible to any herpes viruses (in my case shingles...which is from chicken pox and actually called herpes zoster...who knew?) do your research before taking this. Herpes require the amino acid Arginine to replicate and Gelatin, Chocolate, Nuts and Coconut (not coconut oil though) is very high in this amino acid.

It is basically like taking herpes candy. I had chicken pox as a kid and now at 28 I managed to get Shingles/Herpes Zoster by taking too much of this supplement without balancing it with its competing amino acid Lysine. Google it before you start dosing. (I was doing a tablespoon or two a day.) I may continue it after I recover, but in much smaller doses...I don't know. Shingles are not fun!

As for this Gelatin, I like it. I think they should warn people about over dosing, but other than that its the best tasting collagen/gelatin I have tried. Not noticeable in coffee :-)

product_title: Once Again Organic Raw Crunchy Almond Butter, 16 Ounce -- 12 per case.
review_body:
Well - first the item was "lost" (a case of almond butter??) .....But it turns out that this item is not what it appears to be. After speaking with an Amazon rep and then a Once Again rep, I've learned that this is NOT raw. In fact, it never was, according to the Once Again rep. Apparently new "guidelines" require it to be stated that, in fact, the nuts HAVE ALWAYS BEEN DRY ROASTED!!! Only fairly recently did they have to tell us this.
So - I have the "new labels" that admit this is an Almond butter made from "slightly roasted nuts" .... What you will get (and what I got) has the NEW LABEL!! Imagine that!

Well, I am disappointed that this is not a raw item. And even though Once Again did not have to tell us [all these years] that indeed the nuts were roasted (but at a VERY high temperature, so they chose to ignore this detail) ... they ought to have!!

product_title: Arrowhead Mills Gluten Free All Purpose Baking Mix, 20 Ounce (Pack of 6) - Packaging may vary
review_body:
I ran out of my favorite baking mix, XO, and was excited to try this product as a replacement. There are plenty of pictures of baked goods, and it clearly says on the box: "Use....as ONE:ONE replacement for flour in ANY of your favorite recipes. Recipe change is not needed." How false can you get?
I tried to make Martha Stewart's biscuits with milk and butter, the pastry for southern fried pies. Things seemed to go smoothly till they went into the iron skillet to fry. The pastry began to disintegrate and soaked up an inch of oil. 1/2 cup loose Crumbs fried brown on the bottom of the skillet and the pies would not hold together, even after letting them cool. I called the company, and don't even bother if you have a problem. I got a lecture on how different gluten free flours are. Really? That's not what you claim on the box. I would never try another recipe with this concoction even though Logan offered to send me some of their own recipes. Their biscuit recipe requires sour cream, oil, etc. etc. And I'M STUCK WITH THIS AWFUL MIX.

product_title: Tuscan Dairy Whole Vitamin D Milk, Gallon, 128 oz
review_headline: The worst part of the purchase was awaiting its arrival and ...
review_body:
After reading the reviews and seeing the price on this milk I decided that it must be life changing and I had to have it. The worst part of the purchase was awaiting its arrival and I missed two days of work worried with anticipation.
The sound of a diesel engine and squeaky brakes was the climax of my anticipation knowing that the FedEx truck was certainly near. Before the driver could unbuckle his seat belt I was already rummaging through the myriad of packages looking for my milk. After nearly 5 minutes of frantic searching, there it was, like a beacon in the night my Tuscan Whole Milk!
I rushed into the house shaking like a dog passing peach pits, ready to pour the nectar of the gods over a bowl of cinnamon toast crunch that had been carefully prepared the night before. Unfortunately the packaging was like trying to break into Fort Knox and I nearly wet myself with excitement. After locating a razor knife I ever so carefully made the necessary cuts to free my Tuscan Whole Milk from the restraints that imprisoned the ecstasy I had been anticipating. Finally free from its containment I removed the milk and to my utter dismay the jug was filled with a rancid chunk of clumpy, congealed, foulness that had no similarity to the dairy delight I envisioned so many nights in my dreams.
At this point I absolutely lost my cool. I ran screaming from the house literally smashing through the patio door nearly severing my finger but that would have to wait. I smashed my car window with a brick and kicked in the door like an MMA fighter would kick his opponent. At this point I guess you could say the entire neighborhood was "aware" that I was unhappy. My neighbor came over to try and diffuse the situation which didn't turn out well for him when he found himself on the receiving end of a nasty throat punch! I vaguely remembering hearing someone saying "call 911" but that was no deterrent for my anger. I took my anger out of shrubs, fences, mailboxes, small forest animals, and a stray cat or two. The flashing red and blues had no effect on my rage and soon I found myself on the receiving end of a tazer which caused my bladder and bowels to leak all over the street. After being treated for multiple cuts, bruises, contusions, and a concussion I was booked into jail and given a court date. Luckily I found another inmate that had a similar experience with Tuscan Whole Milk and he contacted his wife to place another order for the both of us. My new gallon of Tuscan Whole Milk should be just in time for my homecoming and I know that this time it will be everything I anticipate it to be.

product_title: Roasted Peaberry Bean 100% Jamaica Blue Mountain
review_headline: A truly excellent cup of coffee, but probably not worth the price.
review_body:
I don't review many products I buy on Amazon, but because this purchase was very extravagant by my standards and I bought it almost purely on speculation, I thought I should write a review so others might have a little more information than I did.

Admittedly, this is a very good cup of coffee. Just moments ago I brewed and enjoyed my first cup. It is 24 August, and the beans were roasted (medium roast) on 31 July. I used filtered water, 2 tbsp. of coffee per 6 oz. of water, and brewed the coffee in a French press. The coffee has a wonderful smell, and excellent flavor with just the right amount of acidity. Of course, I drank it black, but I always drink coffee black. If you are a coffee lover, I can recommend trying this coffee at least once in your life.

I also had the pleasure of talking to a representative - Mark - at Wallenford. I wanted to make sure I was getting coffee that was roasted recently and not sitting on Amazon's shelves somewhere for months. Mark was great, and responded very quickly to my inquiries. A total professional.

Now, the other thing I must address is the cost. I have had coffee that is comparable in taste for considerably less money. While I look forward to enjoying the rest of the bag, it probably will be the last time I order this product.

product_title: Natural Value Coconut Milk
review_headline: Actual coconut taste, but the price?
review_body:
I bought this for the first time with a ten dollar discount that makes each can roughly $2.50. I have used a few cans so far, and I must say this is the best tasting canned coconut milk I've had. It tastes like actual coconut, whereas the others I've tried have tasted disgusting. When I first opened a can, I thought that this wasn't coconut cream. It was all watery and chunky at the bottom, but once I put it in the refrigerator, it was apparent that there was coconut cream and water in it. I could find the coconut oil "hardenings," and all of the good stuff found in coconut meat!

With the fact that I paid ten dollars less for my first purchase, I don't think I'll buy this again for the full price. I think $2.50 a can is reasonable.

I would rather have too much salt so I can choose how I want to eat it than not enough.  The flavor is very good.  I also purchased some buttery topping to get the complete "theater effect".Can't afford to go the show as often as I like so this popcorn, a soda and my big screen tv help bring the movie experience alive.  I will say this 2.5 ounce size is perfect for one person.  If you want to make for more, you might want the 4 ounce size instead.

review_body:

https://www.facebook.com/CocoLibreOrganic/posts/548593005198443

What I found lumpy is best by Sep 25, 2015 which is almost expired when I received it. Those units were definitely produced before summer of 2013.

I didn't read reviews from Amazon when I buy this, because I have already had sample from a race event. I found it very tasty so I ordered it from Amazon.

product_title: Chef Piggy Tail Microwave Pork Puffies Pork Rinds, Jalapeno, 8 Ounce
review_headline: Decent for the price and the effort.
review_body: We tried the directions (90 seconds) and I think we literally had 1 rind puff up.  We knew we did not have the patience to sit in front of the microwave and add ten seconds to the cooking time (as the directions suggested) so we tried adding 30 seconds at a time (the ez function on our microwave), taking the popped ones out as they cooked.  We still really did not have much patience for that so what my husband ended up doing was covering a microwave safe plate with a paper towel and nuking the little bits for four minutes.  So far (the last 6 batches) most of the rinds have puffed up, very few are hard and I don't believe we have had any issues with burnt ones.  Actually surprisingly good for the effort put into making them (which is almost none).

product_title: So Delicious Unsweetened Cashew Milk, 32 Fluid Ounce -- 6 per case.
review_headline: Ok for latex food allergy sufferers who can tolerate cashews
review_body:
If you have a latex food allergy and you are able to eat cashews, know that So Delicious keeps this product separate from the other products it makes, like coconut milk. Almonds can kill me and coconut breaks me out in eczema, so I must avoid those at all costs. If I can drink this stuff, it must be pure. I hope that now that So Delicious has been bought out by WhiteWave (http://vegnews.com/articles/page.do?pageId=6558&catId=1), which owns Silk which is cross-contaminated, it will continue this practice of keeping the So Delicious products' production separate.

I use the vanilla cashew milk when cooking something sweet or adding to tea/coffee and the regular cashew milk when cooking something savory. It's thicker than rice milk, which is a plus.

product_title: Bear Naked Sea Salt Caramel Apple Granola
review_headline: Good snack size but tastes bad.
review_body: Good snack size but tastes bad:  I was so excited to try this new granola snack! Perfect size for a little treat or combined with other things to snack on. I really enjoy having granola treats and many bars. 1.2 oz pouch, just 140 calories. So easy to throw in jacket pocket, car glove holder, lunch box, locker, etc.  Alas, I'm sorry to say that I really did NOT like these. Opened up eagerly, scooped up and threw into my mouth enthusiastically.Tasted surprisingly bland and hard texture. But I'm used to sometimes having it grow on me.Next bite, didn't like how small and flaky size the granola was - no clusters - more like dried wheat as an ingredients for something. Next bite, I had big a chunk of salt which made me stick out my tongue. Eeek! I do not like salty things in general, but then I poured more from the pouch into my palm and poured it into my mouth, I got a whole salt batch all over, and I was only half way through the pouch (not the left over at bottom salt crumbs).  Then it has pieces of dried apple. I do like apple flavors, but the texture is terrible here. They are like really, really stale un-edible pieces of shriveled up apple flakes. Very thin, and hard to chew into. Couldn't eat a single one. Bummer!  I hope Bear Naked changes this and improves (immensely) as it could be much better.

product_title: Lars' Own Belgian Pearl Sugar, 8 oz
review_headline: Does not work for Liege Waffles because it does not melt.
review_body:
These may look the part, but they do not work for liege waffles. I started out using Lars' Own, and I could not get the sugar nibs to melt and caramelize on the surface of the waffle. At first, I thought my iron wasn't getting hot enough. Then I ordered pearl sugar from another source based on numerous customer recommendations, and I finally got the crunchy caramel crust I'd been looking for. Something about the manufacturing process used for Lars' Own makes it so that the nibs stay hard and solid.

Online sources that sell the correct version also offer the sugar for cheaper prices than you see here. If you're using this for an application that requires the pearl sugar to stay solid and unmelted, then this may work. My rating is based on the fact that it is marketed as "Belgian Pearl Sugar," which falsely suggests that it is appropriate for Liege waffles.

product_title: Flavor God Seasonings, 5oz, Pack Of 4
review_body:
I noticed a significant amount of hype about this particular seasoning via Instagram. I am one who enjoys home-cooked meals and learning different recipes for meal prepping. Quite a few fitness enthusiasts claim to use this product when it comes to their seasoning of choice. After witnessing how prominent Flavor God seasoning was among the people I follow on Instagram, I decided to try it for myself.

In the few months that I have experimented with this particular seasoning brand, I have drawn several conclusions. First, it definitely has significant flavor. When added to your meal, it makes it much more enjoyable and great to eat. Secondly, the bottles themselves carry a good amount of seasoning so you certainly get your money's worth.
