review_headline: Matcha Green Tea Powder by Jun Nippon Matcha
review_body:
If you are a tea lover and you can never seem to find one strong enough the you are going to love love lover Matcha. With green tea you are steeping the tea leaves and then removing them. However, with Matcha tea you are actually using the leaves which have been ground into a fine powder. It is heated to just below a boil and then whisked with a bamboo brush until frothy.

Matcha tea is already a stronger tea due to the way it is grown, protected by shade, harvested and chilled. It is higher in nutrients than regular green tea and is very rich in polyphenols which is an antioxidant and has been tied to protection against heart disease, cancer, blood sugar regulation, blood pressure reduction and anti aging. It also helps in boosting the metabolism.

Jun Nippon Matcha Green Tea Powder is organic and gives you a boost in energy. One glass of matcha green tea is equal to ten glasses of regular green tea. Yes it is higher in caffeine which means high energy but it also has I-theanine which creates and alert calm and induces relaxation without drowsiness.

Jun Nippon includes with this purchase a free e-book with matcha recipes. It is certified by the USDA and is used in high ceremonial rites in Japan. Just the process of making matcha tea is putting you in that slow down mode and then the ceremony of drinking it gives you an instant calm and then the effects of the tea are going to improve upon that. There are so many more benefits of drinking matcha tea.

You get the satisfaction or money back guarantee and it's a fast and simple way to order on Amazon and very easy to stay stocked in the product for further benefits.

I received this product at a discount in exchange for my honest and unbiased opinion and is based solely on my experience with the product.

After I have cooked my oatmeal, I add: a teaspoon of Splenda, a few shakes of ground cloves, a few shakes of ground ginger, a splash of vanilla extract, two handfuls of blueberries, two tablespoons of flaxseed meal, a tablespoon of blackstrap molasses (that I purchase through Amazon.com), and a chopped-up banana. product_title: French COCOA POWDER 1 LB By Morning Pep Dark and Rich USDA certified ORGANIC by NFC, NATURAL , Unsweetened Raw NON GMO (16 OZ) Nice Resealable Stand Up Pouch Bag
review_headline: False advertisement - Cocoa powder is NOT CACAO powder - they are two different things
review_body: This product is incorrectly referred to in this Amazon write-up as Cacao.  Cocoa and Cacao are two different products - its either one or the other.  If you look at the product picture (front and back), you will see that it is not referred to as cacao on the bag ... only on this Amazon write-up.  Cocoa is a luxurious chocolaty powder and cacao is not decadent, but still nice-tasting, but it is a very healthy elixir, which is known to be beneficial to your health in many ways.  It is becoming more well-known by healthy connoisseurs and is a popular up-and-coming health drink.  It strikes me that this is being advertised in a way to try to capture both markets.  I am giving it 1 star for false-advertisement.  I thought when I received this product about 2 months ago, I saw that it is Cocoa and not Cacao, and I thought that I made the mistake in ordering the wrong product.  I just sat down to write a review and noticed the improper marketing and I realized that I wasn't negligent, I was deceived.  But it is too late to bicker with Amazon.  In addition, the description says it is "Dutch Processed Alkalized for quick and easy dissolving in COLD and hot water".  Bologny!  You need to warm up the liquid and whisk it in a large bowl for several minutes to get it dissolved.  Even then, it does not dissolve well.  That is not too much of a complaint for me, but those properties are more like cacao than cocoa.

product_title: Szeged Hot Paprika Seasoning Spice, 4 Ounce
review_headline: Is this still from Paprika grown in Hungary??
review_body:
I am hesitant to write this for fear of hurting sales, since I consider this to be the best widely available paprika in this country.
However, I have noticed that the packaging and, more importantly, the wording has charged on this brand. Previously it was a red tin bordering a WHITE sqaure, now that square is tan. But what concerns me is that the new packaging states that it is "Hungarian Style", where as previously it did not. So I am concerned that it may no longer really be Hungarian Paprika.

Beer companies, and others, like to use the word "style" when a product is no longer authentic, or the real thing, if you will. Next time in a liquor store, take a close look at Becks or Red Stripe beer, they are respectively labelled as German and Jamaican "style" beer. But neither is any longer brewed in those countries.
???

product_title: Organic ​Darjeeling​ Black Tea Leaves​ from Himalayas (200+ Cups), 100% Certified Pure Unblended Darjeeling, Packed at Source in India, 16-Ounce Bag
review_body: UPDATE: I loved the tea so much that I ordered 2 more packet (paying full Price) recently after the 1st packet was over and was highly disappointed. Darjeeling tea is mostly famous for its flavor and taste, but the one I got on December, 2015 is one of the poorest quality Darjeeling tea I have ever tasted. Not only it lacks the flavor it had before, I need double the quantity of tea too to make 2 cups of tea (previously I needed 1 1/2 tea spoon of leaves now even putting in 3 tea spoon does not give me the flavor or the taste). I have returned the 2nd unopened packet back but the one I have opened and has spend almost $20 for is sitting in my pantry. I would not recommend it any more.   I am from West Bengal, India,  it is the state where Darjeeling is. At my home tea is the first thing that we drink every morning and a good cup of tea makes the perfect start of the day. From tea all over the world I am a huge an of Darjeeling tea mainly because of the flavor and the aroma. The packaged arrived around noon and I was impatiently waiting for the evening to try it out. I like my tea with milk so after brewing the tea leaves for about 1-2 mins, when I was truly enjoying the amazing aroma of the tea leaves, I added a little milk and took my first sip and wow I was blown away by the taste of the tea. My husband who is not much of a tea drinker, loved the tea too and says no matter how costly the tea is we are going to buy this tea again. If drinking Darjeeling tea with milk you wont get the deep color of black that we generally get but the flavors are out of the world. For me as a true tea drinker (well I consider myself that, and it is difficult for me to really like a tea if they lack of taste) I give the tea a full 5 stars for it taste. But I really hope the price was a little low, because considering the price of tea we get from Indian stores it is too high. If Price is not a matter give this a try if you really want flavor in your cup of tea.  **I received the product at a discount (previously) in exchange of my unbiased review.

product_title: Numi Tea Organic Spiced Rooibos Herbal Tessan Ruby Chai Tea -- 6 per case.
review_body:
*****
Numi Tea's Ruby Chai Spiced Rooibos Herbal Teasan is a premium Numi tea, both certified organic and kosher. The ingredients are fair trade certified organic rooibos, organic cloves, organic allspice, organic ginger, organic cardamom, organic nutmeg, and organic cinnamon. There is nothing artificial in this or in any Numi tea.

On the box, the manufacturer writes: "Earthy, vanilla organic Rooibos and a sultry blend of spices come together from around the world in Numi's caffeine-free chai. Warmed by the South African sun, Rooibos is lauded for its high anti-oxidant properties. It is full-bodied like black tea, yet soothing any time of day. We've sourced the highest quality cloves, nutmeg, and cinnamon, mingling them with sweet Indian allspice and vivacious Chinese ginger. A kiss of cardamom unites this fabulous caffeine-free alternative to Numi's Golden Chai. Share in the savory warmth of Ruby Chai."

If you are used to black tea or coffee and are wanting to get accustomed to herbal caffeine-free teas, this is a great way to start, since the flavors are stronger and thus less subtle than most herbal teas. Although this is a "chai", note that it contains no sweeteners...I prefer this, but commercial chais often have sugar added, so be prepared for a wonderful no-calorie indulgence that is beautifully flavored but not "sweet" in the same way that sugar is.

If you like Numi teas, get it---you won't be sorry. If you are new to Numi but love chai, this will probably be the best you've ever had.

Highly recommended.
*****

I got to try a bite of the crunchy peanut butter bar, but that was it. They were so good that my boys ate them up before I barely made it in the house with them. So I rate the Jif peanut butter bars 6 stars for my boys. Jif peanut butter bars are now a family favorite. Thanks Crowdtap and Jif for helping me find my family a more healthier snack!

I've drank this flavor daily for 1+ years and I still love it.  They are much cheaper on Amazon then in the store!

review_body: Who knew you could enjoy an energy drink for breakfast? I received this product to try and review from bzzagent.com. I love the taste of this V8 V-Fusion +Energy. You can taste both the peach and the mango and the combination is delicious. It is not as sweet as fruit juice. The drink contains 50% juice, and and also green tea. There is no added sugar which is a selling point for me as my fiance has diabetes and I try to watch my sugar intake as well. There is a mild amount of caffeine in the drink which I prefer as too much caffeine makes me jittery. V8 V-Fusion +Energy has just enough to get you through a busy morning. I would definitely recommend it.

product_title: PUR Gum Aspartame Free, 2.8 Ounce
review_headline: Wonderful to be able to enjoy gum again
review_body: When I discovered that there was such a thing as aspartame free gum, I was excited. I love gum, but my stomach goes into knots when I have anything with even a small amount of aspartame in it. I tried several flavors of this Pur gum, thinking that I would like the Pomegranate the least. As is turns out, I like it the best. By far! Now it's the only version of Pur gum I buy. The flavor is great and the gum texture is very good. Not at all grainy. However each piece of gum it very tiny. But it's worth it to finally be able to have gum again that both tastes great and doesn't have any aspartame to bother my stomach.

It's not pricey and that's a very good thing because it doesn't taste very good. Weak, bitter and dusty tasting. Another sad case of an American institution sold-out and resting on its laurels? review_body:
Today at work we had a cookout with the whole corporate office. Being on the FUN committee that planned it, i helped grill out. Grilling for over 60+ people, we were outside in the beating sun for 4 hours. When it was our turn to eat, i grabbed my Pomegranate Blueberry free V8 V-Fusion® +Energy to get some much needed energy.

My sluggest morning turned brighter thanks to the free V8 V-Fusion® +Energy and i pepped right up soon afterwards. The 6 other employees who were tired after helping cook kept asking why i seemed to have more energy. I told them that i got the drink for free from BzzAgent and said they needed to try. They asked if i had any discounts and i promised i'd bring some coupons that i got tomorrow to work for them!

product_title: Pumpkin Seeds
review_body:
Bob's Red Mill Pumpkin Seeds are quite simply the best pumpkin seeds that I have ever found. They are shelled, raw, and grown/packaged in the USA (according to both the package and their website). Every other shelled brand that I found was a product of China -- and, given their less than desirable environmental/industrial record, I was very uneasy about that with food products such as this. Specifically, their Facebook page says that these pumpkin seeds are grown in Oregon.

These can be eaten raw, but for an awesome taste I usually lightly coat them with olive oil (which seems to prevent them from puffing up and popping in the oven), salt them a little and bake them on a cookie sheet (or in a cake pan) at 300 degrees for a few minutes.

Plus -- pumpkin seeds are an extremely healthy food that contains a whole lot of protein!

Bzzagent sent me free jars and coupons, so I just put it in a reusable squeeze pouch and handed it over.... HE LOVED IT! He sucked down every last drop :) My husband and I are very particular about the food we feed our son. 95% of his food is "real" or "organic". I am very leary of feeding him any processed or prepackaged foods. After carefully studying the ingredients in Beech-Nut Organics and reading more about the company, I felt good about the choice to feed him the Beech-Nut Organics food. I am confident in the quality, taste and convienance of Beech-Nut Organics baby food.

review_headline: Not hard like the ones my mother as a kid,from the late 50's remembers them.
review_body: I bought these for my mother as a treat because she can't find them where she lives. She was attacked by Africanized bee or AKA killer bees while out hiking with 13 other people just the other day. Some were rushed to the ER even. Long story short, I bought these for her as a treat and to ease the pain of the recent attack of killer bees she sustained. She was so delighted to receive them that I received a phone call close to 12pm because she was so excited. We have a 3 hour time difference so it was only 9pm for her. I was told today that these are the softer ones but still none the less she said that they are amazing and loves them anyway. Her one friend wishes to write the company directly because she would like the original texture of the jujubes as they use to be very hard and took time to work on them instead of just chewing up like a gummy bear as they now are being made. None the less if I can't find any hard ones then this will have to do. I remember them being hard when I was a kid eating them once and a blue moon when my mother would get a box while we would be at the movies in the early to mid 90's. I assume close to gone are the days of the hard jujubes.

product_title: WhistleStop Cafe Fried Green Tomato Batter Mix, 9-Ounce
review_headline: Good coating for just about anything.
review_body: We're southern but have never been into fried food, either eating it or having to clean up the mess afterward. However, we did accidentally hit upon Whistle Stop batter mix in our local chain grocery store while looking for something to spice up all the fish we eat. It's just right for nearly any meat, fish or vegetable. We just put a small amount on a plate and lightly coat whatever is on the night's menu. I've been able to get everyone to eat a mixed green salad for lunch or dinner by lightly coating sliced mushrooms or even sliced bell peppers, along with chicken breast or salmon. Yes, we did try it with sliced green tomatoes, as well as with cauliflower and broccoli.

product_title: Oreo Thins 10.1oz Package
review_body:
This is the best thing to happen to Oreo's in a long time. They are perfect now.

For store-bought cookies, Oreos are my favorite. It is hard to beat Oreos and milk but I was always taking them apart to remove most of the middle and making a big mess. No more! Now, there's just the right amount of filling and the wafers are perfectly matched. They seem extra crispy and even tastier.

The Original Oreo package is 14.3 ounces, the Thins are 10.1 ounces. However, the Thins have 40 cookies compared to 36 cookies in a normal Original package.

Amazon is not providing the nutritional info found on the back of the package. Info found on the back of Original Oreos is for three Oreos but the Thins list the info for 4 Oreos. So, I used Calorie King to adjust the info 'cookie for cookie,' based upon three cookies. Divide by three to get info per single cookie.

Original Oreos: (Info for 3 cookies using package info)
Calories 160. Calories from fat 60.
Fat 7 grams. Saturated fat 2 grams.
Total Carbs 25 grams with 14 grams from sugar.
Protein 1 gram
Sodium 135mg

Oreo Thins: (info for 3 cookies)
Calories 106. Calories from fat 41.
Fat grams 4.5. Saturated fat 1.5.
Total Carbs 15.9 with 9.1 grams from sugar.
Protein 0.8 grams.
Sodium 72mg.

For those of you that like to use Oreos for crusts and toppings, the Thins work in the food processor much better. I don't even need to remove the middle part to avoid clumping. The decreased middle provides just enough cohesion and taste to make them great for recipes.

My Walmart has the 10.1 ounce package for $2.98, Walgreens is $3.99 (just bought another bag).

product_title: Java Factory Single Cup Coffee for Keurig K Cup Brewers
review_body:
I really like these coffees, with the exception of the two flavored varieties. However, I am not a huge fan of flavored coffees to begin with, so that contributes a lot to my lack of enthusiasm for the Choconut (which tasted more like choclate and hazelnut than chocolate and coconut) and Vanilla Dream. I drink my coffee sweetened and with cream and found both of these flavors to be cloying when prepared that way. They are not "gently" flavored.

But, I really liked the other, non-flavored, choices in the box. My favorite is Da Bomb which makes a beautifully dark and smooth cup of coffee. Many times dark roasts can be a bit overpowering, but this was "just right". The Smooth Caffeinator lives up to it's name; smooth and enjoyable. Likewise with Dark and Handsome. My least favorite is Wakey Wakey which I found to be a bit too weak for my liking. But, if you enjoy a very light "blonde" style of coffee, this may be to your liking.

Overall, a nice variety containing choices to please various palates.

product_title: Dixie Diners' Club - Chicken (Not!) Strips
review_body: It's unfortunate that the vendor does not state that this TVP product is completely UNFLAVORED otherwise, I believe the reviews would be better.  Most vegan / vegetarians familiar with buying TVP expect the package to denote whether the product is flavored or not.  When you buy TVP that is labeled "chicken" or "beef", etc. you expect it to be seasoned to mimic those tastes.  If it's unflavored, as this product is, it is typically labeled as such because you're expected to decide what you want it to taste like and then, season it yourself.  I purchased this product in its "chunk" form (not actually these dish-shaped strips - which actually aren't strips, at all) and cooked up a batch and was very pleasantly surprised at the authentic texture - but again, was disappointed that it was completely unseasoned.  After a quick fix with a serving of McKay's "chicken" seasoning and a dab of soy sauce, this made a very satisfying chicken-like product that holds together well and can be added to pasta dishes, soup or stews successfully.

product_title: Jeremiah's Pick Coffee Organic Water Processed Dark Roast Decaf Ground Coffee, 10-Ounce Bags (Pack of 3)

product_title: Brooklyn Bean Roastery Single-Cup Hot Cocoa for Keurig K-Cup Brewers
review_headline: Unique variety of hot cocoa flavors 4.5 stars
review_body:
This review is for the 40 count hot chocolate variety.

This 40 pack has six different hot cocoa varieties and this is a great way to try different unique flavors of hot cocoa. Some of the flavors I like better than others but each has a different taste that should suit most hot cocoa fans. I personally like Carnival cocoa with its slightly caramel flavor, Midnight with its rich chocolate flavor and the milk chocolate flavor. The Campfire with its hint of a cup of cocoa with fresh marshmallows in it is good as well, although not my favorite. My least favorites of the bunch are the peanut butter cup and Mexican which has a bit of cinnamon flavor in it. Both are unique and different and good for changing up to a different flavor but not my favorites. This brand makes solid coffee and the cocoa is just as good. This variety pack is a great way to try a several hot cocoa flavors, especially if you have a variety of tastes in the household or the office.

4.5 stars

I was provided a sample for review and I do not promise a good rating in return, nor am I affiliated with this company or compensated in any way. I am just a regular Amazon customer who shops a lot on Amazon.

product_title: Ricochet Candies with Xylitol, Licorice, 100-Count Mints (Pack of 6)
review_headline: IF YOU LOVE LICORICE.........CHECK IT OUT.......
review_body:
These licorice candies are really 'licoricey.' They smell like licorice right from the tin when you open the cardboard box. I love licorice but a few years ago it cost me $3700 worth of dental work because the licorice tends to cling to your teeth causing all kinds of problems (who knew).

Because of that I have stayed away from one of my favorites until now. GOOD NEWS-this product is not only delish but has an ingredient - Xylitol - that is GOOD FOR YOUR TEETH.

Look it up online - it's used for a multitude of products and with very few drawbacks-some digestive issues if you are prone to that sort of thing. If you don't pig out and eat the whole tin you shouldn't have a problem.

The price is HIGH and EXPENSIVE but this is my second order so you know they must be good--try them if you're a licorice lover-and get the benefit of two... flavor-teeth health!!!

They are small, teeny small and they melt in your mouth immediately with a yummy licorice flavor-this can be an expensive addition!!

product_title: Spice Appeal Turmeric Ground, 16 Ounce
review_body:
Bought this product at the recommendation of health gurus that suggest that Turmeric can stave away cancer. Turmeric is used for arthritis, heartburn (dyspepsia), stomach pain, diarrhea, intestinal gas, stomachbloating, loss of appetite, jaundice, liver problems and gallbladder disorders.
It is also used for headaches, bronchitis, colds, lung infections, fibromyalgia, leprosy, fever, menstrual problems, and cancer. Other uses include depression, Alzheimer’s disease, water retention, worms, and kidney problems.
Some people apply turmeric to the skin for pain, ringworm, bruising, leech bites, eye infections, inflammatory skin conditions, soreness inside of the mouth, and infected wounds.
You also need to know that the active ingredient in Turmeric is curcumin that does this and that the compound itself is not digestible without using black pepper to aid in the digestion.

product_title: Blue, Saga Cheese (1 lb)
review_headline: Creamy, mild and perfect with wine, coffee or ...
review_body:
I purchase this locally, so my review is going to remain focused on the cheese. This has been one of my staples that I enjoy for breakfast, or when entertaining.

This cheese is very creamy with a mild mold that has only a hint of bitterness. In fact, part of the creaminess and mildness come from the triple-cream process and the cow milk base.

For me this is one of the cheeses I enjoy at breakfast (usually spread on a baguette or toasted pumpernickel bread), paired with espresso. It's buttery and has a touch of earthiness and tang, so it also pairs with most fruit except citrus. For wine, this pairs nicely with most reds and whites, but to my palate is best with a Beaujolais Villages for reds and a Cortese di Gavi for white.

Nutritionally each 200 gram wedge in the wheel contains two servings, each with 450 calories and 44 grams of fat. Sodium is fairly high at 680mg per serving, but is actually less than most blue cheeses, which can be over twice that.

product_title: Ruta Maya Whole Bean Organic Medium Roast Coffee, 2.2 Pounds
review_headline: If you like flavor, this is the coffee for you.
review_body:
Many years ago, a friend brought back Kona coffee from a trip to Hawaii. He raved about this stuff! One morning, I joined him in a cup. Yes, it was smooth, no harsh after taste—but I didn't really see what the hype was all about. Nowadays, there are coffee shops all around the USA with the word "Kona" in the name. I don't know . . . I guess I just never got on the trend-wagon.

Now, if you like flavor, Ruta Maya is for you. The first few sips will open your eyes. The first few sips remind you of why you buy the good stuff, why you put the effort into grinding the beans yourself, why you pour every mug separately rather than use those heartless machines. (For the record, I use a ceramic filter cone and #4 filters, although #2's would work, too.)

After a few minutes, you have that taste on your tongue. Yes, THAT taste. Not unpleasant, perhaps a touch bitter, I consider it a gentle reminder of all that is good in life.

Then, mid-way down your second mug, when you aren't drinking your coffee so quickly, you realize that Ruta Maya still tastes good lukewarm!

I'm going to be totally honest with you, full disclosure: I'm drinking Ruta Maya right now. I have literally been sipping between paragraphs. I love this stuff.

product_title: Hampton Creek, Just Mayo, Original, Gluten Free, Soy Free, Egg Free, 30oz Jar
review_body:
Whether there is egg or not doesn't seem as important as the canola oil in these products. I don't know how manufacturers can claim to use "non GMO" Canola oil. Canola is not a plant like sunflower or safflower, it is derived from the rapeseed plant which is highly toxic to humans and the source of Mustard Gas used in WWI. Monsanto created a GMO canola plant in 1995.
Canola oil from the rape seed, referred to as the Canadian oil because Canada is mainly responsible for it being marketed in the USA. The Canadian government and industry paid our Federal Food and Drug Administration (FDA) $50 million dollars to have canola oil placed on the (GRAS) List, "Generally Recognized As Safe". Thus a new industry was created. Laws were enacted affecting international trade, commerce, and traditional diets. Studies with lab. animals were disastrous. Rats developed fatty degeneration of heart, kidney, adrenals, and thyroid glands. When canola oil was withdrawn from their diets, the deposits dissolved but scar tissue remained on all vital organs. No studies on humans were made before money was spent to promote Canola oil in the USA.
Read more at http://www.snopes.com/medical/toxins/canola.asp#A1UIOJ2EJVVFydO5.99
Like all modern vegetable oils, canola goes through the process of refining, bleaching and degumming, all of which involve high temperatures or chemicals of questionable safety. Because Canola oil is high in omega 3 fatty acids which easily become rancid and foul-smelling when subjected to oxygen and high temperatures, it must be deodorized. The standard de-odorization process removes a large portion of the omega 3 fatty acids turning them into trans fat. Avoid hydrogenated oils like the plague!

It's always driven me nuts to be so wasteful, but I couldn't find pure olive juice. I bought some god-awful bottle at the local packy that had vinegar as a key ingredient. I assume that it acts as a preservative. I ended up dumping that down the drain and searched for alternatives. Most that I came across also had vinegar, until I saw this. This is not exactly the taste of pure olive juice, but it's darned close enough and convenient enough that I'll probably stick with it from this point forward. It's not as strong as juice, so I need to add more. I probably use 1-2 teaspoons.

product_title: Poseymom Kombucha Scoby and 1/2 Cup Starter Tea
review_body: Please read review in full!!! Upon receiving the scoby, this was my original review...... Nothing like advertised. Stated there would be two Scobies - only one received. Stated they would be 3 - 3.5 inches each.  It is smaller Stated there would be generous amount of reserve tea. There was less than 1/4 cup. Stated there would be detailed instructions included.  There were none.  ************************** The above was written upon receiving the scoby and I originally gave it 2 stars.  I just found out how to edit my review and want to update my review.  Originally, I purchased a dehydrated scoby from a company and made two batches.  While the acid content did go down, no baby scoby formed and the original scoby sat at the bottom.  I then purchased the one from Poseymom.  I started a second batch using her scoby.  Within 10 days, the scoby rose to the top and a baby scoby began to form.  It is 3 weeks into the process and a thick baby is forming.  The scoby I received was small; however, the batch of kombucha I put it in is 1/2 gallon.  I am very pleased with the results.  Just changed my review from 2 to 5 stars even though I only received one scoby, it has 'taken off'!

review_headline: With French Vanilla creamer! Delish! -From a non-tea drinker.
review_body: I can't say yet if it has increased mikk because I bought it today, BUT...I will say it is delish.  I am NOT a tea drinker. In fact, I downright hate tea. Iced tea, etc. I also don't like licorice (which everyone says this tastes like).  I can stand, however, chai teas with soy from Starbucks or other coffee shops.  Anyway --- I was really worried. I need the tea to increase my meager supply and I will try just about anything.  First cup I made was great! I added some Carnation French Vanilla creamer.  Wow!  I'm sure another brand would work.  Later, I brewed the tea, put it in the fridge, then later added crushed ice (as much as I could fit in my reusable cup) again with French vanilla creamer.  I actually said out loud that it tastes better than my go-to Starbucks chai.  Seriously.  Anyway, just a tip :) I saw someone else say they do soy, almond, or coconut milk. I'd like to try that too, but I think I found a winner.  Another note -- since I'm the anti-tea drinker, I don't own a kettle.  I used my Keurig and did the hot water feature. Worked great.

product_title: Honey Hawaiian Lehua
review_headline: Hawaiian Lehua Honey, a Very Different Kind of Honey
review_body:
Ever since I discovered the Lehua honey about five years ago, I do not even look at any other kind of honey anymore. I buy it by the bucket from Amazon every year (usually 2 to 3 pails, 12-lb. each, to save on shipping), and use it in my daily coffee, yogurt and cooking. Once in a while, I put it on bagels or a piece of toast. It adds so much interest to the food!

Caution: this honey is distinctively 'spicy.' If you prefer mild honey, it may not be for you. Its texture is also very different - thick & creamy when the weather is warm and almost lard-like solid in the cold.

product_title: Jade Leaf - Organic Japanese Matcha Green Tea Powder, Classic Culinary Grade (For Blending & Baking)
review_body:
I am very impressed with this Matcha. I've had the opportunity to try a lot of different Matchas sold on Amazon, but this one currently sticks out. It's a great culinary grade matcha, and a really great deal. First of all, there's quite a bit in the bag, it appears as though Jade Leaf Organics may sometimes offer more than one size, but currently only has the 100g bag on Amazon. It really contains quite a lot of matcha, especially for the price.

It's comforting to know that this matcha is organic, and is sourced from a farm in Japan. While more information doesn't always mean a product is safer or better, it helps when a company is upfront about where its product comes from. It's also USDA certified organic, which is a great way to know you can believe the company's claims.

The matcha powder is very vibrantly colored, and very flavorful. I made a smoothie with the matcha, almond milk and a banana, and the matcha flavor was very strong and prominent. Some of the other brands I have tried have not been as flavorful, but this one really has that great matcha flavor that I love.

I had the opportunity to purchase the matcha at a reduced price in exchange for an honest review, but I will definitely repurchase at the full (and very reasonable!) price.

product_title: Wheat Thins Toasted Pita Original, 8 Ounce by Wheat Thins Toasted Pita
review_headline: I've Got to admit...I like these things!
review_body:
Well snack stuff comes and snack stuff goes. I can remember the only chip type snack you could purchase were potato chips packaged in a cellophane bag that often time had green edges around them and were pretty stale by the time you got them home from the grocery store. Now days there are shelves and shelves of chip snacks...some of them will last; some of them will go...chip snacks are apart of our ever changing world.

Now I purchase a bag of these toasted wheat thins Pita bread dipping chips for the express use of eating a spinach and avocado dip my wife makes. I must tell you that I am impressed with these things. I like the texture and I like the flavor. Of course I must admit to being a fan of Pita bread anyway, but in chip for...well, it adds a different dimension.

For those of you who care:

Serving size 17 ciips.....

Calories 130
Total Fat 4.5g
Cholesterol 0% (Yeah)
Sodium 160 mg (bummer)
Potassium 60mg
Carbohydrates 20g

While you may not be able to call these \\"health food,\\" I have most certainly put worse in my stomach.

Now I prefer these to the ones which are flavored with garlic as the garlic they use is a bit overwhelming.

These are stiff enough and strong enough so that they are not constantly breaking up when I use them on my wife's dip...which is pretty thick.

I purchase mine at our local Wal-Mart and while the are not the cheapest things in the world, the eating pleasure is well worth the extra cost...in my opinion.

These puppies will fill you up rather quickly.

Don Blankenship
The Ozarks

product_title: Signatures Milk Chocolate, Almonds, 48 Ounce
review_body:
Product tastes great however it depends on who you're buying from. One time I recieved them and they were perfect. Another time (sent from "Better Live") they had completely melted and arrived on my doorstep resolidified into one big massive brick. I literally had to use a sharp tool to chisel out a chunk. (See pics)
I would suggest, perhaps, contacting the seller before ordering and ask how they will ship it. Mine arrived in a simple box. Hence, the reason I recieved a chocolate brick instead of individual choc covered almonds. The product itself is wonderful but absolutely addicting. So be careful. You may need to check yourself into rehab or else fall into a cocoa induced coma.
And lastly, if after all of this, you are willing to chance it, I would strongly urge you to invest in a good chisel.

She is continuing to try to get the kefir to work, and I'll update this review if she's able to revive it. For now though, Buyer Beware: It seems a lot of people have received dead kefir from this vendor.

product_title: Nitro2Go Mega Shot
review_headline: Good flavor and packs a punch
review_body:
First I want to state that I received this product in exchange for my honest opinion.

The Mega Shot by Nitro2Go is a 4oz bottle with two servings in each bottle. All of the bottles where sealed when I got them. I opened one bottle to give it a try, and the "lift and peel" sticker was quite a challenge, I actually had to use a knife to cut open the tab...nothing like it being sealed for freshness! I am quite picky when it comes to energy shots but the flavor was not bad at all! It had a very mild berry flavor and reminded me of a WarHead candy once the sour powder was gone. It was quite delicious and I would definitely drink it again! As far as energy goes, I definitely felt energized without feeling jittery​ or shaky. I only drank one serving at a time as I was not sure what to expect but was pleasantly surprised and energized all day. Will definitely recommend and buy again!

At Domino's I had to plead to use the employee bathroom in the kitchen because they didn't have one for customers. The unholy demon spawn they came out of my rectum in Dominos probably seeped into everyone's pizza that was cooking. My 40 minute commute took me 2 hours.

Still though - the taste was great. 6 stars! Would recommend! Note to self - keep paper towels in car to stuff in pants after eating these.

product_title: Matcha Green Tea Powder 1 oz loose tea sample
review_headline: Gets me the caffeine I need
review_body:
I use this in my smoothies every morning, or if I'm in a hurry, I mix a teaspoon with an ounce or two of water and drink it like a shot of wheat grass. I have given up coffee altogether thanks to Matcha and my gut is so much happier. And everyone around me is happy because I'm still getting caffeine.
One of the things I really like about Matcha is that I don't get the jittery feeling I get when I've had too much coffee but it keeps me alert and focused (I'm a student so I NEED my caffeine).
I'll admit that I am not a fan of green tea in general, I hate green tea flavored things so I resisted Matcha for quite a while. The taste of this is innocuous enough that it blends in with a smoothie or when I down a quick homemade energy shot of it. If you are someone who enjoys the taste of Matcha of green tea, I cannot offer up an opinion if you'll like this or not.
The color is bright green and it's in a resealable package like shown.

product_title: V8 +Energy Drinks
review_body: These v8 fusion energy drinks are awesome!  I hate other energy drinks because they taste disgusting.  Like horrible chemicals.  These drinks taste just like delicious juice, but I get about the same pick-me-up as a good cup of coffee.  I've actually tried several flavors of these and they are all fantastic.  No chemical taste at all.  I also like how easy they are.  I like to buy 2 6-packs at  time because my husbands steals all mine!  The size of the juice cans is perfect, not too big.  Perfect snack size.  I don't have to refrigerate them if I don't want to, so I tend to carry one (or two!) in my purse with me for those afternoon slumps.  I only wish they tasted good as a mixer with vodka like other juice (don't try it, its awful!).  What can I say?  I was desperate and tired...  Also, a great price for the product.  I have already bought several 6-packs and I plan on continuing.  I got my first 6-pack free to try and review, and I'm so glad (because I had never heard of it before).  I never bought energy drinks before these.  They tasted awful and made my stomach turn.  I have no problem with v8 fusion though.  It's just like drinking regular juice.

product_title: Amoretti Premium Syrup, Mai Tai, 25.4 Ounce
review_headline: Tastes great in iced tea! Naturally flavored with no artificial sweeteners!
review_body:
Amoretti Mai Tai is a combination of the flavors of rum, Curacao liqueur, and lime. Prior to receiving this product from the company for review I had not tried this flavor. I do not like the taste of alcohol but found this combination of flavors very enticing - and it is non-alcoholic. I love that I do not need to refrigerate the bottle. It did come with its own pump that reaches clear to the bottom of the bottle so none of the syrup gets wasted.

I added one pump to a 16 ounce glass of iced tea and have been enjoying it that way. It does work for both hot and cold drinks as well as baked goods, icings and more.

This product arrived quickly, was nicely packaged and the customer service was quick to reply with answers to some of my questions such as the request if it needed refrigerated. Furthermore the lady I spoke with was very familiar with the product and actually used it herself. I appreciated this.

I like that is product is made with natural flavor, real sugar and is only 35 calories per serving.

Disclaimer: I received this product in exchange for my honest, unbiased review. All opinions are my own.

product_title: V8 V-Fusion Diet Strawberry Lemonade Energy Drink, 8 Fl Oz (Pack of 6)
review_body:
I wasn't expecting too much from the V8 energy, but was pleasantly surprised. The only way I could think to get a good idea of how much energy this really gives was to replace my Monday morning coffee with this drink. I expected to be groggy and running on empty all day, but was super impressed by how I felt. I was awake, alert, and had plenty of energy to get me through the day. Assuming it was a fluke, I again replaced my morning coffee with V8 Energy the next couple of days with similar results. I have purchased several of these 6-packs since initially trying it, and will continue to do so.

There are two flavors of the diet version of this drink, and I have tried both of them. The cranberry flavor is pretty good; it tastes a little bit like watered down cranberry juice but not in a bad way. The strawberry lemonade flavor isn't the best. I drank all six of them, and would drink it again if I didn't have a choice, but I won't go so far as to say it tastes good. Plus, it's a weird not-found-in-nature yellow color when I poured it into a glass and that put me off.

Finally, I really like they they only have 3 carbs per can, which is awesome for my low-moderate carb lifestyle. Overall, I give these 5 stars!

product_title: Victoria Marinara Sauce, 24 Ounce (Pack of 6)
review_headline: No sugar, No Tomato Paste and it tastes freshly made.
review_body:
Though I generally review entertainment products on Amazon I do occasionally review food products and after returned from the Fancy Food Show in New York , this product was sent to me by the manufacturer for an objective review. I had a chance to try it there but I wanted to try it at home.

The first thing I look for on a jarred pasta sauce (no, I don’t make my own “gravy”), is the list of ingredients to see where both sugar and tomato paste rank on the list of ingredients. Victoria puts the list on the front of the jar and in print size you can read easily.

Here are the ingredients for the Marinara Sauce: 100% Italian tomatoes, Imported Olive Oil, Fresh Garlic, Basil and Onions, Sea Salt & Spices. What? No tomato paste? No sugar? No chemical names? Nope! I still have issues with companies that list the word “spices” without identifying them (I’m guessing there is some oregano here and maybe some pepper but it is not what we call “spicy”) but this sauce tastes freshly made with no chemical or tomato paste taste.

It’s pricier than Barilla and other major brands but in line with premium ones like Rao’s. But I like this one better. I plan to try their vodka sauce too.

The 24 oz jar is about 50% larger than most jarred sauces so it’ll last longer and I used it one night over pasta and the next night over pan fried breaded pork chops. Both times it tasty and I can certainly recommend it.

It is listed a number of times on Amazon and the price ranges greatly. So look for feedback on seller when you compare the price.
I hope you found this review both informative and helpful.

Steve Ramm
“Anything Phonographic”

product_title: Pepsi Cola, Caffeine Free 12 Fl Oz 12 Pack
review_headline: Good stuff, but it needs to come in a 12-oz bottle!
review_body:
The Caffeine-Free Pepsi Cola is a wonderful alternative to those seeking a caffeine-free drink with good taste. I have a relative with a heart condition (angina) who has found that drinking this brand keeps her from having chest pain, which the caffeine in the regular products tend to induce.

But there are two main problems with Caffeine-Free Pepsi.

One is the availability factor. Stores that carry regular Pepsi products might not carry the Caffeine-Free-- or, if they do, then they carry only the large bottles and/or the cans. I live in a region of the U.S. where we have to drive six hours to find the 20-ounce bottles (in packs of eight, which is much appreciated!), and even then, the stores seldom stock more than 15 cases per day.

The second problem is that-- contrary to the Amazon website listing by "Unknown"-- this product is NOT sold in a 12-ounce bottle. A can cannot be re-sealed; a 20-ounce bottle can be too much soda in one go. A 12-ounce bottle would be a much-appreciated "dose" of Caffeine-Free Pepsi, something that can be consumed in one sitting without having to either drink it all then and there, or close it back up for future use. It would also be easier to store, both outside and inside a refrigerator, if this product came in small 12-ounce bottles.

product_title: SweeTARTS Soft and Chewy Ropes Formerly Kazoozles
review_body:
I saw a package of these on the street at my bustop and decided that they were just too intriguing to pass up.

Not to worry, I bought a new packet, no street candies for me!

Anyways, I thought they'd be pretty horrid, like the time I bought diet bubblegum soda and mixed it with ice cream to make a bubblegum float, but they are actually pretty good. The dark pink candy rope doesn't really taste of much, it's vaguely sweet and super duper-ty chewy but not much else, it's the creamy, tart punch filling that really uplevels this bizarre filled treat. It tastes like Kool Aid fruit punch before it's been diluted with water.

The powder so strongly reminds me of kool aid that I was tempted to run out and buy some. My friend and I used to fill straws with kool aid and call it redneck pixie sticks. We'd save straws from McD's and fill them with kool aid (you bend the end of the straw before starting to fill it, of course) and if you were brave enough (or sugar deprived enough) you'd eat it. We had a lot of unsupervised time on our hands and very little money.

Anyways, the rope filling is sweet and just the tiniest bit tart. I hope someday the SweetTarts people will make a whole line of these, maybe a caramel rope with a sour green apple filling or a kiwi rope with a strawberry tart filling. The variation possibilities are endless!

product_title: Artisan-Style Gourmet Peanut Butter by Squirrel Brand (16 ounce)
review_body:
After enjoying Squirrel Brand's other products (nuts) I decided to give their PB a try after reading the rave reviews. Unfortunately it didn't live up to the hype. The ingredients are simple: roasted peanuts and salt. A LOT of salt. It was OK; kind of like a Payday bar sans the caramel, but too salty for my taste. With this PB salt actually overpowers the peanut flavor. Some people may like peanuts with lots of salt, but I'm not one of them. If you do and have $14 burning a hole in your pocket, go for it. I would, however, highly recommend any of Squirrel Brand's nuts, especially the Creme Brulee Almonds, Five Pepper Cashews, and Buttercream Pecans. For anyone who's curious, the nutrition facts for this peanut butter are as follows:

Serving size: 2 Tbsp; Servings per container: 14; Calories: 190; Calories from fat: 130; Total fat: 16g; Saturated fat: 2g; Trans fat: 0g; Cholesterol: 0mg; Sodium: 330mg; Total carbohydrate: 7g; Dietary fiber: 2g; Sugars: 0g; Protein: 8g.

I then put them in the oven at about 425 and bake uncovered for about 45 minutes.  The gluten free bread crumbs brown up nice and the meat tastes as good as deep fried with a lot less fat to grease your arteries.  I have baked skinless chicken breasts and minute steak this way. product_title: V8 V-Fusion +Energy Drinks, 8 Fl Oz
review_headline: Light taste, finally a energy drink that doesnt taste bitter.
review_body:
I'm not a big fan of drinking huge amounts of energy drinks to feel sluggish from consuming so much liquid. Plus the taste of common energy drinks always puts me off, I don't like it when I have to grimace through a harsh after taste. This is also why I don't drink coffee.
Seeing that BzzAgent had offered me a chance to try out the V8 V-Fusion+Energy, I was super excited.
Why not try a V8 drink that offers a natural energy boost, compared to other drinks this was perfect in size and I had found it in a grocery store that was close to my house. I didn't have to track it down.
I tried the Diet version as opposed to the regular V-Fusion+Energy, I wanted to see how sugary the Diet flavor could be. I was surprised with a light flavor, the can was chilled and I was on the go. Perfect for a commute to work then to tackle my morning. I didn't feel a crash like I normally would after having a coffee.

product_title: Naked Nutrition Sample Pack
review_headline: A great way to test your options before buying bulk
review_body:
I was looking for a simple, pure protein source to add to my morning smoothies. So far the casein has been the least noticeable.
Sunday i used the pea protein. It has an earthy flavor that I didnt mind but my husband wasnt fond of. Had I added more rice milk I think it would have helped. I liked it but had to adjust my mind to it. I noticed that I was certainly not hungry 2.5 hours later like normal with a smoothie. I like this protein since its dairy free and didnt upset my stomach at all.
Monday I used the casein protein. I was happy that this didnt alter the taste of my smoothie aside from making it creamier and slightly more dull tasting. I was fine with this. I really liked this since it didnt really taste but am still on the fence about the dairy issue. I am not vegan but we are trying to cut our intake down as its really just not great for you in large quantities.
Tuesday: Goat protein. I was excited and nervous about this all at the same time. I love the idea of goat milk but find that the flavor is more than our family can adjust to. Upon opening the package I detected no hint of goat smell. After adding it to my smoothie it added the same creamy smoothness to it like the casein did. I only noticed a hint of goat as a mild aftertaste that left quickly. If you didnt know what that flavor was it could easily be dismissed. I really enjoyed this smoothie but its still a dairy and it the most expensive protein offered by naked nutrition so that is certainly a factor.
Wednesday: rice protein day. So in fairness to this smoothie, I upped the maca a bit and added a fistful of kale which is different to the others. This smoothie is decidedly grittier than the others as has no hint of creaminess that they had. Since this is a plant protein I this the pea is the only one you can fairly compare to. It is gritty but has less of a flavor than the pea. Its still drinkable and not tastes fine but I dont know if this is the one I want to have daily for the full 5 pounds.
Thursday: Whey protein. I saved this for last since I was the least excited about it for some reason. I'll post tomorrow after I try it.the full 5 pounds.

product_title: Walden Farms Coffee Creamers
review_headline: Didn't Cut It For Me, But Might For You
review_body: I had truly hoped I had found the perfect creamer that would allow me to drink my coffee they way I like and yet reduce, if not nearly eliminate, my consumption of Calories, Cholesterol, Gluten, Fat, Sugar and Carbohydrates, and still have a decent taste.  Regrettably, for me, the search continues, (sigh). I bought the different flavors, (my #1 choice being Hazelnut), because I know sometimes a 'favorite' flavor may not taste good, but another may be more palatable, and the benefits will win out over the taste buds; but this was not the case for me, (primary user), nor for the family members that tried it. To me the taste of the artificial sweetener was overwhelming, and it just didn't taste anything like the flavor it claimed to be. I am willing to accept that it is just a matter of personal tastes, and if you are an avid coffee drinker, which I am,looking for creamer that will reduce all the 'bad' stuff, it is worth at least a try, you may like it. It is just subjective. I cannot say it tasted spoiled, or that it smelled bad, or tasted like some chemical, it just failed for me at least, to come close to my expectations of Hazelnut, Mocha, or plain cream.

product_title: Zevia Naturally Sweetened Drinks
review_headline: Zevia has been my favorite soda for years
review_body:
I stopped drinking regular soda (with sugar) in 2010, so my preference for taste is probably different from someone who drinks regular soda often. I have been drinking Zevia for several years and it is the only sweetened soda I drink. I find the sodas to be very flavorful, refreshing, highly carbonated and sweet. My favorite flavors are cola, ginger root beer, Dr. Zevia, and black cherry. I was excited to come across the rainbow pack as it included five flavors I had never tried before. I liked all of the flavors except two (the Mountain Zevia and Orange had strange aftertastes to me). But I'm very happy to like 10 out of the 12 flavors.

I've now ordered the rainbow pack from Amazon twice and plan to continue ordering it in the future. If you are interested in trying Zevia, I would recommend the rainbow pack versus buying a 6-pack of one flavor at the grocery store. Then you can sample the variety of flavors and find if you like any.

product_title: 2LB Cafe Don Pablo Signature Blend Coffee - Whole Bean Coffee - Medium Dark Roast - 2 Lb Bag (Whole Bean)
review_headline: Mediocre coffee with woody and cardboard aroma
review_body:
This is my third day tasting this coffee with consistent notes. I think this is mediocre coffee. I just dumped out the rest of the beans.

I use a medium fine grind. The aroma of the ground coffee is pleasant but muted with cocoa, caramel and earthy notes. I use an AeroPress single cup brewing method. The brewed coffee has a subtle, unpleasant woody and cardboard aroma. The balance of the coffee is reasonable, but seems to lack in depth. It plays a little on my bitters, but the finish is a pleasant caramel.

The flaws in this coffee are obvious and I don't find anything that elevates it.

review_headline: Poor impulse buy on a bad day...
review_body: I wish I looked at the nutrition facts before buying.  First off, 10mg of Theanine is nothing, there's more in a cup of green tea and wasn't shown to have a calming effect in studies until dosages of 200mg or more.  20mg of Valerian is also nothing, most capsules come with a serving size of 1000mg.  Rosehips have nothing to do with stress, they're just a source of vitamin C and look good on a can.  I really felt nothing from this, but I'll give it 2 stars before I actually like the flavor even if it has no cola flavor to speak of, and tastes more like cotton candy than cherry.

product_title: Crockery Gourmet Seasoning Mix For Beef, 2.5 Ounce (Pack of 12)
review_headline: Makes the best pot roast ever!
review_body: This product makes the best pot roast you've ever tasted. I've been using it for years and everyone raves over my pot roast. It's very easy. You just empty the contents of the packet into the crock pot and add the specified amount of water, then mix with a whisk. Then place in your roast (I use a 3lb chuck roast,) veggies (potatoes, carrots and onions) and your bay leaves. I cook mine on high for several hours. You don't have to add the veggies at first, just as long as you add them in time for them to cook thoroughly. This product is a bit salty and be sure to check ingredients if there's anything you're avoiding. It's definitely not a low carb sauce. You can also use this sauce to make beef stew, beef bourguinon, etc. It's really versatile and delicious! Highly recommend! Make it your "secret ingredient."

review_headline: It's good for a preserved fish product- I've not seen any bones or skin, good in recipes

I mainly wanted it for salmon patties. It is salty, it is preserved so I don't personally find that alarming. I just did not add any salt to the salmon patty recipe that I made and it was fine.
I found it to be a pale salmon color, and not as colorful as fresh or frozen salmon. It made great salmon patties, and it was very fast and convenient.
My son, who has never ATE salmon or tuna, actually enjoyed the salmon patties and ate several.
THe packs are small - I used 5 instead of a 14.5 ounce can of tuna that my recipe had called for. It worked, I think because there isn't a lot of water in this pouch product.

product_title: Savannah Bee Company Acacia Honey (12 Ounce Tower Jar)
review_headline: She heard Acacia honey is good for people with stomach issues (intestines)
review_body:
My mom asked me to order "Acacia honey" for her. She heard Acacia honey is good for people with stomach issues (intestines). Which she wouldn't be able to tell if it really does help or not since she just start using it.
Beside what she is using it for, first, Acacia Honey is slow to crystallize, so it stays in a liquid state for a long period of time due to its high concentration of fructose. It's very sweet, but Acacia honey has low sucrose, but high fructose, thus it is the best choice of honey for diabetics too. This Acacia honey is not as thick(more liquidity) and taste also not as strong as regular honey. When mixing this honey with tea, it mixes very well and fast. (regular thick honey will take a while to mix).
On image, it looks dark, but it is because of the color of the jar. The honey itself is clear, yellowish color. So far, I like it and so is my mom.

product_title: Kirkland Signature Dark Chocolate Covered Almonds, 32 Ounce
review_headline: Misleading label: Contains GMO Beet Sugar
review_body:
The taste of this product is very good, although a little too sweet for my taste. I am rating it one star only because I highly dislike their deceptive packaging. Kirkland touts turbinado sugar on the front of the package suggesting it as a primary ingredient. Truth be told, the chocolate coating's main ingredient is genetically engineered beet sugar!

If you wish to avoid GMOs do not buy this product. If you don't care, buy and eat at your own risk!

Know that sugar beets are genetically engineered to be resistant to the herbicide glyphosate sold by Monsanto under the name Roundup. The Roundup Ready crops are doused with huge amounts of the herbicide and likely contain significant residues. Glyphosate, although safer than some other herbicides, is used in such large quantities and is linked to birth defects and other hazards. It is also not good for the environment nor the future sustainability of our agriculture system for food. They glyphosate excess runs off into water supplies.

Ask Costco to replace the beet sugar with cane sugar.

This review is based on information received directly from Costco on August 18, 2015. The info on the hazards of GMOs is from my own research and can be collaborated if you inform yourself through organizations such as Institute for Responsible Technology (IRT).

review_headline: Not ok to suddenly start sending MUCH less product for the same price
review_body:
Ordered this a month ago and really liked it. Ordered again and was confused to receive only one packet of the Metabolic Boost (had received two on the prior order). However, it seemed like they had stuffed the package more and, given the fact that the price didn't change, I assumed I had received the same amount of tea.

But then I quickly ran out. Only then did I notice that they also changed the instructions to say 1 tsp instead of 1 Tbsp.

I realize that is my bad for not noticing, but I think they should have done a little more to communicate the change.

So now I am annoyed because:

1. I got 1/3 of the product for the same price.
2. I ran out of it very quickly because it wasn't made super clear that the serving instructions had changed.

I just think it's a shady business practice. I won't be ordering again.

After I was done I drained the left over juice in the sink..it had a yellowish color to it...from all the peaches no doubt.
Now I must admit the pears were firm, and tasted good, my last can of pears from a "competitor" had tough pieces in it (hence an extra star).
The can of fruit as a whole just tasted lightly lightly lightly (3x) sour, just a hint.
Maybe I just bought this item out of season (Aug 2015) or it was a bad year for grapes...I am not impressed.

product_title: Chef Boyardee Pizza Sauce with Cheese
review_body:
I was first introduced to pizza around 1960 when the Chef Boyardee Pizza kits came out. I thought it was the most wonderful thing created but I can remember my dad hating the "stinky" smell of the Parmesan cheese as it baked.

I've tried other pizza sauces down through the years and have found a couple that are acceptable but none is as good as the Chef Boyardee sauce. After many years, the company started selling the sauce in a can by itself. This was great as I didn't like to have to buy the whole kit, preferring different cheese adorning my pizzas.

There is no other taste quite like this sauce. It's thick and rich and savory, complementing whatever I top my pizza with. I just purchased a can and stuck my spoon in after opening...wow....I was once again transported back over half a decade. Nothing like it.

It contains only 50 calories/ serving which makes it a good choice compared to most juices with the added benefit of a natural energy source from green tea, so it makes for a great pick me up for the health conscious.

These drinks offer 1 full serving of combined fruits and vegetables per serving, which give the added benefits of vitamins. Below I have included some general nutrition facts about the variety I tried:

Calories: 50

review_body: I thought about giving this product four stars, but to be honest I had three wraps in less than 12 hours so that has to say something. I am not a vegan, nor a vegetarian, so I filled one up with scrambled eggs and it was so delicious. I am going to try other variations, but you do have to be cautious because if you are not use to the whole "raw" food or health nut way of life, you might be expecting the excellent buttery taste of a tortilla only to find that it really does taste like apple, kale, and spinach dried flat up like a fruit roll up so you can fill it up with whatever you want. product_title: Stevia Sweetener In The Raw, 50 Count Package
review_headline: You'll be better off if you forget this phony stevia product and ...
review_body: This is NOT stevia, it's maltodextrin with just a little bit of stevia added.  Maltodextrin contains EXACTLY the same caloric content as table sugar, but it's is made from chemically processed GMO corn. Maltodextrin and table sugar have exactly the same effect on the human body.  You'll be better off if you forget this phony stevia product and just buy real sugar.

product_title: V8 V-Fusion +Energy Drinks, 8 Fl Oz
review_body: I had the chance to try V8 V-Fusion free from BzzAgent.  It is canned convenience....perfect with breakfast (or any meal) on the go!  It has a full serving of fruit and vegetables, 80 mg of caffeine, and vitamins A,B, and C.  Also, unlike my coffee, it has no added sugar!  I could definitely see myself replacing a morning coffee habit with a can of V8 V-Fusion Energy.  So far I have tried the Black Cherry, Peach Mango, and Orange Pineapple; orange pineapple is my least favorite.  I expected it to be more like orange juice, like Tropicana or Simply Orange.  The taste doesn't remind me of oranges or pineapple very much- I won't buy that kind again.  On the other hand, the Black Cherry and Peach Mango are fantastic!

product_title: Miracle Noodle Gluten-free Shirataki Pasta
review_headline: A suggestion and how to prepare
review_body:
I went to high school in Korea and grew to love a particular dish called Japchae. I have PCOS, so it was recommended I start a low carb diet. I was okay with giving up rice and most pasta (still waiting for a low carb mushroom ravioli), but sometimes all I want is a taste of home. The angel hair miracle noodles are almost the perfect texture. I bought the variety pack of the rice, fettuccine, and the angel hair. The rice I used to make sweet coconut tapioca pudding, and I tried to use the fettuccine with Alfredo (I DO NOT RECOMMEND THIS). As others have stated, use this product with Asian inspired dishes. Japchae is very easy. I make it with chopped onion, green onion, egg, sesame seed oil, and soy sauce.

Now, I have experimented with different ways to cook these, and the best way is as follows:
1. Rinse with cold water. I do this for a good minute or two, moving the noodles with my hand.
2. Drop noodles into boiling water. Boil for about a minute. Remove.
3. RINSE AGAIN.
4. Place noodles in a hot pan and stir. This is to help remove some of the water and helps get rid of the "rubbery" texture. This is when I will start to add my sauce, veggies, and meat. I will continue to stir fry the dish for a few minutes so the noodles absorb the taste.
5. Eat and be merry =)

product_title: Stash Tea Teabags, Red Velvet
review_headline: as it's not my favorite tea. It smelt strongly of hibiscus and berries ...
review_body:
Purchased this on a whim at the grocery store. I'm reviewing it here because there aren't many reviews. I bought this because I'm in search of caffeine free dessert teas. I wasn't sure what I was expecting, but I was skeptical with the Rooibos, as it's not my favorite tea. It smelt strongly of hibiscus and berries when I opened the foil packet. I brewed it up anyway and added 1 tsp of vanilla simple syrup and a serving of half and half (2 tablespoons).

It tasted like the milk that remains in your cereal bowl after eating berry berry Kix or some other berry cereal, like Fruity Pebbles or something. It wasn't bad, but it wasn't great. The "red" berry part is overwhelming and you really don't get any chocolate notes at all. It's okay, but I know it won't be in my tea rotation very heavily. I may try and go through it just to get rid of it, but it's certainly not a second buy. Although, I would venture to guess that brewing it with a cup of My Morning Mate (from Teavana), might give it the chocolatey edge I'm looking for, but then it would be a morning/day tea instead of an evening dessert tea.

Even if it tastes like berry cereal milk, if you are dieting, you may want to try it. Generally its under $2.50/box at the grocery store.

product_title: Sherpa Pink Himalayan Salt
review_headline: Much better than any other salt out there
review_body:
So I had never had Himalayan Salt before but I am trying to be healthier and move away from the processed regular table salt. So I did my research and found that Himalayan salt and sea salt are apparently the best salts out there. I have always heard good things about sea salt but if you think about what we put in the oceans now days from our garbage, oil, and all that other junk I really doubt that sea salt can be all that healthy for you since it would absorb all of those things. Also colored foods like blueberries, tomatoes, etc... have extra healthy compounds in them that give them their color, so since this salt is pink I would imagine it has to have extra healthy compounds in it to. So I got this Himalayan salt instead.

It tastes far better than regular salt and really has a distinct kick to it compared to the regular salt. It still tastes like salt, just better. This salt is almost like a powder and really small in size. The bag is fairly thick and is resealable. Also this is 100% pure Himalayan salt and has no chemicals or anything else added to it unlike regular salt, and it comes from the Himalayas where the air is pure and fresh. I really like this salt and use it in everything from baking, cooking, eggs, chicken, or whatever. My family also enjoys the extra taste that this salt gives and not a one complained about. This is definitely my new go to salt.

product_title: Slimming Integra Tea - Herbal Loose Leaf Blend of Jiaogulan (Gynostemma), White Mulberry, Tulsi and Oolong
review_headline: A refreshing taste with a healthy reward
review_body:
I’ve just started drinking loose leaf tea within the past six months. In fact, the first loose leaf tea that I tried was the White Mulberry Tea from Immortalitea. I really like that tea so I was looking forward to trying this Integra Tea health blend.
The Immortalitea Company packages their loose leaf tea in resealable pouches so that the tea always stays fresh. The package is easy to open & easy to reseal. When I opened the pouch, I could smell that wonderful earthy scent that I love. I measured the tea & steeped it in my French Press for 8 minutes. When I poured the tea into my mug, it had a nice floral scent. The tea tastes slightly sweet, but not a sweet taste like sugary tea. I like the tea just like it is so I don’t add any honey or other sweetener.
This health blend loose leaf tea is supposed to help manage blood pressure, blood sugar & cholesterol. I drink it each morning before breakfast & I can’t say that it has caused any weight loss (because I haven’t been drinking it long) but it’s a nice healthy start to my day. I look forward to my cup of tea every morning.
I received this product in exchange for my honest review. The opinions expressed are 100% my own based on my personal use of the product.

product_title: Mountain Dew DEWshine, 25 Fl Oz, Limited Edition Collectible Glass Jug
review_headline: The pop is great, the packing is ridiculous
review_body:
The taste of the pop itself is great. No where near as syrupy sweet as well as none of the artificial coloring as regular Mt Dew. All with less carbonation which I liked. Overall I really like the pop itself.

The packaging though...Limited edition? Really? Who cares. 99% of people are going to rip it open, drink the pop and recycle(hopefully) the packaging. The bottle is a nice bottle, but completely superfluous. I mean this is not a 30 year old bottle of some fine vintage liquor or something. It is pop, produced in the last few months. I am glad that with the coupon I was given this was free.

So if you want to try this, make sure you get it at a screaming deal or in another packaging. It is definitely worth drinking of pop like this is your thing.

product_title: Energy Tea - Blue Lady Black High Caffeine Tea - As Much Caffeine As Coffee Per Bag (20 Tea Sachets)
review_body:
Trying to reduce the fat and sugar I ingest with my daily coffee; I am drinking Zest Energy Tea, Apple Cinnamon flavor, lately. I do get a surge of energy from it without any crash like can happen with coffee; since I use cream and sugar unless I have it bulletproof (but that is a story for another day). Now this tea has three times the caffeine of regular tea and even more than caffeinated coffee. Possibly you would like to be sure to drink it earlier in the day if you are one of those affected by caffeine and unable to sleep afterward. As for me, I need the caffeine and am having it with my first meal of the day. I need the mental clarity it provides, plus the energy, so trying this for me was a no-brainer.

The bag is attractive. It contains 50g, which is 20 sachets, inside. It smells heavenly, which sets you up to want to drink it. I drink it without cream or sugar, but you can certainly add them if you like, too. Honey or coconut sugar come to mind as healthy sweetening options that would pair well with this cup. I highly recommend Zest Tea Apple Cinnamon Black Tea. I want to try their others varieties now that I like this one so much.

I received this product at a discount to test and write an unbiased, honest review.

product_title: Bolthouse Farms DAILY GREENS "Kale, Spinach, Romaine" 15.2 oz (12 Count)
review_body:
Based on the ingredients, this stuff packs a mean green punch! I purchased this green drink because I suffer from iron deficiency anemia. I do not eat green vegetables at all! After nearly passing out at work, I decided I needed to make a change. This product provides 50% iron! It's not loaded with sugar and I appreciate Bolthouse Farms for not "sugar coating" the drink. It DOES NOT tastes good, but that's not why I bought it. It's loaded with all the greens that I hate, but the hint of lemon helps. I kept it cold. I only took 80ml shots of this with my lunch and dinner, then finished the last 80ml at bedtime. I always followed with plenty of water and brushed my teeth as well. I can't imagine drinking a whole bottle at once, but that's just me as a beginner. I got use to the taste by the end of the bottle though.

Intestinal temper tantrums? I didn't have any. I did note that chicory, a dietary fiber, is an ingredient though.

Would I purchase again? Absolutely! Health wins over my tastebuds. I also find that this drink is a great conversation starter when I pull it out!

review_headline: Carb Delicious Delivered To My Door

Delicious, carb-loading at its best! This is my favorite brand of Yakisoba that I can get, here in the U.S.
It includes a bowl to prepare the noodle dish in
a vegetable package to add, before adding water
A package of sauce to add once noodles are drained
A seasoning package with kelp and ginger to sprinkle on top
And a package of "mayo mustard" a spicy mayonnaise to add if you please.
I also, separately, buy the hot pink colored pickled ginger and serve it on the side to add in as needed.
I hope you enjoy this yummy taste of Japanese street food as much as I do.

product_title: Nutryttiva Banana Bar (Pack of 24)
review_headline: good for you -- and they taste good -- but super small
review_body:
This review is for the "no sugar added" variety.

One thing that irritates me about fresh bananas is how quickly they go bad. If you'd still like to get some banana flavor and nutrients, but don't want to keep fresh bananas around, this might be a good snack to try. (It is brown/oxidized because it doesn't contain preservatives.) This also tastes the same if it gets squashed, and would therefore be good for travel.

I don't have my wrapper any longer to check the ingredients list but the company's site informs the consumer that these are vegan, gluten-free, Kosher, non-GMO, and fair trade, and that they contain no artificial colors. I see the product image says "100% natural banana," though!

I thought this tasted good, but was a bit small. You'd want to have it with something else (perhaps something containing protein, like a handful of nuts or roasted chickpeas) to make it more filling. You get 24 in a package, for what it's worth (that is not clear from the product listing). These are pretty low calorie and high in potassium, too.

review_headline: I use this as a moisturizer on my legs and ...
review_body:
I use this as a moisturizer on my legs and arms at night. My skin has never been so toned!

I also use it in place of butter for baking cakes and cookies. If I really want the taste of butter, I add a tablespoon of it to the batter, but for the most part, it is not needed. My cakes are just as moist and my cookies are a tad browner in color but people tend to think it's added cinnamon. Coconut oil does tend to decrease the baking time of cookies by a minute or two, but other than that it works very well and is much healthier - even if it doesn't change the taste.

I can take a drink with me without toting a bottle and trying to find a place to leave it.( I have a collapsible cup).I can take it to visit friends, go on a car ride, even stay at a friends overnight and still have my citrus tea. You have taken a wonderful product off the market and subsituted it with horrid tasting non-tea. Your people that check what products are selling seem to be a lot for presenting new products
that arent convenient and expect the public to have it rammed down their throats without repercussions. The Lipton name has been a good name as is Red Rose. Seems as if many people wanted this product and you have avoided their voices. I was given the excuse that Green Tea to go with citrus wasent selling and was heading down a slippery slope. NOT SO CONSIDERING ALL THE SITES I HAVE BEEN ON. I think you loose.

product_title: Stevita Liquid Extract 1.35 Ounces
review_body: In my search to find non-bitter Stevia, this one rates the best so far. No weird aftertaste either. I found that I needed to use more than some other Stevia products in order to get the desired sweetness, but it's well worth it not to have that "BLEGH!" bitterness and medicinal aftertaste. Great in iced teas, homemade lemonade, hot tea. I don't drink coffee so I can't rate it in coffee. Pleased to have discovered this brand!A low-cost liquid Stevia, perfect to use in cold drinks where a powder wouldn't really dissolve, and tastes more like sugar than any artificial sweeteners that I've tried. The container is small enough to fit in a purse and take on the go. Takes 5-8 drops to make a drink sweet. Well worth it if you are turning to Stevia as a healthier alternative to refined sugar or artificial sweeteners. I try to carry one with me and leave one at home. I use to sweeten my cereal and coffee. It is very strong, so start out slowly with only 2 or 3 drops and then adjust to taste. If you use too much, you'll get a subtle, yet distinct, unusual taste.

product_title: Bulksupplements Pure Stevia Powder
review_body:
In the past, I've purchased my stevia extract from Nature's Candy. Although the product was excellent...the packaging with Nature's Candy
was not as good as I would have hoped for.

This product has much better coated foil on outside and inside to resist moisture better than Nature's Candy packaging that has paper outside.
In my kitchen, that is a must to good resistance to moisture on outside as well as inside.

The sealing is thicker and easier to close than the Nature's candy thin strip.

A pinch (1/64 teaspoon) is all you need for one serving size. Becomes too bitter tasting to do more than that.

No additives! Just simply true Stevia! Which is too hard to package in one serving sizes. This size will last a long time.

Only 1/4 of a tsp is required for my cookie batches.

It is sweet but not TOO sweet and never raises my blood sugar level like truvia does because of the sugar MALTOSE added to it.

I use 1/4 of a tsp of this in my toothpaste powder recipe and it works well to add that sweet taste.

The quality stevia in this is EXCELLENT!

Very pleased with this product and would buy from again!
