product_title: Amazing Grass Green Superfood
review_body:
Wow.

Bought this based on the overwhelming majority of excellent seemingly unpaid reviews (thanks fellow shoppers!) and must concur with the majority.
I have now worked my way through 2-3 of the full size jars, which we put on Subscribe & Save, after initially trying the smaller jar. So...I don't work out regularly, partly because it just ain't my bag and partly because I have little free time...and partly because I'm tired ALL THE TIME and it's a vicious circle. You're lethargic so you don't work out so your metabolism slows so you stay lethargic so you don't work out so your metabolism....on and on.
Went to a GP and then to an endocrinologist who suspected hypothyroidism because I'm female/tired/losing hair but too young to be losing hair/cold all the time/retaining weight even on ultra-low calorie intake/dry skin, yada yada yada. But the thyroid test comes back negative whilst meanwhile I want to lay down and die now and then. They gave me uppers, but though it makes me 'normal' ( not speedy, just how I used to be when I was normal) I don't want to be chained to Big Pharma if I don't hafta be.
So I bought this. I bought chocolate flavor despite not caring for chocolate because it seemed to be one of the best-reviewed in terms of mixability and flavor...not tasting like a bag of lawn clippings. It is a VERY fine powder that smells of cocoa powder with a hint of...lawn clippings. I bought a carton of chocolate almond milk to mix it into, since chocolate-on-chocolate seemed strategically sound. Do yourself a favor and buy one of those shaker cups with the flip top and the wire mixing ball inside. Stirring with a spoon is tiresome and lengthy and results in grittiness unless you want to devote a half hour to the task, which you don't. Do yourself another favor and put the liquid in FIRST. And only THEN add the powder. If you put the powder in first, you will never get it all off the bottom, where it will stay, hard and defiant and a waste of dollars.
I sipped gingerly, expecting grass flavor. But although you will not mistake it for Nestle Quik, it is not bad tasting in chocolate milk. I'm telling you. If I can drink it you can. I'm the world champion of dubious eaters. Try it. It grows on you, too. At first sip I thought...well, I can choke this down...I guess. Now I care not, though I do keep it in the blender cup so I can reshake as desired ( I'm picky about texture too and eventually it does settle out). Use more liquid than recommended if you want to hide the taste/texture even more (I do).

So as I sat on the couch staring at the wall reflecting on what might have brought me to this sad state of drinking chocolate grass out of a gym rat bottle, I actually noticed, within twenty minutes of the first sip....HEY. I feel awake. Not high, or amped up like I just took phentermine or a load of caffeine, just...alert. Is that the same wall I was staring at? Why was I tired? Is there time before I have to leave the house to put in some laundry and wipe down this leather couch that the dogs keep befouling with their muddy feet? Where ARE the dogs anyway? They could use a wash too, since the couch is about to be clean.
I felt normal. I have no testimonial about how much weight I've lost or how glorious my skin looks or how much weight I can bench now. I'm sure if you were normal to begin with you might achieve some of that. I was just looking to not feel like a zombie, a potted plant, and that I definitely got.
When I do NOT take it I DO feel a difference. I then recommended it to my husband, who does work out regularly, and firmly in the no-hippie-lawn-clippings-for-me category. He would not add even a pinch to his workout shake. I made him sip mine one morning on the promise it did not taste like dirt or grass. He said it wasn't bad.
A short while later he confessed he had subsequently added a SMALL scoop to his workout drink and d@&$ if he didn't feel more alert and had more stamina that morning. Since then, he uses more than I do.

For alertness and energy with no crash and no jitteriness, this is your daddy right here. Is it unfortunate that it's made out of vile green things that you have to hide to keep from gagging? Yeah, maybe. But you CAN hide it, so pick up the small size and see for yourself.

It's very competitively priced here, btw. Our local grocer wants almost as much for the smaller jar.e stamina that morning. Since then, he uses more than I do.

product_title: Bigelow Lemon Ginger Probiotics Herbal Tea (Pack of 4)
review_body:
This tea is so good -- tastes like lemon cookies to me. It isn't too strong on the ginger, which works for me since I'm not crazy about strong gingery flavors.

I cold brew my tea by the pitcher by putting a teabag per cup of water and letting it sit on the counter overnight. A pitcher doesn't last long as iced tea in my house. Everyone I serve it to loves it and asks what kind/brand it is so they can buy it themselves. If I don't have any in the fridge, I'm asked, "Where is the lemon cookie tea?"

A note regarding the probiotics -- the probiotics used are a strain of beneficial bacillus coagulans, a lactic acid producing bacteria. The strain used makes spores, which can survive high temperatures, so the vast majority of the bacteria (80%) make it into your digestive tract alive, even you brew this with hot/boiling water, according to the instructions. Great news, since any way you brew this you'll still be getting all the good probiotics!

Amazing tea!

product_title: Viva Naturals Organic Extra Virgin Coconut Oil, 16 Ounce
review_body:
What can I say.... I love this product! The smell is amazing and I have to say I hate coconut! Like I hate the taste and smell of it. But for this oil. the smell is wonderful and its not overbearing. I use it for my hair and skin. I use to mostly when using the LOC Method; I use it as my oil and it makes my hair very soft. I am African American with a 4b to 4c mixed hair texture and I am pleased.

I love the smell and the feel is great; I don't like it as an oil though. I'd prefer to use it as a solid but when it arrived it was a liquid. However, that wasn't a problem. I put it in the refrigerator for about 2 hours and it wet back to being solid. I left it in my room and overnight it became a liquid (maybe due to the summer heat) but if you refrigerate it you should have no worries. I also leave the AC on sometimes and the product stays in its solid form soooo this is best as a winter product lol but I'll buy all year round.
The size its also great for its price. Its huge and it was filled to the very top; I was impressed for the cost and quality of the product.
I also used it on my skin and my I feel so soft lol. I also like the smell, its not overbearing and it keeps me warm (once again. I see this as a winter product because warm in summer is not a good combination lol)
This is a great product and I will not be changing this as my oil for y LOC Method (Liquid/Oil/Cream); this is method is a MUST for new naturals!! So I'd recommend this product as an amazing oil.

product_title: Religious Experience "The Wrath" Salsa 17 oz.
review_body:
I first experienced The Wrath several years ago; a purchase based on the label's proclamation that it was \\"extremely hot.\\" Well, I've tried many salsas claiming to be hot but they mostly turned out to be false prophets. Those that were actually hot got so by the use of vinegar or raw capsaicin (the active ingredient in chili peppers), but sacrificed flavor in the process. The Wrath is different, and makes its heat the old-fashioned way - with peppers! And what do peppers add? Of course heat - and there's plenty of that - but what sets The Wrath apart is FLAVOR!

Hot food lightweights might want to start with one of their milder salsas and work their way up, delicious bite after bite. But once you experience The Wrath you just might agree it is a religious experience! If you can find the Religious Experience salsas at your local grocery store consider yourself blessed. My grocer stopped carrying it so I've been buying The Wrath directly from the manufacturer's website (thewrath.com). If you buy a 12-jar case as I do, fear not - despite having no preservatives it has excellent shelf life. (Perhaps because it's so hot even bacteria won't touch it... but that's just a theory LOL.) In any case The Wrath is true to its word - extremely hot and flavorful.

product_title: Daves Gourmet Scorpion Pepper Hot Sauce, 5 oz
review_headline: Fantastic sauce, a totally approachable introduction to the super hots
review_body:
Man oh man, this stuff is good. I bought this sauce because this is my first time growing super hots and I wanted to know how hot they really were before I start cooking with them. With all the rumors and stupid videos of people eating ghost peppers and ending up floored, in the hospital, and/or internet famous; I thought this sauce would be a total novelty, some stupidly hot sauce you buy to trick your friends into emasculating themselves by volunteering to eat some chokingly hot poison, this was not the case.

Firstly I should mention that my spice tolerance is pretty high, but I wouldn't call mysefl a "chili-head". I douse my eggs with Trader Joes habanero sauce and it burns me pretty well, but I love the flavor and the rush so I just keep doing it. Opening a bottle Daves Scorpion Sauce and taking a wiff, you're greeted with the smell of freshly crushed peppers, no vinegar sting like Tabasco, it smells delicious. The taste is amazing, right before the burn really kicks in, the unique and intense tropical fruit flavor of scorpion peppers shines through. Holy crap is it hot, way hotter than any habanero sauce! The heat stings instantly and builds slowly on the back of the tongue and the throat to a real five alarm level. Its not totally unmanageable and the flavor keeps me coming back for more.

If you are looking for a stupid, suffocatingly hot pepper sauce, buy something with extracts. If you want a delicious, simple, fiery and flavorful scorpion pepper sauce, Dave is your man.

review_headline: Black Cherry is the best Zevia flavor!!
review_body:
I really like the Zevia brand sodas and this is my favorite flavor. I wanted a soda made with natural ingredients, and WITHOUT aspartame, so I went looking in natural food stores and found Zevia! Zevia sodas are made with Stevia (a plant) instead of artificial sweeteners. I found them a few years ago and have tried many of the different flavors. The Stevia flavored drinks have a different kind of sweet flavor than sugar or artificial sweeteners, so you might need to have a few to get used to the flavor, but I liked it right away and was thrilled to have an alternative to the mainstream sodas for a zero calorie drink. It costs more than your typical aspartame sweetened soda, but it's far cheaper than coffee at the local coffee shop!

The black cherry flavor does not have caffeine. Some of the other flavors do have caffeine, like the cola and the Dr. Zevia flavors.

I can't seem to live without this soda :) It's my drink of choice!

product_title: Green Unroasted Coffee, 5 Lb. Bag, Fresh Roasted Coffee LLC.
review_body:
I will start by saying it would be nice if each specific bean could have its own set of reviews. If someone does not specifically call out the exact bean they ordered, there is no way of knowing which ones they speak of.

This review is for the Monsooned Malabar Beans. I mainly wanted to try these ones for the novelty of it and because I have been interested in trying some monsooned coffee for quite some time now.

First off, I will say this company (Fresh Roasted Coffee LLC.) has my vote of confidence. The coffee came in a nice sturdy sealed bag. It arrived in a timely fashion and intact (not always the case with some online green coffee bean retailers).

This coffee is very mellow (as expected). There is absolutely nothing wrong with this coffee. Unfortunately I like very bold flavors but don't like to waste beans by taking them to French Roast level. It in my opinion is a waste of quality beans.

If you like very mellow but delicious coffee, BUY THIS COFFEE.

It is pretty much too mellow for my acquired coffee taste preference. I will not buy this again because I like coffees with a real flavor punch even at medium roasts. I will however be open to buying other beans from this same seller.

As a final experiment I will play around with these beans and take them as dark as a Vienna Roast to see if they can be bolder and maintain some of their subtleties. Will give an update if it turns out anything great.

product_title: Java Factory Single Cup Coffee for Keurig K Cup Brewers
review_headline: Nice selection of coffee - most come in stand-alone flavor packs also.
review_body:
I enjoy having a chance to try variety packs -- it's the best way to try new flavors without ending up with a big box of a flavor you may not like. The Java Factory Variety Pack K-cup coffee pack contains:
7 - Wakey Wakey - Since these are light roast and have a bit more caffeine than medium or dark roast and a lighter flavor it's good for breakfast or whenever you need a pick me up and a flavor to not over-power your food.
8 - Smooth Caffeinator<//B> - Medium Roast with a smooth flavor bold but not harsh.
8 - Dark & Handsome - dark roast with bolder flavor than Smooth Caffeinator.
7 - Da Bomb - dark roast with double caffeine. This one has the boldest flavor but not a burnt taste. It also stands up very nicely to strongly flavored (sugar-free) syrups such as Raspberry and Gingerbread. Without or without creamer or flavor syrup it was a nice strong cup of coffee.
NOTE: I mostly drink my coffee black now-a-days since I"m cutting back on calories and creamers even the low calories one have just too many calories for me. The three bolder flavored coffees didn't taste burnt to me as many with 'bold' or 'robust' flavors seem to do lately. Cream in my coffee is for after dinner with dessert.
6 - Vanilla Dream - This one was a bit of a surprise. Nice nutty buttery flavor over the coffee and really, the aroma was heavenly. Make it and don't drink it the smell is enticing enough to bring a smile.
4 - Choconut - Wow. Finding a chocolate flavor coffee that actually has a hint of chocolate flavor has been a bit of a grab bag lately. Tried lots but this one is so far the top contender and it has a nice coconut flavor also. With creamer it's like drinking a liquid Mounds candy bar (used Sweet Italian Cream no sugar creamer).

All in all a great selection. I'm definitely going to have to try more of the Choconut flavor especially once colder weather gets here.It will be a nice warm treat of a drink.

If you check out Google for getting around DRM in the Keurig 2.0 these k-cups work very well and make excellent coffee.

product_title: Hershey's S'mores Kit, 3 LB 9.1 Oz
review_headline: Ideal in every way, save for one concern
review_body:
My wife and son went camping this weekend along with some friends and family, including many youngsters. This kit was perfect at campfire time; everything to make s'mores was together from the start, no fear of having forgotten or run out of one of the basic elements, and knowing that Hershey's chocolate was the main ingredient.

And there was plenty of it! Enough to make 36 monster s'mores, and we're talking serious snacking, each one made with 1/2 a chocolate bar.

This was the second time we used our new camper, and it was super-convenient to have this one box available right out of one of the cabinets to bring outside to the fire. No fumbling about with an already opened bag of old marshmallows, a box of graham crackers and a handful of some form of chocolate; everything in one box. Even knowing I wasn't going to be there this weekend but that I'd sent them with the kit, which I'd seen in the cabinet before they left, made me feel good about the product.

The only drawback, and this was really unfortunate, was that almost all of the 18 bars of chocolate had melted to some extent somewhere along the way. It might have been during shipping of the product or during storage, but almost every bar was misshapen and side-heavy. Big deal? Not so much. According to all concerned, the s'mores were just as delicious as they could possibly be, but it must be noted that such a thing can happen, as, in all fairness, could happen if all the ingredients were purchased separately. We certainly could have removed the chocolate from the box right away and stored it in the fridge, thus reducing a little bit of the convenience, and next time we probably will do that.

In short, I do recommend this kit, but my caveat is... check your chocolate right away, and know about the special storage concerns. Enjoy!

product_title: Roland Sea Salt, Fine Crystals, 27.8 Pounds
review_body:
We had been buying our sea salt in small little packages from one of the big name box stores that one needs a membership for. As we use sea salt for our soaking baths at times and to use with our cooking needs. As iodized salt just is not that good for us anymore at our age. Plus the Roland sea salt has a much better taste with food compared to normal table salt.

We are now on our third bucket of Roland Fine Crystal Sea Salt, that is how much we enjoy this product from Roland. At first we thought a single bucket of Roland salt would last us well over a year. As it is 27 pound bucket of sea salt after all. But after finding so many uses for it in our daily lives from health to food to soaking baths to even packing aching joints which works very well. We have started to use sea salt more and more. We highly recommend Roland sea salt to anyone who is seeking a more healthy body all around.

Compare how much you pay for sea salt per pound from any of the normal retail store locations. Then compare that price to Roland's sea salt 27 pound bucket. And you will see how much more you have been paying for sea salt, for most likely an inferior product. Pick up a bucket or two, you won't be disappointed. Plus know that salt can sit around for ever, and I mean forever. So stock up when you can!

We were not paid nor discounted in any way, shape or form for giving our honest opinion here of this product as a review to assist others. We believe, as a family, it is important to give an honest and accurate review to assist others so they can make good purchasing decisions that can benefit them and their family. And not be lead to cheap, unwanted purchases that are scripted by advertisers to buy their products. We are a family that believes in a minimalist lifestyle while trying to be green as much as possible in all departments of our life. As we care to leave something, anything, of this planet to our grandchildren so they may enjoy it as we have.

product_title: EBlend Coffee Medium Blended Roast
review_body:
In a bleak world riddled with fear and doubt, you tend to cherish the little sunlit moments of serenity all the more. That's sitting on the porch at 6am, before the hustle bustle of kids and work and marriage and chores, watching the sun whisk the mist away and remind me of what it was like to watch when the world turned the midnight sun into morning sun, with a steaming mug of EBlend. As both smell and taste are tickled by the awesome aroma of this Alaskan roast, the thoughts are free to finally wander to the most remote places of the mind. For me? That's reminiscing of Alaska, home.

I've had many cups of coffee in my adult years, some good and some bad. But after I discovered EBlend, I realized how I've all too often settled for less in my past. No more. For both special treats and the every day quick cup of joe alike, EBlend hits the spot.

Only word of warning: you may forget time and place when you get settled with that hot cup of java.

product_title: Quaker, Cap'n Crunch Original Cereal, 14oz Box (Pack of 4)
review_body:
Quaker, Cap'n Crunch Original Cereal, 14oz. (398g) Box (Pack of 4), from Cap'n Crunch.
The taste of this cereal reminds me a bit of Kellogg's Corn Pops, except in a flat shape, rather than round. That's where the similarity ends. The strangely sweet taste of this cereal snuck up on me after I finished eating a bowl of it mixed with milk. The next thing I noticed was a slight feeling of nausea. I looked at the list of ingredients, and I am not sure what caused the nausea problem. The expiration date was 28 February 2016 (six months in the future), so that was not the problem. The remainder of the box promptly went into the birdfeeder, mixed in with the birdseed. I cannot give this cereal a rating of more than two stars, and even that is being generous.

The Nutrition Facts, and Ingredients, as listed on the box:
Nutrition Facts:
Serving Size ¾ cup (27g), Servings Per Container about 15.
Amount Per Serving:
Calories 110 (Calories from Fat 15).
Total Fat 1.5g (Saturated Fat 1g, Trans Fat 0g, Polyunsaturated Fat 0g, Monounsaturated Fat 0g).
Cholesterol 0g.
Sodium 200mg.
Total Carbohydrate 23g (Dietary Fiber 1g, Sugars 12g, Other Carbohydrate 10g).
Protein 1g.

Ingredients:
Corn flour, sugar, oat flour, brown sugar, coconut oil, salt, niacinamide (Vitamin B3), yellow 5, reduced iron, zinc oxide, yellow 6, thiamin mononitrate (Vitamin B1), BHT (a preservative), pyridoxine hydrochloride (Vitamin B6), riboflavin (Vitamin B2), folic acid (Vitamin B9).

Sold by Shopper Ease and Fulfilled by Amazon.

review_headline: I received these from a coworker that found them horrible. She gave me 11 / 12 bars in ...
review_body:
I received these from a coworker that found them horrible. She gave me 11 / 12 bars in the box she bought and, while they taste horrible, they're bearable if you just need something in your stomach before the gym. However, I went to grab one of these and cut it open to discover the same nasty mold other customers have reported on. The expiration date says "06 FEB 2016", so that's not the issue. I read in another review that customer service told them it was a "issue with a small batch" but I have to call BS on that after seeing multiple people with the same issue. I opened up the other bars I had left and every single one had mold that started from the ends of the bar. On the plus side, the site of the mold ruined any hunger I had.

Overall, I would highly recommend staying far away from this product. Get the MusclePharm bars if you don't have diet restrictions, they're leaps and bounds better than these.

product_title: Terrasoul Superfoods Amla (Amalaki) Berry Powder (Organic)
review_body:
This seems to be very high quality amla. I use it internally and externally on hair and face.

With other brands of amla, mixing with water to make a hair or face mask, it would be really really clumpy and impossible to apply. This amla mixes with water into a very smooth consistency. I stir in just enough water to make it the consistency of yogurt. Apply it as a facial mask for about twenty minutes then rinse off thoroughly. Same with hair. I absolutely love how my hair looks after using it! My hair is incredibly soft and the waves are very clearly defined with no frizz at all.

Internally, I mix a half teaspoon in water and drink it every morning. I'm not sure why but it seems to give me some energy and just makes me feel good somehow. Hard to describe.

Will order Terrasoul amla again.

Maltodextrin contains EXACTLY the same caloric content as table sugar, but it's is made from chemically processed GMO corn. product_title: Rusty's Hawaiian Coffee, Ka'u Classic Medium Roast, Single Origin Whole Bean Coffee
review_body:
Okay, I have a huge coffee addiction, and I am a total coffee nut in every possible way...

I love Kona coffees, and I discovered Rusty's Hawaiian on a visit to Hawaii a few years back. Rusty's is a popular brand to the coffee cognoscenti, scoring well on Coffee Review and in other sites. The Ka'u Classic Medium Roast scored a solid 92, and some of their other coffees have hit 95 points - definitely rare territory. The brand is a local favorite, and I was hoping to get the same quality delivered to my home 6,000 miles away. Unfortunately, the order did take a fairly long time to arrive (two weeks), but for the most part, it's been worth the wait.

The packaging I usually choose is 8 oz beans, since this is the size of the container on my grinder and I have less chance of stale beans laying around. The beans themselves are a true medium roast: dry looking (meaning the oils are still locked inside) and roasted to a medium shade of brown with very little cracking or damage to the beans. I found the uniformity of the beans to be quite good, with virtually no broken pieces mixed in. I may have missed it, but I couldn't find a roast date on the package - that would be a big plus to me.

I'm not sure how popular this is, but my favorite way to prepare Kona coffees is in my nuclear espresso machine - it gets every little bit of flavor out of them. At first, shots were coming up a bit flat and bland, but I finally homed in on the right settings. I set my grinder just a bit finer than I usually would, 10.5 seconds of grind, tamped with a 30 pound calibrated tamper and brewed at 199 degrees for 30 seconds...the resulting shots are amazing.

I'd describe the taste as definitely sweet, smooth and refined, with notes of caramel, brown sugar and maybe cinnamon. Words don't do justice, but it's definitely a luxury taste that I never tire of. I've tasted a lot of coffees, and I'd put this batch in the top ten, right up there with my favorite Konas and JBMs.

On the other hand, ground a little more coarsely and stuffed into a reusable K-cup, the resulting coffee was nothing spectacular. I was actually stunned that one bean could taste so different. I tried a batch in a French press - better than the K-cup certainly, but if I had started there, I'm not sure I would have been impressed. Seems Rusty's beans require good brewing technique and attention to detail. But if you put a few minutes in, the results are amazing.

One other thing I like about Rusty's is that they cater to the serious coffee aficionado - they will even sell you green beans you can roast yourself. I'd definitely encourage you to at least give them a try - if you like Hawaiian coffees, you won't be disappointed.

If he knew I would be visiting him and Gram at the farm, he'd hide a roll of the fruit flavored LifeSavers in one of the small chest pockets of his bib overalls for me to find.  What a treat! product_title: Crazy Cups Flavored Coffee Single Serve Cups for Keurig K Cups Brewer Variety Pack Sampler 40-count
review_headline: These were great, i didnt think i was going to get ...
review_body: These were great, i didnt think i was going to get so many duplicates that kinda sucked a bit but i wasnt like super upset, it was like more of an Awe moment then that was it. as for flavors, i tried 3 so far, because i wanted to get an idea based on how most coffees taste to me ( this is my first time using pods just got me a Keurig) so far all have worked in there. Caramel Truffle Sundae is what i used first, smelled divine, don't think i really tasted what made it a sundae or truffle but the caramel was good. Banana foster i tried, because A)normally not a banana person and B) its a strong differnt flavor then im use to with most of my coffee or similar drinks, so i thought this would give me a good idea of how flavored they are. IT was not over powering, just a hint at the first sip each time, but then turned to the more familiar warm coffee taste. this was good but it made me worry since banana is such a strong flavor and it was so mild, so i tried peppermint mocha one. WHICH IS MY FAVORITE DRINK EVER by the way. i love almost all things peppermint mocha. i was a bit disappointed i wanted the peppermint to be stronger, while it was there, it was barely a hint. and no chocolate tasting at all. These are still great, don't get me wrong, they taste yummy and are great coffee in general. not too strong not too weak. but i do wish the flavors were a bit more bold. still got more flavors to try ( by the way i got 16-17 flavors in the 4 count, one set of 4 several sets of 3, 2 sets of 2 and then 2 singles)

product_title: Winona Pure Popcorn Butter Theater Style 5oz. [Pack of 3]
review_body:
This is without a doubt the best tasting and easiest to use popcorn flavoring that I can find on the market. While it is a bit pricey, to me it is worth it for my 'go to snack' of microwave popcorn. I stopped using the pre-made commercial bags of microwave popcorn because of all the junk and chemicals in those convenient packages (often convenience has a cost), many studies show carcinogens in those packages, and I'm just not comfortable with that, as I eat popcorn almost every night.
What I really like about this 'butter spray' , is just that, it's a SPRAY!!! This means that I can save myself calories from the cooking process, and spray this onto the popped corn after it is done, giving it MUCH more flavor. It also helps my popcorn salt stay on the popped corn.
With no artificial colors or flavors, I can at least feel a little bit better about spraying on OIL onto my popcorn. Because that is exactly what it is,,,OIL,,,and it is NOT low calorie so it must be 'counted as such' if you keep track of your daily calorie counts. HOWEVER, because this is a nice fine spray, it does give you the ability to use it lightly on your popcorn for flavor without having to coat it just to get that flavor.

product_title: SweetLeaf Sweet Drops Liquid Stevia Sweetener, Cola, 2 Ounce
review_headline: Indeed not all Sweet Drops are created equal.
review_body:
I use Sweet Drops to flavor plain seltzer. Some flavors work well, some do not.

I have to say, I do not understand why reviews for ALL Sweet Drops flavors are listed together here in the reviews section. That makes no sense at all. It even seems a little deceptive, because some of the flavors I've tried are good, some are not. Not allowing reviews for each flavor leaves the Amazon customer in the dark about each flavor, unless s/he has time to read through over a thousand reviews.

For instance, the Root Beer flavor is often good, resulting in a beverage that tastes like good root beer. However, the root beer drops are inconsistent; some bottles have tasted really good, others have had a weak flavor of root beer and a heavy aftertaste. Lemon barely tastes like lemon. Lemons are tart, and the Sweet Drops are... very sweet with zero tartness. Cola doesn't taste much like any cola I've ever had, and as some others have noted, it tastes metallic. Orange was okay if you used a minimum of 3x the recommended amount; otherwise, the orange flavor is barely discernible. Grape had no recognizable grape flavor to it at all.

With Zevia's recent reformulation of all their once-delicious-now-atrocious colas and other beverages to remove caramel coloring (an unpopular move, judging by the reviewers here at Amazon and verified by my tastebuds), my search continues for a stevia-based or monkfruit-based flavor additive suitable for adding to seltzer.

Because it is tough to find reviews for each flavor, you may have to experiment. Taste is subjective anyway, so YMMV.

product_title: Amazing Grass Green Superfood
review_headline: After reading the reviews about Garden of Life and Amazing Grass I decided to try this product
review_body:
After reading the reviews about Garden of Life and Amazing Grass I decided to try this product, mainly because of flavor, I love chocolate - how bad could this be?

Let me just say the name, "Amazing Grass" is very descriptive of the taste. There is a hint of chocolate mixed in with a grainy, grassy, dirt flavor. It also says something about "new improved texture". I definitely recommend using a blender, almond milk/milk, coffee or something with some other flavor besides water to mask the actual taste of what you will be drinking. Maybe a banana? get to that below.

For myself I currently use almond milk mixed with a teaspoon of another vegetarian protein powder and this makes it palatable. I also take half the recommended dose otherwise for the first little bit you might find yourself a tad bloated due to all healthiness loaded into this little canister. I am a petite sized woman so I find a teaspoon plus a teaspoon of my other veg. protein powder works for me.

I have been taking this for a couple weeks and really notice a difference in how I feel, my energy level has increased, and cravings have decreased A LOT! I work out everyday, eat very healthy but even individuals such as myself are prone to those "I only had couple cookies" when it may have actually more than a "couple"...then leads to the dreaded extra long workout.

I have found with traditional protein powders in the past something lacking, and usually hungry after an hour. I find it tedious to add fruits to make a smoothie when this is already loaded with everything you could imagine. I can see adding half a banana?? to mask. Ohh and this will be green.

Taste, texture aside great product. Stick with it you will notice a difference. Something to remember, the stuff that is the healthiest for us sometimes doesn't taste the best.

product_title: Nitro2Go Instant Energy
review_headline: These took the edge off, but not as much as other popular name brand ones
review_body: I got these as an alternative to 5 Hour Energy drinks which are so crazy expensive. Unfortunately, I'm not a fan. I barely noticed any difference with these, and it certainly didn't last for 7 hours. The taste is comparable to 5 Hour (so both are gross), and the bottles are the same size. Caffeine doesn't have a huge effect on me in the first place, so when I need an extra boost the 5 hours just take the edge of my sleepiness. I don't get the jitters like my husband does. With this, I did feel slightly less sleepy but more in the sense like how I'd feel if I got up, went to the bathroom, came back and sat back down. After about 15-20 min (depending on the day and how sleepy I am) I would start to get tired again. With the 5 hour I would at least be able to feel somewhat alert for at least an hour. (I do medical transcription, which can be quite monotonous and requires a lot of sitting and typing, obviously). So ultimately, these aren't awful. They did help a little. There is a very good possibility that someone who is more sensitive to caffeine would feel a bigger boost with these. I personally will just have to stick with 5 Hours to get the needed results. Note: Item purchased at a discount to test and provide a review.

product_title: Fiber One Chewy Bars Oats & Chocolate: 36 Bars of 1.4 Oz
review_body:
I needed more fiber in my diet and decided to try these Fiber One Oats and Chocolate Bars. They satisify my need for fiber and my love of chocolate.

Each bar is 1.4 ounces and provides 35% of your daily fiber. They have 4g of fat per bar. These bars are made by General Mills. The bars are chewy and a little crunchy. They are naturally flavored. Some of the ingredients in the bar are semisweet chocolate chips, whole grain oats, barley flakes, sugarcane fiber, soy lecithin and milk.

This is a great value for this hard cardboard box. This is the price that I would get if I went to Sam's Club. These bars are regularly sold in vending machines and at convenience stores. They are great snacks, for the kids in-between meals, to take on the run, to take on vacations or anytime you want something healthy to eat on the go.

I was very pleased with these bars and I continue to purchase them.

review_headline: Perhaps FarmFresh are just as good although smaller and more broken pieces
review_body:
The Farm Fresh brand were OK. I might purchase them again.
Food to Live brand are bigger and tastier, but more expensive.
Perhaps FarmFresh are just as good although smaller and more broken pieces.
I like to soak and sprout, then dehydrate at 105o.
The Farm Fresh were absolutely fine, but not as beautiful as some other, more expensive brands.
Sprouting and dehydrating is the best way to keep nuts long time without danger of going mouldy.
Sprouting also increases the health benefits. with almonds. I skin half of them and leave the skins on half.
I like food to live brand for this. Google has a lot of info about sprouting nuts etc, also about dehydration.
Low oven is possible as long s temp doesn't go over 115o max or many healthy nutrients are destroyed.

product_title: McCormick Garlic Butter Shrimp Scampi Seasoning Mix (Pack of 4) .87 oz Packets
review_body: Just open a packet and dip your finger in for a taste and you'll immediately taste that this stuff would be great on so many things! For a slap yo mama delicious chicken/veggie soup recipe try this: Cube about a half pound to a pound of boneless chicken thighs, lightly brown the chicken in a deep skillet in some oil and butter, mix a can of cream of mushroom soup with a can of chicken broth and add it to the chicken. Next, sprinkle in some of this shrimp scampi mix to taste along with some grated parmasan cheese and a little ranch dressing mix. i add some chopped celery too. Simmer it until desired thickness is achieved. Served with Pringles tortilla chips on the side, this combo will knock your socks off! I even tried the scampi mix in a similar recipe with beef for a delicious beef soup/stew and walah! That was delicious too!!!!

If you like your coffee mild you should use the Large setting. Otherwise Medium on the Keurig makes your average cup of joe. One that works very well with a bit of chocolate for a treat.

product_title: Chips Ahoy Cookies
review_body: I'm not a cookie snob.  I enjoy an Oreo or a regular Chips Ahoy cookie just fine.  These things,  though,  are just not good.  The sweetness is just about all they have going for them. The texture is soft but crumbly.  There's a touch of birthday cake icing flavor and chocolate,  but all with an artificial taste nearly overpowering all but the sweetness. I was surprised that sugar, flour, and fat could taste so artificial. By the way, I'm not rating these against home made cookies or cookies from a fine local bakery.  I'm comparing these to Oreo or regular Chips Ahoy. If they both were to get 5 stars,  these soft icing filled cookies should get about 1/2 star for being,  allegedly,  edible.

product_title: Brewer's Best Peanut Butter Beer and Wine Fruit Flavoring
review_headline: Finally a peanut butter flavor that is almost as good as the discontinued Watkins product.
review_body:
It was a sad day when Watkins quit making peanut butter extract. One eighth ounce liquid measure in a cappuccino, or even better also adding a eighth ounce of chocolate extract made a fantastic beverage. We have tried several other brands and had dismal results. One barely tasted of peanut butter at all, but tasted strongly of being scorched. Others were so weak one had to almost imagine the peanut butter flavor in their beverage.

This brand finally does have very noticeable peanut butter flavor. Although it is slanted toward beer brewing, it works for cappuccinos.

While one can obviously purchase peanut butter flavor regular coffee syrup, using extracts gives us more control. With two teaspoons of stevia in the cup, and the eighth ounce of extract per double shot of espresso, we know what we are drinking, and we don't have over forty bottles of syrups taking up space on the counter. Now if only the flavor were just a bit stronger.

review_body: ok, I am not sure if I just rec'd a bad bag or if this is a product quality issue.  My husband and I sprout seeds.  We love the taste and the nutrient density.  We've been able to sprout most seeds by the soaking method and then either leaving them in a glass jar to sprout or placing them in soil.  We have tried both ways with this "seed" and both times the product soured and turned moldy and had to be thrown away.  The third time my husband decided to soak them in hydrogen peroxide.... well... something is tragically wrong with these seeds.  The peroxide "boiled" from the bacterial content on the seeds.  We soaked them twice in peroxide until the "boiling" stopped.  We then planted them directly into soil and they are barely sprouting.  We have 3 other varieties of wheat berries and all of them have sprouted.  We realize that not all seeds can be sprouted, but you can easily find information about sprouting Einkorn berries all over the sprouting forums, so something is terribly wrong with this product.  I would estimate we have about a 25% germination rate.  That makes these berries way too expensive for sprouting.  AND, it also means you can't do sprouted flour.  What a shame.  We'll keep looking!

review_body:
In reality I'd like to give this a one star rating. The reason I'm not doing that is that I am aware that I will not be able to give this product an unbiased review. The reason I'm writing a review at all is to warn other people who might share my dislike of gooey, mucous-like textures in their consumables. This might be a great product - I'll never know because I simply can't swallow it without gagging. I find that I avoid using it even stirred into juice because of the memory of that gooey-niss. I've been on a product search for a liquid vitamin/mineral supplement. So far, Intramax has been at the top of my list while this product is at the bottom. This is a reasonable expensive product so if you're like me and squeamish about textures, do your pocketbook a favor and look elsewhere. I was going to add a photo but the "add a photo" button is not working for me.
THIS IS AN ADDENDUM TO MY EARLIER REVIEW. WHILE MY EXPERIENCE OF THESE PRODUCTS HASN'T CHANGED, MY EXPERIENCE WITH THIS SELLER HAS BEEN EXCEPTIONAL. I WAS IMMEDIATELY CONTACTED BY THE SELLER AS A RESULT OF MY REVIEW AND OFFERED A REFUND. FURTHER, I WAS INTERVIEWED AS TO MY EXPERIENCE WITH THE PRODUCTS IN A GENUINE EFFORT TO "TWEAK" THE PRODUCTS TO MAKE THEM MORE PALATABLE TO MORE PEOPLE. I DON'T KNOW ABOUT YOU, BUT I HAVEN'T SEEN CUSTOMER SERVICE LIKE THIS IN A LONG, LONG TIME. THIS IS CLEARLY A COMPANY THAT CARES ABOUT IT'S CUSTOMERS AND IT'S REPUTATION. BASED ON THIS, I'M SURE THAT THE PRODUCT PROVIDED IS ALSO AN EXCEPTIONAL ONE AND I'M TRULY SORRY THAT IT WASN'T FOR ME.

product_title: Torani Caramel Sauce, 16.5 oz Squeeze Bottle, New Packaging
review_body:
Yum! I'm not a big coffee drinker so when I go to Starbucks I order a caramel apple cider. My kids recently asked for some of my drink and totally loved it. So I decided to get a copy cat recipe and this is the same Caramel Syrup they use at Starbucks.

If you would like the recipe it is below:
12 ounces of apple juice heated in the microwave or on the stove until steaming
1 tablespoon of cinnamon dolce syrup (can be bought on Amazon or made from scratch)
1 tablespoon caramel sauce for the cider
whipped cream and caramel sauce for drizzling

Heat the juice, caramel and dolce syrup until steaming. Add whipped cream and drizzle with caramel sauce. Perfecto! Great for a cold fall day or after going trick or treating with your kids!

This sauce is also great for ice cream, apple pie and any sweet dessert. My last piece of advice is to serve at room tempature by either sitting out or placing the bottle in some warm tap water to make it easier to drizzle.

product_title: Officially Licensed Sriracha2Go (Empty)
review_headline: Perfect gift for the Sriracha Lover
review_body:
These are so cute! If you have friends that just love Sriracha sauce, then you need to get some of these. Such a great gift (or gag gift) for those who love hot sauce.

Another use is for Boy Scouts. I know this sounds weird, but many Scouts feel like the standard backpacking (freeze-dried) stuff is just way too bland. These little tiny bottles are just right for backpacking. I am sure they will show up at Philmont soon as standard equipment.

review_body: Very good cinnamon candy, really like the individually wrapped ones.

product_title: Organic Raw Tigernuts
review_body: Definitely interesting. The taste is pleasant. The chewiness doesn't bother me, but I could only eat one at a time in order to manage the amount of saw-dust/coconut cluster-like leftover in the mouth. I guess chewing one at a time is better to get help you get full faster. I found that keeping an empty cup around was helpful to spit out the residue. Therefore, it's not a convenient travel snack for me. I tried soaking for several days. I did not notice a difference with this technique.

product_title: Gefen Nutola Corn Oil, 128 Ounce
review_body: I love this oil for deep-frying. Superb flavor, very clean and natural tasting. Fries cooked in this stuff are awesome. My sense is that it's a bit "heavier" than peanut oil, so let the food drain just a bit longer.

product_title: True Nopal Cactus Water (Pack of 12)
review_body: I had never tried Cactus water, though have read of the benefits, so I was excited to be able to try this in exchange for my honest review.  I love coconut water for the hydration benefits, but don't love the added carbs and calories that come with it.  I love that this cactus water has the same benefits of coconut water, with a fraction of the calories.  The taste is great, sweet but not too sweet, and definitely not overpowering.  I used this recently to replenish myself after cutting out water in order to make weight for a weightlifting meet.  It worked quickly to make me feel hydrated but not full.  It was a perfect addition to my pre meet plan.
